results.description: The Application class initializes the system's core event management and AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation) mode based on configuration settings. It creates an event group for inter-task communication and sets up a periodic timer to generate clock tick events. The AEC mode is configured as either device-side, server-side, or disabled, ensuring mutual exclusivity between device and server AEC options.

38: void Application::Initialize()
39: {
40:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
41:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateStarting);
42: 
43:     // Setup the display
44:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
45: 
46:     // Print board name/version info
47:     display->SetChatMessage("system", SystemInfo::GetUserAgent().c_str());
48: 
49:     // Setup the audio service
50:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
51:     audio_service_.Initialize(codec);
52:     audio_service_.Start();
53: 
54:     AudioServiceCallbacks callbacks;
55:     callbacks.on_send_queue_available = [this]()
56:     {
57:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO);
58:     };
59:     callbacks.on_wake_word_detected = [this](const std::string &wake_word)
60:     {
61:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED);
62:     };
63:     callbacks.on_vad_change = [this](bool speaking)
64:     {
65:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE);
66:     };
67:     audio_service_.SetCallbacks(callbacks);
68: 
69:     // Add state change listeners
70:     state_machine_.AddStateChangeListener([this](DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
71:                                           { xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED); });
72: 
73:     // Start the clock timer to update the status bar
74:     esp_timer_start_periodic(clock_timer_handle_, 1000000);
75: 
76:     // Add MCP common tools (only once during initialization)
77:     auto &mcp_server = McpServer::GetInstance();
78:     mcp_server.AddCommonTools();
79:     mcp_server.AddUserOnlyTools();
80: 
81:     // Set network event callback for UI updates and network state handling
82:     board.SetNetworkEventCallback([this](NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
83:                                   {
84:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
85:         
86:         switch (event) {
87:             case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
88:                 display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::SCANNING_WIFI, 30000);
89:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
90:                 break;
91:             case NetworkEvent::Connecting: {
92:                 if (data.empty()) {
93:                     // Cellular network - registering without carrier info yet
94:                     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
95:                 } else {
96:                     // WiFi or cellular with carrier info
97:                     std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECT_TO;
98:                     msg += data;
99:                     msg += "...";
100:                     display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
101:                 }
102:                 break;
103:             }
104:             case NetworkEvent::Connected: {
105:                 std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECTED_TO;
106:                 msg += data;
107:                 display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
108:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED);
109:                 break;
110:             }
111:             case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
112:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
113:                 break;
114:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
115:                 // WiFi config mode enter is handled by WifiBoard internally
116:                 break;
117:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
118:                 // WiFi config mode exit is handled by WifiBoard internally
119:                 break;
120:             // Cellular modem specific events
121:             case NetworkEvent::ModemDetecting:
122:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
123:                 break;
124:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorNoSim:
125:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::PIN_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_PIN);
126:                 break;
127:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorRegDenied:
128:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::REG_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_REG);
129:                 break;
130:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorInitFailed:
131:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
132:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
133:                 break;
134:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorTimeout:
135:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
136:                 break;
137:         } });
138: 
139:     // Start network asynchronously
140:     board.StartNetwork();
141: 
142:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
143:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
144: }

146: void Application::Run()
147: {
148:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
149:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
150:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
151:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
152:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
153:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
154:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
155:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
156:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
157:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
158:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
159:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
160:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
161:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
162: 
163:     while (true)
164:     {
165:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
166: 
167:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
168:         {
169:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
170:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
171:         }
172: 
173:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
174:         {
175:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
176:         }
177: 
178:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
179:         {
180:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
181:         }
182: 
183:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
184:         {
185:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
186:         }
187: 
188:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
189:         {
190:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
191:         }
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
194:         {
195:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
196:         }
197: 
198:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
199:         {
200:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
201:         }
202: 
203:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
204:         {
205:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
206:         }
207: 
208:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
209:         {
210:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
211:             {
212:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
213:                 {
214:                     break;
215:                 }
216:             }
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
220:         {
221:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
225:         {
226:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
227:             {
228:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
229:                 led->OnStateChanged();
230:             }
231:         }
232: 
233:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
234:         {
235:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
236:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
237:             lock.unlock();
238:             for (auto &task : tasks)
239:             {
240:                 task();
241:             }
242:         }
243: 
244:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
245:         {
246:             clock_ticks_++;
247:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
248:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
249: 
250:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
251:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
252:             {
253:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
254:             }
255:         }
256:     }
257: }

259: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
260: {
261:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
262:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
263: 
264:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
265:     {
266:         // Network is ready, start activation
267:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
268:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
269:         {
270:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
271:             return;
272:         }
273: 
274:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
275:                     {
276:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
277:             app->ActivationTask();
278:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
279:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
280:     }
281: 
282:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
283:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
284:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
285: }
286: 
287: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
288: {
289:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
290:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
291:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
292:     {
293:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
294:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
295:     }
296: 
297:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
298:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
299:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
300: }

303: {
304:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
305: 
306:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
307:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
308: 
309:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
310: 
311:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
312:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();

315: 
316:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
317:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);

319:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
320:     ota_.reset();
321:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
322:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
323: }

325: void Application::ActivationTask()
326: {
327:     // Create OTA object for activation process
328:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
329: 
330:     // Check for new assets version
331:     CheckAssetsVersion();
332: 
333:     // Check for new firmware version
334:     CheckNewVersion();
335: 
336:     // Initialize the protocol
337:     InitializeProtocol();
338: 
339:     // Signal completion to main loop
340:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
341: }

343: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
344: {
345:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
346:     if (assets_version_checked_)
347:     {
348:         return;
349:     }
350:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
351: 
352:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
353:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
354:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
355: 
356:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
357:     {
358:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
359:         return;
360:     }
361: 
362:     Settings settings("assets", true);
363:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
364:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
365: 
366:     if (!download_url.empty())
367:     {
368:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
369: 
370:         char message[256];
371:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
372:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
373: 
374:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
375:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
376:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
377:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
378:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
379: 
380:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
381:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
382:                                                      {
383:                 char buffer[32];
384:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
385:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
386:                                              .detach(); });
387: 
388:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
389:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
390: 
391:         if (!success)
392:         {
393:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
394:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
395:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
396:             return;
397:         }
398:     }
399: 
400:     // Apply assets
401:     assets.Apply();
402:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
403:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
404: }

406: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
407: {
408:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
409:     int retry_count = 0;
410:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
411: 
412:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
413:     while (true)
414:     {
415:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
416:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
417: 
418:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
419:         if (err != ESP_OK)
420:         {
421:             retry_count++;
422:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
423:             {
424:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
425:                 return;
426:             }
427: 
428:             char error_message[128];
429:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
430:             char buffer[256];
431:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
432:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
433: 
434:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
435:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
436:             {
437:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
438:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
439:                 {
440:                     break;
441:                 }
442:             }
443:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
444:             continue;
445:         }
446:         retry_count = 0;
447:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
448: 
449:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
450:         {
451:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
452:             {
453:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
454:             }
455:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
456:         }
457: 
458:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
459:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
460:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
461:         {
462:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
463:             break;
464:         }
465: 
466:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
467:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
468:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
469:         {
470:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
471:         }
472: 
473:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
474:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
475:         {
476:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
477:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
478:             if (err == ESP_OK)
479:             {
480:                 break;
481:             }
482:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
483:             {
484:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
485:             }
486:             else
487:             {
488:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
489:             }
490:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
491:             {
492:                 break;
493:             }
494:         }
495:     }
496: }

498: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
499: {
500:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
501:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
502:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
503: 
504:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
505: 
506:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
507:     {
508:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
509:     }
510:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
511:     {
512:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
513:     }
514:     else
515:     {
516:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
517:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
518:     }
519: 
520:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
521:                            { DismissAlert(); });
522: 
523:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
524:                               {
525:         last_error_message_ = message;
526:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
527: 
528:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
529:                                {
530:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
531:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
532:         } });
533: 
534:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
535:                                     {
536:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
537:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
538:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
539:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
540:         } });
541: 
542:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
543:                                     {
544:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
545:         Schedule([this]() {
546:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
547:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
548:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
549:         }); });
550: 
551:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
552:                               {
553:         // Parse JSON data
554:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
555:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
556:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
557:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
558:                 Schedule([this]() {
559:                     aborted_ = false;
560:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
561:                 });
562:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
563:                 Schedule([this]() {
564:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
565:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
566:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
567:                         } else {
568:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
569:                         }
570:                     }
571:                 });
572:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
573:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
574:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
575:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
576:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
577:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
578:                     });
579:                 }
580:             }
581:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
582:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
583:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
584:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
585:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
586:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
587:                 });
588:             }
589:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
590:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
591:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
592:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
593:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
594:                 });
595:             }
596:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
597:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
598:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
599:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
600:             }
601:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
602:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
603:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
604:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
605:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
606:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
607:                     Schedule([this]() {
608:                         Reboot();
609:                     });
610:                 } else {
611:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
612:                 }
613:             }
614:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
615:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
616:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
617:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
618:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
619:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
620:             } else {
621:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
622:             }
623: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
624:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
625:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
626:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
627:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
628:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
629:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
630:                 });
631:             } else {
632:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
633:             }
634: #endif
635:         } else {
636:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
637:         } });

827: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
828: {
829:     if (!protocol_)
830:     {
831:         return;
832:     }
833: 
834:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
835: 
836:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
837:     {
838:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
839: 
840:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
841:         {
842:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
843:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
844:             {
845:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
846:                 return;
847:             }
848:         }
849: 
850:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
851:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
852: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
853:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
854:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
855:         {
856:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
857:         }
858:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
859:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
860:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
861: #else
862:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
863:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
864:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
865:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
866: #endif
867:     }
868:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
869:     {
870:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
871:     }
872:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
873:     {
874:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
875:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
876:     }
877: }

879: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
880: {
881:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
882:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
883: 
884:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
885:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
886:     auto led = board.GetLed();
887:     led->OnStateChanged();
888: 
889:     switch (new_state)
890:     {
891:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
892:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
893:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
894:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
895:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
896:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
897:         break;
898:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
899:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
900:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
901:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
902:         break;
903:     case kDeviceStateListening:
904:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
905:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
906: 
907:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
908:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
909:         {
910:             // Send the start listening command
911:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
912:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
913:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
914:         }
915: 
916:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
917:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
918:         {
919:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
920:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
921:         }
922:         break;
923:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
924:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
925: 
926:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
927:         {
928:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
929:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
930:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
931:         }
932:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
933:         break;
934:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
935:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
936:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
937:         break;
938:     default:
939:         // Do nothing
940:         break;
941:     }
942: }

984: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
985: {
986:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
987:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
988: 
989:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
990:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
991: 
992:     // Close audio channel if it's open
993:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
994:     {
995:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
996:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
997:     }
998:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
999: 
1000:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
1001:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1002: 
1003:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
1004: 
1005:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
1006:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
1007: 
1008:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
1009:     audio_service_.Stop();
1010:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
1011: 
1012:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
1013:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
1014:                                                       {
1015:             char buffer[32];
1016:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
1017:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
1018:                                               .detach(); });
1019: 
1020:     if (!upgrade_success)
1021:     {
1022:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
1023:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1024:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
1025:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
1026:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
1027:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1028:         return false;
1029:     }
1030:     else
1031:     {
1032:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
1033:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1034:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1035:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1036:         Reboot();
1037:         return true;
1038:     }
1039: }

The Application class initializes the system's core event management and AEC (Acoustic Echo Cancellation) mode based on configuration settings. It creates an event group for inter-task communication and sets up a periodic timer to generate clock tick events. The AEC mode is configured as either device-side, server-side, or disabled, ensuring mutual exclusivity between device and server AEC options.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly initializes the event group using `xEventGroupCreate()` and sets up a clock timer with a callback that signals an event bit, which aligns with the ground truth's intent.
- It also attempts to handle AEC mode selection via preprocessor definitions, although it uses different macro names (`CONFIG_ENABLE_DEVICE_AEC` vs `CONFIG_USE_DEVICE_AEC`) which may lead to incorrect behavior if not aligned with actual configuration headers.
- However, there are additional initializations for member variables not present in the ground truth, suggesting extended functionality beyond what was required.

Structure Similarity:
- Both versions use constructor initialization lists and define a timer creation block.
- The lambda capture and casting mechanism differ slightly (`(Application*)arg` vs `static_cast<Application*>(arg)`), but both achieve the same result.
- The ground truth includes conditional compilation checks with #error directive for conflicting configurations, which is missing from the generated version, reducing structural fidelity.

Functional Completeness:
- The core function of creating an event group and setting up a periodic clock timer is covered.
- However, critical error-checking logic ensuring mutual exclusion between device-side and server-side AEC modes is omitted.
- Additional fields like `state_machine_`, `listening_mode_`, and others indicate feature creep or inclusion of unrelated components, making the implementation broader than necessary.
- No explicit handling of `dispatch_method` and `skip_unhandled_events` in the timer args as seen in the ground truth, possibly affecting real-time behavior or task dispatch semantics.

results.description: The function initializes and configures the core system components including the display, audio service, and network interface. It sets up event-driven callbacks for audio processing, device state changes, and network events to manage user notifications and system behavior. Additionally, it starts periodic timers and registers common tools for the MCP server during initialization.

104:         }
105: 
106:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
107:         {
108:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
109:         }
110: 
111:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
112:         {
113:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
114:         }
115: 
116:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
117:         {
118:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
119:         }
120: 
121:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
122:         {
123:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
124:         }
125: 
126:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
127:         {
128:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
129:             {
130:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
131:                 {
132:                     break;
133:                 }
134:             }
135:         }
136: 
137:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
138:         {
139:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
140:         }
141: 
142:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
143:         {
144:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
145:             {
146:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
147:                 led->OnStateChanged();
148:             }
149:         }
150: 
151:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
152:         {
153:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
154:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
155:             lock.unlock();
156:             for (auto &task : tasks)
157:             {
158:                 task();
159:             }
160:         }
161: 
162:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
163:         {
164:             clock_ticks_++;
165:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
166:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
167: 
168:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
169:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
170:             {
171:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
172:             }
173:         }
174:     }
175: }

177: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
178: {
179:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
180:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
181: 
182:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
183:     {
184:         // Network is ready, start activation
185:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
186:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
187:         {
188:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
189:             return;
190:         }
191: 
192:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
193:                     {
194:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
195:             app->ActivationTask();
196:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
197:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
198:     }
199: 
200:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
201:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
202:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
203: }
204: 
205: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
206: {
207:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
208:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
209:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
210:     {
211:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
212:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
213:     }
214: 
215:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
216:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
217:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
218: }
219: 
220: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
221: {
222:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
223: 
224:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
225:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
226: 
227:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
228: 
229:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
230:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
231:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
232:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
233: 
234:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
235:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
236: 
237:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
238:     ota_.reset();
239:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
240:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
241: }
242: 
243: void Application::ActivationTask()
244: {
245:     // Create OTA object for activation process
246:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
247: 
248:     // Check for new assets version
249:     CheckAssetsVersion();
250: 
251:     // Check for new firmware version
252:     CheckNewVersion();
253: 
254:     // Initialize the protocol
255:     InitializeProtocol();
256: 
257:     // Signal completion to main loop
258:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
259: }
260: 
261: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
262: {
263:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
264:     if (assets_version_checked_)
265:     {
266:         return;
267:     }
268:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
269: 
270:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
271:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
272:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
273: 
274:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
275:     {
276:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
277:         return;
278:     }
279: 
280:     Settings settings("assets", true);
281:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
282:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
283: 
284:     if (!download_url.empty())
285:     {
286:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
287: 
288:         char message[256];
289:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
290:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
291: 
292:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
293:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
294:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
295:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
296:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
297: 
298:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
299:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
300:                                                      {
301:                 char buffer[32];
302:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
303:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
304:                                              .detach(); });
305: 
306:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
307:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
308: 
309:         if (!success)
310:         {
311:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
312:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
313:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
314:             return;
315:         }
316:     }
317: 
318:     // Apply assets
319:     assets.Apply();
320:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
321:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
322: }
323: 
324: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
325: {
326:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
327:     int retry_count = 0;
328:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
329: 
330:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
331:     while (true)
332:     {
333:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
334:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
335: 
336:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
337:         if (err != ESP_OK)
338:         {
339:             retry_count++;
340:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
341:             {
342:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
343:                 return;
344:             }
345: 
346:             char error_message[128];
347:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
348:             char buffer[256];
349:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
350:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
351: 
352:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
353:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
354:             {
355:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
356:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
357:                 {
358:                     break;
359:                 }
360:             }
361:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
362:             continue;
363:         }
364:         retry_count = 0;
365:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
366: 
367:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
368:         {
369:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
370:             {
371:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
372:             }
373:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
374:         }
375: 
376:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
377:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
378:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
379:         {
380:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
381:             break;
382:         }
383: 
384:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
385:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
386:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
387:         {
388:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
389:         }
390: 
391:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
392:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
393:         {
394:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
395:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
396:             if (err == ESP_OK)
397:             {
398:                 break;
399:             }
400:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
401:             {
402:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
403:             }
404:             else
405:             {
406:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
407:             }
408:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
409:             {
410:                 break;
411:             }
412:         }
413:     }
414: }
415: 
416: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
417: {
418:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
419:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
420:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
421: 
422:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
423: 
424:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
425:     {
426:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
427:     }
428:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
429:     {
430:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
431:     }
432:     else
433:     {
434:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
435:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
436:     }
437: 
438:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
439:                            { DismissAlert(); });
440: 
441:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
442:                               {
443:         last_error_message_ = message;
444:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
445: 
446:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
447:                                {
448:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
449:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
450:         } });
451: 
452:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
453:                                     {
454:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
455:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
456:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
457:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
458:         } });
459: 
460:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
461:                                     {
462:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
463:         Schedule([this]() {
464:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
465:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
466:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
467:         }); });
468: 
469:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
470:                               {
471:         // Parse JSON data
472:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
473:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
474:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
475:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
476:                 Schedule([this]() {
477:                     aborted_ = false;
478:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
479:                 });
480:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
481:                 Schedule([this]() {
482:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
483:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
484:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
485:                         } else {
486:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
487:                         }
488:                     }
489:                 });
490:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
491:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
492:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
493:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
494:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
495:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
496:                     });
497:                 }
498:             }
499:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
500:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
501:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
502:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
503:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
504:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
505:                 });
506:             }
507:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
508:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
509:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
510:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
511:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
512:                 });
513:             }
514:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
515:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
516:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
517:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
518:             }
519:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
520:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
521:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
522:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
523:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
524:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
525:                     Schedule([this]() {
526:                         Reboot();
527:                     });
528:                 } else {
529:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
530:                 }
531:             }
532:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
533:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
534:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
535:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
536:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
537:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
538:             } else {
539:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
540:             }
541: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
542:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
543:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
544:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
545:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
546:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
547:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
548:                 });
549:             } else {
550:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
551:             }
552: #endif
553:         } else {
554:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
555:         } });
556: 
557:     protocol_->Start();
558: }
559: 
560: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
561: {
562:     struct digit_sound
563:     {
564:         char digit;
565:         const std::string_view &sound;
566:     };
567:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
568:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
569:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
570:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
571:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
572:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
573:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
574:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
575:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
576:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
577: 
578:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
579:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
580: 
581:     for (const auto &digit : code)
582:     {
583:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
584:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
585:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
586:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
587:         {
588:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
589:         }
590:     }
591: }
592: 
593: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
594: {
595:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
596:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
597:     display->SetStatus(status);
598:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
599:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
600:     if (!sound.empty())
601:     {
602:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
603:     }
604: }

611:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
612:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
613:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
614:     }

632: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
633: {
634:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
635: 
636:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
637:     {
638:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
639:         return;
640:     }
641:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
642:     {
643:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
644:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
645:         return;
646:     }
647:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
648:     {
649:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
650:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
651:         return;
652:     }
653: 
654:     if (!protocol_)
655:     {
656:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
657:         return;
658:     }
659: 
660:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
661:     {
662:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
663:         {
664:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
665:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
666:             {
667:                 return;
668:             }
669:         }
670: 
671:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
672:     }
673:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
674:     {
675:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
676:     }
677:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
678:     {
679:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
680:     }
681: }
682: 
683: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
684: {
685:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
686: 
687:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
688:     {
689:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
690:         return;
691:     }
692:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
693:     {
694:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
695:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
696:         return;
697:     }
698: 
699:     if (!protocol_)
700:     {
701:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
702:         return;
703:     }
704: 
705:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
706:     {
707:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
708:         {
709:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
710:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
711:             {
712:                 return;
713:             }
714:         }
715: 
716:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
717:     }
718:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
719:     {
720:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
721:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
722:     }
723: }

725: void Application::HandleStopListeningEvent()
726: {
727:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
728: 
729:     if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
730:     {
731:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
732:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
733:         return;
734:     }
735:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
736:     {
737:         if (protocol_)
738:         {
739:             protocol_->SendStopListening();
740:         }
741:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
742:     }
743: }
744: 
745: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
746: {
747:     if (!protocol_)
748:     {
749:         return;
750:     }
751: 
752:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
753: 
754:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
755:     {
756:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
757: 
758:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
759:         {
760:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
761:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
762:             {
763:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
764:                 return;
765:             }
766:         }
767: 
768:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
769:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
770: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
771:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
772:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
773:         {
774:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
775:         }
776:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
777:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
778:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
779: #else

781:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
782:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
783:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
784: #endif
785:     }
786:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
787:     {
788:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
789:     }
790:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
791:     {
792:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
793:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
794:     }

797: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
798: {
799:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
800:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
801: 
802:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
803:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
804:     auto led = board.GetLed();
805:     led->OnStateChanged();
806: 
807:     switch (new_state)
808:     {
809:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
810:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
811:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
812:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
813:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
814:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
815:         break;
816:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
817:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
818:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
819:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
820:         break;
821:     case kDeviceStateListening:
822:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
823:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
824: 
825:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
826:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
827:         {
828:             // Send the start listening command
829:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
830:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
831:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
832:         }
833: 
834:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
835:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
836:         {
837:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
838:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
839:         }
840:         break;
841:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
842:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
843: 
844:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
845:         {
846:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
847:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
848:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
849:         }
850:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
851:         break;
852:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
853:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
854:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
855:         break;
856:     default:
857:         // Do nothing
858:         break;
859:     }
860: }

902: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
903: {
904:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
905:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
906: 
907:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
908:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
909: 
910:     // Close audio channel if it's open
911:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
912:     {
913:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
914:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
915:     }
916:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
917: 
918:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
919:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
920: 
921:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
922: 
923:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
924:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
925: 
926:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
927:     audio_service_.Stop();
928:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
929: 
930:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
931:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
932:                                                       {
933:             char buffer[32];
934:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
935:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
936:                                               .detach(); });
937: 
938:     if (!upgrade_success)
939:     {
940:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
941:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
942:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
943:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
944:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
945:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
946:         return false;
947:     }
948:     else
949:     {
950:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
951:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
952:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
953:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
954:         Reboot();
955:         return true;
956:     }
957: }

959: void Application::WakeWordInvoke(const std::string &wake_word)
960: {
961:     if (!protocol_)
962:     {
963:         return;
964:     }
965: 
966:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
967: 
968:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
969:     {
970:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
971: 
972:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
973:         {
974:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
975:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
976:             {
977:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
978:                 return;
979:             }
980:         }
981: 
982:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
983: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
984:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
985:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
986:         {
987:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
988:         }
989:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
990:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
991:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
992: #else
993:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
994:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
995:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
996:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
997: #endif
998:     }
999:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
1000:     {
1001:         Schedule([this]()
1002:                  { AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone); });
1003:     }
1004:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
1005:     {
1006:         Schedule([this]()
1007:                  {
1008:             if (protocol_) {
1009:                 protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1010:             } });
1011:     }
1012: }

The function initializes and configures the core system components including the display, audio service, and network interface. It sets up event-driven callbacks for audio processing, device state changes, and network events to manage user notifications and system behavior. Additionally, it starts periodic timers and registers common tools for the MCP server during initialization.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code significantly diverges from the ground truth both semantically and structurally.

Semantic Analysis:
- The ground truth `Initialize()` function sets up various services (display, audio, network), registers callbacks, starts timers, and initializes system components.
- In contrast, the generated version not only performs initialization but also includes an infinite event loop (`while(true)`), which changes the entire semantic purpose of the function. This suggests a confusion between initialization logic and runtime event handling.
- Key setup steps such as setting chat messages, registering specific tools with `McpServer`, adding state change listeners, or configuring detailed network event callbacks are missing in the generated code.

Structural Differences:
- Function signature differs; the ground truth appears to be a constructor or part of one (indicated by trailing `} void Application::Run()`), whereas the generated is explicitly defined as `void Application::Initialize()`.
- Control flow differs drastically — ground truth follows sequential initialization, while generated combines initialization with an embedded run-loop.
- API usage varies: e.g., `audio_service_.Initialize(codec)` is missing in generated; instead it directly calls `audio_service_.Start()`. Also, `SetNetworkEventCallback` logic is completely absent in generated code.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical functionalities like:
  * Display message setting via `SetChatMessage`
  * Audio codec initialization properly linked to audio service
  * Registration of specific toolsets (`AddCommonTools`, `AddUserOnlyTools`)
  * Detailed network event handling with UI notifications
  * State machine listener registration
- Some overlapping elements exist, such as starting the clock timer and basic access to board/display/audio services, but these are insufficient for functional parity.

Overall, the generated code conflates two distinct phases (initialization vs. execution loop) and omits most of the required setup procedures found in the reference implementation.

results.description: The function manages the main event loop of the application, processing various system and user events such as network changes, audio commands, and state transitions. It coordinates core functionalities like error handling, chat toggling, listening states, and scheduled tasks while updating the device state and UI accordingly. The loop ensures responsive control flow and system stability by reacting to both internal and external triggers in real time.

288:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
289:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
290:                 return;
291:             }
292:         }
293: 
294:         // Apply assets
295:         assets.Apply();
296:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
297:         display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
298:     }
299: 
300:     void Application::CheckNewVersion()
301:     {
302:         const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
303:         int retry_count = 0;
304:         int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
305: 
306:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
307:         while (true)
308:         {
309:             auto display = board.GetDisplay();
310:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
311: 
312:             esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
313:             if (err != ESP_OK)
314:             {
315:                 retry_count++;
316:                 if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
317:                 {
318:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
319:                     return;
320:                 }
321: 
322:                 char error_message[128];
323:                 snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
324:                 char buffer[256];
325:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
326:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
327: 
328:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
329:                 for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
330:                 {
331:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
332:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
333:                     {
334:                         break;
335:                     }
336:                 }
337:                 retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
338:                 continue;
339:             }
340:             retry_count = 0;
341:             retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
342: 
343:             if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
344:             {
345:                 if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
346:                 {
347:                     return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
348:                 }
349:                 // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
350:             }
351: 
352:             // No new version, mark the current version as valid
353:             ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
354:             if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
355:             {
356:                 // Exit the loop if done checking new version
357:                 break;
358:             }
359: 
360:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
361:             // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
362:             if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
363:             {
364:                 ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
365:             }
366: 
367:             // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
368:             for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
369:             {
370:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
371:                 esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
372:                 if (err == ESP_OK)
373:                 {
374:                     break;
375:                 }
376:                 else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
377:                 {
378:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
379:                 }
380:                 else
381:                 {
382:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
383:                 }
384:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
385:                 {
386:                     break;
387:                 }
388:             }
389:         }
390:     }
