The `CheckAssetsVersion` function verifies the validity of the assets partition on the device's storage and ensures this check is performed only once during the application lifecycle. If the partition is invalid or disabled, it logs a warning message indicating that assets are not available for the current board configuration. This function also signals the completion of the activation process by setting an event bit in the main event group.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness: The generated code does not align well semantically with the ground truth. While both functions seem to handle post-activation logic, the ground truth focuses on initializing protocol and signaling completion via an event group, whereas the generated code includes UI updates, emotion settings, and enabling wake word detection which are unrelated to the core activation-done signaling mechanism. Additionally, the error handling and asset checking present in the ground truth is completely missing in the generated version.

Structure Similarity: There's partial structural similarity in that both functions belong to the `Application` class and perform some form of post-initialization tasks. However, the function signature differs (ground truth appears to be a continuation or part of another function based on closing brace), and control flow diverges significantly — the ground truth calls `InitializeProtocol()` and sets event bits, while the generated code has a more complex sequence involving display updates, audio service interactions, and state management. API call patterns also differ substantially.

Functional Completeness: The generated code misses critical functionalities from the ground truth such as setting the `assets_version_checked_` flag, accessing invalid assets partitions, logging warnings about asset partitions, and most importantly, signaling the main loop through `xEventGroupSetBits`. Although it performs additional actions like updating display emotions and enabling wake word detection, these were not required by the ground truth and therefore do not compensate for the missing core functionality.

results.description: The function manages the download and application of assets, displaying progress and handling errors, while also checking for new firmware versions with retry logic and user alerts. It coordinates hardware power management and updates the display with status messages throughout the process.

81:     callbacks.on_send_queue_available = [this]()
82:     {
83:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO);
84:     };
85:     callbacks.on_wake_word_detected = [this](const std::string &wake_word)
86:     {
87:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED);
88:     };
89:     callbacks.on_vad_change = [this](bool speaking)
90:     {
91:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE);
92:     };
93:     audio_service_.SetCallbacks(callbacks);
94: 
95:     // Add state change listeners
96:     state_machine_.AddStateChangeListener([this](DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
97:                                           { xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED); });
98: 
99:     // Start the clock timer to update the status bar
100:     esp_timer_start_periodic(clock_timer_handle_, 1000000);
101: 
102:     // Add MCP common tools (only once during initialization)
103:     auto &mcp_server = McpServer::GetInstance();
104:     mcp_server.AddCommonTools();
105:     mcp_server.AddUserOnlyTools();
106: 
107:     // Set network event callback for UI updates and network state handling
108:     board.SetNetworkEventCallback([this](NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
109:                                   {
110:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
111:         
112:         switch (event) {
113:             case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
114:                 display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::SCANNING_WIFI, 30000);
115:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
116:                 break;
117:             case NetworkEvent::Connecting: {
118:                 if (data.empty()) {
119:                     // Cellular network - registering without carrier info yet
120:                     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
121:                 } else {
122:                     // WiFi or cellular with carrier info
123:                     std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECT_TO;
124:                     msg += data;
125:                     msg += "...";
126:                     display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
127:                 }
128:                 break;
129:             }
130:             case NetworkEvent::Connected: {
131:                 std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECTED_TO;
132:                 msg += data;
133:                 display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
134:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED);
135:                 break;
136:             }
137:             case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
138:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
139:                 break;
140:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
141:                 // WiFi config mode enter is handled by WifiBoard internally
142:                 break;
143:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
144:                 // WiFi config mode exit is handled by WifiBoard internally
145:                 break;
146:             // Cellular modem specific events
147:             case NetworkEvent::ModemDetecting:
148:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
149:                 break;
150:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorNoSim:
151:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::PIN_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_PIN);
152:                 break;
153:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorRegDenied:
154:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::REG_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_REG);
155:                 break;
156:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorInitFailed:
157:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
158:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
159:                 break;
160:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorTimeout:
161:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
162:                 break;
163:         } });
164: 
165:     // Start network asynchronously
166:     board.StartNetwork();
167: 
168:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
169:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
170: }
171: 
172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }
284: 
285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }
368: 
369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void {
407:             if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
408:             {
409:                 if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
410:                 {
411:                     return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
412:                 }
413:                 // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
414:             }
415: 
416:             // No new version, mark the current version as valid
417:             ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
418:             if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
419:             {
420:                 // Exit the loop if done checking new version
421:                 break;
422:             }
423: 
424:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
425:             // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
426:             if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
427:             {
428:                 ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
429:             }
430: 
431:             // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
432:             for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
433:             {
434:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
435:                 esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
436:                 if (err == ESP_OK)
437:                 {
438:                     break;
439:                 }
440:                 else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
441:                 {
442:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
443:                 }
444:                 else
445:                 {
446:                     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
447:                 }
448:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
449:                 {
450:                     break;
451:                 }
452:             }
453:     }
454:     }
455: 
456:     void Application::InitializeProtocol()
457:     {
458:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
459:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
460:         auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
461: 
462:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
463: 
464:         if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
465:         {
466:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
467:         }
468:         else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
469:         {
470:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
471:         }
472:         else
473:         {
474:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
475:             protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
476:         }
477: 
478:         protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
479:                                { DismissAlert(); });
480: 
481:         protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
482:                                   {
483:         last_error_message_ = message;
484:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
485: 
486:         protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
487:                                    {
488:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
489:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
490:         } });
491: 
492:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
493:                                         {
494:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
495:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
496:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
497:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
498:         } });
499: 
500:         protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
501:                                         {
502:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
503:         Schedule([this]() {
504:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
505:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
506:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
507:         }); });
508: 
509:         protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
510:                                   {
511:         // Parse JSON data
512:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
513:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
514:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
515:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
516:                 Schedule([this]() {
517:                     aborted_ = false;
518:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
519:                 });
520:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
521:                 Schedule([this]() {
522:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
523:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
524:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
525:                         } else {
526:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
527:                         }
528:                     }
529:                 });
530:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
531:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
532:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
533:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
534:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
535:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
536:                     });
537:                 }
538:             }
539:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
540:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
541:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
542:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
543:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
544:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
545:                 });
546:             }
547:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
548:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
549:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
550:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
551:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
552:                 });
553:             }
554:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
555:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
556:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
557:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
558:             }
559:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
560:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
561:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
562:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
563:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
564:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
565:                     Schedule([this]() {
566:                         Reboot();
567:                     });
568:                 } else {
569:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
570:                 }
571:             }
572:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
573:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
574:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
575:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
576:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
577:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
578:             } else {
579:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
580:             }
581: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
582:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
583:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
584:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
585:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
586:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
587:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
588:                 });
589:             } else {
590:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
591:             }
592: #endif
593:         } else {
594:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
595:         } });
596: 
597:         protocol_->Start();
598:     }

600:     void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
601:     {
602:         struct digit_sound
603:         {
604:             char digit;
605:             const std::string_view &sound;
606:         };
607:         static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
608:                                                                digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
609:                                                                digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
610:                                                                digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
611:                                                                digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
612:                                                                digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
613:                                                                digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
614:                                                                digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
615:                                                                digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
616:                                                                digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
617: 
618:         // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
619:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
620: 
621:         for (const auto &digit : code)
622:         {
623:             auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
624:                                    [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
625:                                    { return ds.digit == digit; });
626:             if (it != digit_sounds.end())
627:             {
628:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
629:             }
630:         }
631:     }

633:     void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
634:     {
635:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
636:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
637:         display->SetStatus(status);
638:         display->SetEmotion(emotion);
639:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
640:         if (!sound.empty())
641:         {
642:             audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
643:         }
644:     }

649:         {

651:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
652:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
653:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
654:         }
655:     }

672:     void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
673:     {
674:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
675: 
676:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
677:         {
678:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
679:             return;
680:         }
681:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
682:         {
683:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
684:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
685:             return;
686:         }
687:         else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
688:         {
689:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
690:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
691:             return;
692:         }
693: 
694:         if (!protocol_)
695:         {
696:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
697:             return;
698:         }
699: 
700:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
701:         {
702:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
703:             {
704:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
705:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
706:                 {
707:                     return;
708:                 }
709:             }
710: 
711:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
712:         }
713:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
714:         {
715:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
716:         }
717:         else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
718:         {
719:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
720:         }
721:     }
722: 
723:     void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
724:     {
725:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
726: 
727:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
728:         {

942:     bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
943:     {
944:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
945:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
946: 
947:         std::string upgrade_url = url;
948:         std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
949: 
950:         // Close audio channel if it's open
951:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
952:         {
953:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
954:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
955:         }
956:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
957: 
958:         Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
959:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
960: 
961:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
962: 
963:         std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
964:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
965: 
966:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
967:         audio_service_.Stop();
968:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
969: 
970:         bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
971:                                             { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
972:                                                           {
973:             char buffer[32];
974:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
975:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
976:                                                   .detach(); });
977: 
978:         if (!upgrade_success)
979:         {
980:             // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
981:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
982:             audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
983:             board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
984:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
985:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
986:             return false;
987:         }
988:         else
989:         {
990:             // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
991:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
992:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
993:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
994:             Reboot();
995:             return true;
996:         }
997:     }

The function manages the download and application of assets, displaying progress and handling errors, while also checking for new firmware versions with retry logic and user alerts. It coordinates hardware power management and updates the display with status messages throughout the process.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement asset version checking and downloading but diverges significantly in logic.
- It introduces additional firmware upgrade and activation logic not present in the ground truth's CheckAssetsVersion function.
- The ground truth separates version checking (CheckNewVersion) from asset application, whereas the generated code conflates asset handling with firmware upgrades and activation.
- Download callback implementation differs slightly in formatting and thread handling.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature mismatch: ground truth shows CheckAssetsVersion as part of a larger function ending with CheckNewVersion, while generated code is a standalone function.
- Both use similar APIs (Board::GetInstance, Assets::GetInstance, display operations, vTaskDelay).
- Control flow differs substantially - generated code includes firmware/activation logic not in the reference.
- Progress reporting mechanism exists in both but implemented differently (threaded vs direct call).

Functional Completeness:
- Generated code covers core asset download functionality and progress reporting.
- Missing power save level adjustments during download present in ground truth.
- Lacks proper error handling/retry mechanisms shown in CheckNewVersion.
- Introduces extra functionality (firmware upgrade, activation) beyond the scope of asset version checking.
- No equivalent of the post-download asset apply step from the ground truth.

results.description: The function manages device activation and firmware updates by checking for new versions, performing upgrades if available, and handling activation codes or challenges. If no update is needed, it marks the current firmware as valid and proceeds to activate the device if required. The protocol initialization sets up communication channels (MQTT/WebSocket) and handles audio streaming with power management based on connection states.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }

351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }

369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0)
480:                 {
481:                     Schedule([this]()
482:                              {
483:                     aborted_ = false;
484:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking); });
485:                 }
486:                 else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0)
487:                 {
488:                     Schedule([this]()
489:                              {
490:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
491:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
492:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
493:                         } else {
494:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
495:                         }
496:                     } });
497:                 }
498:                 else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0)
499:                 {
500:                     auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
501:                     if (cJSON_IsString(text))
502:                     {
503:                         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
504:                         Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
505:                                  { display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str()); });
506:                     }
507:                 }
508:             }
509:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0)
510:             {
511:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
512:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text))
513:                 {
514:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
515:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]()
516:                              { display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str()); });
517:                 }
518:             }
519:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0)
520:             {
521:                 auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
522:                 if (cJSON_IsString(emotion))
523:                 {
524:                     Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]()
525:                              { display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str()); });
526:                 }
527:             }
528:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0)
529:             {
530:                 auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
531:                 if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
532:                 {
533:                     McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
534:                 }
535:             }
536:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0)
537:             {
538:                 auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
539:                 if (cJSON_IsString(command))
540:                 {
541:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
542:                     if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0)
543:                     {
544:                         // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
545:                         Schedule([this]()
546:                                  { Reboot(); });
547:                     }
548:                     else
549:                     {
550:                         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
551:                     }
552:                 }
553:             }
554:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0)
555:             {
556:                 auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
557:                 auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
558:                 auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
559:                 if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion))
560:                 {
561:                     Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
562:                 }
563:                 else
564:                 {
565:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
566:                 }
567: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
568:             }
569:             else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0)
570:             {
571:                 auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
572:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
573:                 if (cJSON_IsObject(payload))
574:                 {
575:                     Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]()
576:                              { display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str()); });
577:                 }
578:                 else
579:                 {
580:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
581:                 }
582: #endif
583:             }
584:             else
585:             {
586:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
587:             }
588:         });
589: 
590:         protocol_->Start();
591:     }

593:     void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
594:     {
595:         struct digit_sound
596:         {
597:             char digit;
598:             const std::string_view &sound;
599:         };
600:         static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
601:                                                                digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
602:                                                                digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
603:                                                                digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
604:                                                                digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
605:                                                                digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
606:                                                                digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
607:                                                                digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
608:                                                                digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
609:                                                                digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
610: 
611:         // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
612:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
613: 
614:         for (const auto &digit : code)
615:         {
616:             auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
617:                                    [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
618:                                    { return ds.digit == digit; });
619:             if (it != digit_sounds.end())
620:             {
621:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
622:             }
623:         }
624:     }

626:     void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)

629:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
630:         display->SetStatus(status);
631:         display->SetEmotion(emotion);
632:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
633:         if (!sound.empty())
634:         {
635:             audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
636:         }

665:     void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
666:     {
667:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
668: 
669:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
670:         {
671:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
672:             return;
673:         }
674:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
675:         {
676:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
677:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
678:             return;
679:         }
680:         else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
681:         {
682:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
683:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
684:             return;
685:         }
686: 
687:         if (!protocol_)
688:         {
689:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
690:             return;
691:         }
692: 
693:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
694:         {
695:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
696:             {
697:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
698:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
699:                 {
700:                     return;
701:                 }
702:             }
703: 
704:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
705:         }
706:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
707:         {
708:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
709:         }
710:         else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
711:         {
712:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
713:         }
714:     }

716:     void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
717:     {
718:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
719: 
720:         if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
721:         {
722:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
723:             return;
724:         }
725:         else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
726:         {
727:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
728:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
729:             return;
730:         }
731: 
732:         if (!protocol_)
733:         {
734:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
735:             return;
736:         }
737: 
738:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
739:         {
740:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
741:             {
742:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
743:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
744:                 {
745:                     return;
746:                 }
747:             }
748: 
749:             SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
750:         }
751:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
752:         {
753:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
754:             SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
755:         }
756:     }
757: 
758:     void Application::HandleStopListeningEvent()
759:     {
760:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
761: 
762:         if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
763:         {
764:             audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
765:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
766:             return;
767:         }
768:         else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
769:         {
770:             if (protocol_)
771:             {
772:                 protocol_->SendStopListening();
773:             }
774:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
775:         }
776:     }
777: 
778:     void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
779:     {
780:         if (!protocol_)
781:         {
782:             return;
783:         }
784: 
785:         auto state = GetDeviceState();
786: 
787:         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
788:         {
789:             audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
790: 
791:             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
792:             {
793:                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
794:                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
795:                 {
796:                     audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
797:                     return;
798:                 }
799:             }
800: 
801:             auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
802:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
803: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
804:             // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
805:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
806:             {
807:                 protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
808:             }
809:             // Set the chat state to wake word detected
810:             protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
811:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
812: #else
813:             // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
814:             // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
815:             play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
816:             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
817: #endif
818:         }
819:         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
820:         {
821:             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
822:         }
823:         else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
824:         {
825:             // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
826:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
827:         }
828:     }
829: 
830:     void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
831:     {
832:         DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
833:         clock_ticks_ = 0;
834: 
835:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
836:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
837:         auto led = board.GetLed();
838:         led->OnStateChanged();
839: 
840:         switch (new_state)
841:         {
842:         case kDeviceStateUnknown:
843:         case kDeviceStateIdle:
844:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
845:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
846:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
847:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
848:             break;
849:         case kDeviceStateConnecting:
850:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
851:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
852:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
853:             break;
854:         case kDeviceStateListening:
855:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
856:             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
857: 
858:             // Make sure the audio processor is running
859:             if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
860:             {
861:                 // Send the start listening command
862:                 protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
863:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
864:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
865:             }
866: 
867:             // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
868:             if (play_popup_on_listening_)
869:             {
870:                 play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
871:                 audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
872:             }
873:             break;
874:         case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
875:             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
876: 
877:             if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
878:             {
879:                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
880:                 // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
881:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
882:             }
883:             audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
884:             break;
885:         case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
886:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
887:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
888:             break;
889:         default:
890:             // Do nothing
891:             break;
892:         }
893:     }
894: 
895:     void Application::Schedule(std::function<void()> && callback)
896:     {
897:         {
898:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
899:             main_tasks_.push_back(std::move(callback));
900:         }
901:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE);
902:     }
903: 
904:     void Application::AbortSpeaking(AbortReason reason)
905:     {
906:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Abort speaking");
907:         aborted_ = true;
908:         if (protocol_)
909:         {
910:             protocol_->SendAbortSpeaking(reason);

935:     bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
936:     {
937:         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
938:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
939: 
940:         std::string upgrade_url = url;
941:         std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
942: 
943:         // Close audio channel if it's open
944:         if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
945:         {
946:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
947:             protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
948:         }
949:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
950: 
951:         Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
952:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
953: 
954:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
955: 
956:         std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
957:         display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
958: 
959:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
960:         audio_service_.Stop();
961:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
962: 
963:         bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
964:                                             { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
965:                                                           {
966:             char buffer[32];
967:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
968:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
969:                                                   .detach(); });
970: 
971:         if (!upgrade_success)
972:         {
973:             // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
974:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
975:             audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
976:             board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
977:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
978:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
979:             return false;
980:         }
981:         else
982:         {
983:             // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
984:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
985:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
986:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
987:             Reboot();
988:             return true;
989:         }
990:     }

1014:

1016: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
1017:             // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
1018:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
1019:             {
1020:                 protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
1021:             }
1022:             // Set the chat state to wake word detected

The function manages device activation and firmware updates by checking for new versions, performing upgrades if available, and handling activation codes or challenges. If no update is needed, it marks the current firmware as valid and proceeds to activate the device if required. The protocol initialization sets up communication channels (MQTT/WebSocket) and handles audio streaming with power management based on connection states.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement a version checking mechanism with retries and exponential backoff.
- However, it misses critical parts like activation handling (activation codes/challenges) which are central to the ground truth logic.
- The semantics around firmware upgrade are partially correct but lack proper handling of post-upgrade states and integration with the broader application lifecycle.
- It doesn't handle marking the current version as valid when no new version is found, which is essential behavior in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature differs from what seems implied by the task (`CheckNewVersion()()` suggests possible functor or nested function, whereas generated is a standard method).
- Control flow diverges significantly; ground truth uses an infinite loop that breaks under certain conditions, while generated uses a finite retry loop that exits after one successful check.
- Some API calls match (e.g., `ota_->CheckVersion`, `ota_->HasNewVersion`), but overall call sequence and context differ.
- Missing structures such as event group handling, protocol initialization, and audio stream callbacks indicate structural incompleteness.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality like checking for new versions and attempting upgrades is present.
- Critical functions like activation processing, error alerting with localization/sound, and deep integration with display/audio services are missing.
- No support for MQTT/WebSocket protocol setup or handling incoming data streams.
- Lacks device state management during activation and post-audio channel events.
- Retry logic exists but omits sophisticated user feedback and interaction patterns seen in the ground truth.

results.description: This function processes incoming JSON messages to control device behavior, manage audio and display states, and handle system commands. It updates the device state based on "play", "stop", or other triggers, displays chat messages and emotions, and executes system-level actions like rebooting. The function serves as a central message router that translates remote commands into local hardware and UI responses.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)

281:         }
282:     }

414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));

417:         if (!success)

420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425:

427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }

637:     void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
638:     {
639:                         if (!protocol_)
640:                         {
641:                             return;
642:                         }
643: 
644:                         auto state = GetDeviceState();
645: 
646:                         if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
647:                         {
648:                             audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
649: 
650:                             if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
651:                             {
652:                                 SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
653:                                 if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
654:                                 {
655:                                     audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
656:                                     return;
657:                                 }
658:                             }
659: 
660:                             auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
661:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
662: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
663:                             // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
664:                             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
665:                             {
666:                                 protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
667:                             }
668:                             // Set the chat state to wake word detected
669:                             protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
670:                             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
671: #else
672:                             // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
673:                             // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
674:                             play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
675:                             SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
676: #endif
677:                         }
678:                         else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
679:                         {
680:                             AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
681:                         }
682:                         else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
683:                         {
684:                             // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
685:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
686:                         }
687:     }

689:     void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
690:     {
691:                         DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
692:                         clock_ticks_ = 0;
693: 
694:                         auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
695:                         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
696:                         auto led = board.GetLed();
697:                         led->OnStateChanged();
698: 
699:                         switch (new_state)
700:                         {
701:                         case kDeviceStateUnknown:
702:                         case kDeviceStateIdle:
703:                             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
704:                             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
705:                             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
706:                             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
707:                             break;
708:                         case kDeviceStateConnecting:
709:                             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
710:                             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
711:                             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
712:                             break;
713:                         case kDeviceStateListening:
714:                             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
715:                             display->SetEmotion("neutral");
716: 
717:                             // Make sure the audio processor is running
718:                             if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
719:                             {
720:                                 // Send the start listening command
721:                                 protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
722:                                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
723:                                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
724:                             }
725: 
726:                             // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
727:                             if (play_popup_on_listening_)
728:                             {
729:                                 play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
730:                                 audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
731:                             }
732:                             break;
733:                         case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
734:                             display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
735: 
736:                             if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
737:                             {
738:                                 audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
739:                                 // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
740:                                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
741:                             }
742:                             audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
743:                             break;
744:                         case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
745:                             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
746:                             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
747:                             break;
748:                         default:
749:                             // Do nothing
750:                             break;
751:                         }
752:     }
