853: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
854: {
855:     if (!protocol_)
856:     {
857:         return;
858:     }
859: 
860:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
861: 
862:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
863:     {
864:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
865: 
866:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
867:         {
868:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
869:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
870:             {
871:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
872:                 return;
873:             }
874:         }
875: 
876:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
877:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
878: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
879:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
880:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
881:         {
882:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
883:         }
884:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
885:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
886:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
887: #else
888:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
889:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
890:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
891:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
892: #endif
893:     }
894:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
895:     {
896:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
897:     }
898:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
899:     {
900:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
901:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
902:     }
903: }

The function manages the firmware upgrade process by first delaying execution, setting the device state to upgrading, and displaying a message. It stops audio services, performs the OTA upgrade with progress updates, and handles success or failure outcomes. On success, it reboots the device; on failure, it restarts audio services and alerts the user.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code and ground truth implement fundamentally different behaviors for HandleStateChangedEvent(), resulting in low scores across all dimensions.

Semantic Correctness (30): The generated code implements a generic state machine handler that updates UI elements based on device state transitions. In contrast, the ground truth specifically handles a firmware upgrade scenario with delays, OTA operations, and service management. While both functions respond to state changes, their core logic and purpose are entirely different - the generated version is a general-purpose state observer while the ground truth performs a specific complex workflow.

Structure Similarity (40): Both functions share some superficial similarities like accessing display objects and using switch statements, but the overall structure diverges significantly. The ground truth has a linear procedural flow with specific timing delays and upgrade logic, while the generated code follows a state-machine pattern with per-state handling. Function signatures differ (generated takes no parameters vs implied state context in ground truth). Control flow is markedly different - sequential steps vs conditional state handling.

Functional Completeness (25): The generated code completely misses all critical functionality from the ground truth including: firmware upgrade initiation (Ota::Upgrade), network operations (upgrade_url handling), progress callbacks with threading, audio service interruption and restoration, power management changes, error handling for failed upgrades, device reboot sequences, and specific timing delays. Instead, it provides generic UI state synchronization which, while potentially useful, doesn't address any of the core upgrade-related responsibilities shown in the ground truth.

The fundamental mismatch suggests either the prompt was ambiguous or the generation misunderstood the required behavior. The generated implementation appears to be a valid state change handler but for a completely different use case than what's demonstrated in the ground truth.

94: 
95:     // Add state change listeners
96:     state_machine_.AddStateChangeListener([this](DeviceState old_state, DeviceState new_state)
97:                                           { xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED); });
98: 
99:     // Start the clock timer to update the status bar
100:     esp_timer_start_periodic(clock_timer_handle_, 1000000);
101: 
102:     // Add MCP common tools (only once during initialization)
103:     auto &mcp_server = McpServer::GetInstance();
104:     mcp_server.AddCommonTools();
105:     mcp_server.AddUserOnlyTools();
106: 
107:     // Set network event callback for UI updates and network state handling
108:     board.SetNetworkEventCallback([this](NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
109:                                   {
110:         auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
111:         
112:         switch (event) {
113:             case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
114:                 display->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::SCANNING_WIFI, 30000);
115:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
116:                 break;
117:             case NetworkEvent::Connecting: {
118:                 if (data.empty()) {
119:                     // Cellular network - registering without carrier info yet
120:                     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
121:                 } else {
122:                     // WiFi or cellular with carrier info
123:                     std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECT_TO;
124:                     msg += data;
125:                     msg += "...";
126:                     display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
127:                 }
128:                 break;
129:             }
130:             case NetworkEvent::Connected: {
131:                 std::string msg = Lang::Strings::CONNECTED_TO;
132:                 msg += data;
133:                 display->ShowNotification(msg.c_str(), 30000);
134:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED);
135:                 break;
136:             }
137:             case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
138:                 xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED);
139:                 break;
140:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
141:                 // WiFi config mode enter is handled by WifiBoard internally
142:                 break;
143:             case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
144:                 // WiFi config mode exit is handled by WifiBoard internally
145:                 break;
146:             // Cellular modem specific events
147:             case NetworkEvent::ModemDetecting:
148:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
149:                 break;
150:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorNoSim:
151:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::PIN_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_PIN);
152:                 break;
153:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorRegDenied:
154:                 Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::REG_ERROR, "triangle_exclamation", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ERR_REG);
155:                 break;
156:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorInitFailed:
157:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
158:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::DETECTING_MODULE);
159:                 break;
160:             case NetworkEvent::ModemErrorTimeout:
161:                 display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::REGISTERING_NETWORK);
162:                 break;
163:         } });
164: 
165:     // Start network asynchronously
166:     board.StartNetwork();
167: 
168:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
169:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
170: }

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }

369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
480:             }
481:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
482:         }
483: 
484:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
485:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
486:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
487:         {
488:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
489:             break;
490:         }
491: 
492:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
493:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
494:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
495:         {
496:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
497:         }
498: 
499:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
500:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
501:         {
502:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
503:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
504:             if (err == ESP_OK)
505:             {
506:                 break;
507:             }
508:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
509:             {
510:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
511:             }
512:             else
513:             {
514:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
515:             }
516:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
517:             {
518:                 break;
519:             }
520:         }
521:     }
522: }

524: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
525: {
526:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
527:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
528:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
529: 
530:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
531: 
532:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
533:     {
534:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
535:     }
536:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
537:     {
538:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
539:     }
540:     else
541:     {
542:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
543:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
544:     }
545: 
546:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
547:                            { DismissAlert(); });
548: 
549:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
550:                               {
551:         last_error_message_ = message;
552:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
553: 
554:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
555:                                {
556:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
557:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
558:         } });
559: 
560:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
561:                                     {
562:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
563:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
564:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
565:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
566:         } });
567: 
568:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
569:                                     {
570:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
571:         Schedule([this]() {
572:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
573:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
574:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
575:         }); });
576: 
577:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
578:                               {
579:         // Parse JSON data
580:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
581:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
582:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
583:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
584:                 Schedule([this]() {
585:                     aborted_ = false;
586:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
587:                 });
588:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
589:                 Schedule([this]() {
590:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
591:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
592:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
593:                         } else {
594:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
595:                         }
596:                     }
597:                 });
598:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
599:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
600:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
601:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
602:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
603:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
604:                     });
605:                 }
606:             }
607:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
608:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
609:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
610:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
611:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
612:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
613:                 });
614:             }
615:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
619:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
620:                 });
621:             }
622:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
623:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
624:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
625:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
626:             }
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
628:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
629:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
630:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
631:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
632:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
633:                     Schedule([this]() {
634:                         Reboot();
635:                     });
636:                 } else {
637:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
638:                 }
639:             }
640:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
641:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
642:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
643:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
644:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
645:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
646:             } else {
647:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
648:             }
649: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
650:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
651:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
652:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
653:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
654:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
655:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
656:                 });
657:             } else {
658:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
659:             }
660: #endif
661:         } else {
662:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
663:         } });
664: 
665:     protocol_->Start();
666: }
667: 
668: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
669: {
670:     struct digit_sound
671:     {
672:         char digit;
673:         const std::string_view &sound;
674:     };
675:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
676:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
677:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
678:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
679:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
680:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
681:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
682:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
683:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
684:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
685: 
686:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
687:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
688: 
689:     for (const auto &digit : code)
690:     {
691:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
692:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
693:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
694:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
695:         {
696:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
697:         }
698:     }
699: }

701: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
702: {
703:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
704:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
705:     display->SetStatus(status);
706:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
707:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
708:     if (!sound.empty())
709:     {
710:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
711:     }
712: }

719:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
720:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
721:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");

853: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
854: {
855:     if (!protocol_)
856:     {
857:         return;
858:     }
859: 
860:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
861: 
862:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
863:     {
864:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
865: 
866:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
867:         {
868:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
869:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
870:             {
871:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
872:                 return;
873:             }
874:         }
875: 
876:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
877:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
878: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
879:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
880:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
881:         {
882:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
883:         }
884:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
885:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
886:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
887: #else
888:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
889:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
890:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
891:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
892: #endif
893:     }
894:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
895:     {
896:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
897:     }
898:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
899:     {
900:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
901:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
902:     }
903: }

905: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
906: {
907:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
908:     clock_ticks_ = 0;

910:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
911:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
912:     auto led = board.GetLed();

915:     switch (new_state)
916:     {
917:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
918:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
919:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
920:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
921:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
922:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);

924:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:

938:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
939:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);

941: 
942:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
943:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
944:         {
945:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
946:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
947:         }
948:         break;
949:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
950:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
951: 
952:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
953:         {
954:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);

956:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());

962:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);

1010: bool Application::UpgradeFirmware(const std::string &url, const std::string &version)
1011: {
1012:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
1013:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
1014: 
1015:     std::string upgrade_url = url;
1016:     std::string version_info = version.empty() ? "(Manual upgrade)" : version;
1017: 
1018:     // Close audio channel if it's open
1019:     if (protocol_ && protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1020:     {
1021:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel before firmware upgrade");
1022:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
1023:     }
1024:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting firmware upgrade from URL: %s", upgrade_url.c_str());
1025: 
1026:     Alert(Lang::Strings::OTA_UPGRADE, Lang::Strings::UPGRADING, "download", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
1027:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1028: 
1029:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
1030: 
1031:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;
1032:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message.c_str());
1033: 
1034:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
1035:     audio_service_.Stop();
1036:     vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
1037: 
1038:     bool upgrade_success = Ota::Upgrade(upgrade_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed)
1039:                                         { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
1040:                                                       {
1041:             char buffer[32];
1042:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
1043:             display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
1044:                                               .detach(); });
1045: 
1046:     if (!upgrade_success)
1047:     {
1048:         // Upgrade failed, restart audio service and continue running
1049:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1050:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service
1051:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER); // Restore power save level
1052:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
1053:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
1054:         return false;
1055:     }
1056:     else
1057:     {
1058:         // Upgrade success, reboot immediately
1059:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1060:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "Upgrade successful, rebooting...");
1061:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000)); // Brief pause to show message
1062:         Reboot();
1063:         return true;
1064:     }
1065: }

The `SetAecMode` function sets the acoustic echo cancellation (AEC) mode for the device, updating both the internal state and UI notification accordingly. It also ensures that any active audio channel is closed when the AEC mode changes. This maintains consistent audio processing behavior and prevents conflicts during mode transitions.

The generated code for `UpgradeFirmware` follows a logical sequence appropriate for its task — closing active channels, performing an OTA update, handling success/failure cases, and managing device state. It uses asynchronous constructs like thread detaching for UI updates during the process, which aligns semantically with real-world firmware upgrade procedures. However, it does not exactly mirror any specific function from the ground truth because they are solving different tasks (firmware upgrade vs. AEC mode setting, protocol reset). Still, within its own scope, the semantics are coherent and well-implemented.

Structurally, both the generated and ground-truth codes follow modern C++ practices including lambda usage, scheduling via event loops (`Schedule`), object-oriented access patterns (`board.GetDisplay()`), and conditional logic wrapped in safe checks (e.g., checking if `protocol_` is valid). The use of logging macros (`ESP_LOGI`, `ESP_LOGE`) is consistent with embedded systems development, though this pattern isn't visible in the ground truth. Function signature matches the query expectation but differs significantly in purpose from functions shown in the ground truth due to domain differences.

Functional Completeness:
The generated function thoroughly implements all expected steps for a robust firmware upgrade workflow: preconditions (closing audio channel), core action (OTA download & apply), feedback mechanism (progress reporting on display), error recovery (restart services on failure), and finalization (reboot on success). Compared to the ground truth snippets, it doesn't replicate their exact functionality (like changing AEC modes or resetting protocols), but that’s expected since they address unrelated features. In terms of fulfilling what was asked (implementing `UpgradeFirmware`), it exceeds expectations by incorporating rich user feedback, system resource management, and failure resilience.

172: void Application::Run()
173: {
174:     const EventBits_t ALL_EVENTS =
175:         MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE |
176:         MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO |
177:         MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED |
178:         MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE |
179:         MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK |
180:         MAIN_EVENT_ERROR |
181:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED |
182:         MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED |
183:         MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT |
184:         MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING |
185:         MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING |
186:         MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE |
187:         MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED;
188: 
189:     while (true)
190:     {
191:         auto bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, ALL_EVENTS, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
192: 
193:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ERROR)
194:         {
195:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
196:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, last_error_message_.c_str(), "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
197:         }
198: 
199:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_CONNECTED)
200:         {
201:             HandleNetworkConnectedEvent();
202:         }
203: 
204:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_NETWORK_DISCONNECTED)
205:         {
206:             HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent();
207:         }
208: 
209:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE)
210:         {
211:             HandleActivationDoneEvent();
212:         }
213: 
214:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STATE_CHANGED)
215:         {
216:             HandleStateChangedEvent();
217:         }
218: 
219:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_TOGGLE_CHAT)
220:         {
221:             HandleToggleChatEvent();
222:         }
223: 
224:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_START_LISTENING)
225:         {
226:             HandleStartListeningEvent();
227:         }
228: 
229:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_STOP_LISTENING)
230:         {
231:             HandleStopListeningEvent();
232:         }
233: 
234:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SEND_AUDIO)
235:         {
236:             while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopPacketFromSendQueue())
237:             {
238:                 if (protocol_ && !protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet)))
239:                 {
240:                     break;
241:                 }
242:             }
243:         }
244: 
245:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_DETECTED)
246:         {
247:             HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent();
248:         }
249: 
250:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_VAD_CHANGE)
251:         {
252:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateListening)
253:             {
254:                 auto led = Board::GetInstance().GetLed();
255:                 led->OnStateChanged();
256:             }
257:         }
258: 
259:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE)
260:         {
261:             std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);
262:             auto tasks = std::move(main_tasks_);
263:             lock.unlock();
264:             for (auto &task : tasks)
265:             {
266:                 task();
267:             }
268:         }
269: 
270:         if (bits & MAIN_EVENT_CLOCK_TICK)
271:         {
272:             clock_ticks_++;
273:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
274:             display->UpdateStatusBar();
275: 
276:             // Print debug info every 10 seconds
277:             if (clock_ticks_ % 10 == 0)
278:             {
279:                 SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
280:             }
281:         }
282:     }
283: }

285: void Application::HandleNetworkConnectedEvent()
286: {
287:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Network connected");
288:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
289: 
290:     if (state == kDeviceStateStarting || state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
291:     {
292:         // Network is ready, start activation
293:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
294:         if (activation_task_handle_ != nullptr)
295:         {
296:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Activation task already running");
297:             return;
298:         }
299: 
300:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
301:                     {
302:             Application* app = static_cast<Application*>(arg);
303:             app->ActivationTask();
304:             app->activation_task_handle_ = nullptr;
305:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "activation", 4096 * 2, this, 2, &activation_task_handle_);
306:     }
307: 
308:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
309:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
310:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
311: }
312: 
313: void Application::HandleNetworkDisconnectedEvent()
314: {
315:     // Close current conversation when network disconnected
316:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
317:     if (state == kDeviceStateConnecting || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Closing audio channel due to network disconnection");
320:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
321:     }
322: 
323:     // Update the status bar immediately to show the network state
324:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
325:     display->UpdateStatusBar(true);
326: }
327: 
328: void Application::HandleActivationDoneEvent()
329: {
330:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation done");
331: 
332:     SystemInfo::PrintHeapStats();
333:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
334: 
335:     has_server_time_ = ota_->HasServerTime();
336: 
337:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
338:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::VERSION) + ota_->GetCurrentVersion();
339:     display->ShowNotification(message.c_str());
340:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
341: 
342:     // Play the success sound to indicate the device is ready
343:     audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_SUCCESS);
344: 
345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }
368: 
369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
480:             }
481:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
482:         }
483: 
484:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
485:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
486:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
487:         {
488:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
489:             break;
490:         }
491: 
492:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
493:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
494:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
495:         {
496:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
497:         }
498: 
499:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
500:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
501:         {
502:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
503:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
504:             if (err == ESP_OK)
505:             {
506:                 break;
507:             }
508:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
509:             {
510:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
511:             }
512:             else
513:             {
514:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
515:             }
516:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
517:             {
518:                 break;
519:             }
520:         }
521:     }
522: }
523: 
524: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
525: {
526:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
527:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
528:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
529: 
530:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
531: 
532:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
533:     {
534:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
535:     }
536:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
537:     {
538:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
539:     }
540:     else
541:     {
542:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
543:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
544:     }
545: 
546:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
547:                            { DismissAlert(); });
548: 
549:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
550:                               {
551:         last_error_message_ = message;
552:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
553: 
554:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
555:                                {
556:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
557:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
558:         } });
559: 
560:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
561:                                     {
562:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
563:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
564:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
565:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
566:         } });
567: 
568:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
569:                                     {
570:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
571:         Schedule([this]() {
572:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
573:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
574:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
575:         }); });
576: 
577:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
578:                               {
579:         // Parse JSON data
580:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
581:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
582:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
583:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
584:                 Schedule([this]() {
585:                     aborted_ = false;
586:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
587:                 });
588:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
589:                 Schedule([this]() {
590:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
591:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
592:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
593:                         } else {
594:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
595:                         }
596:                     }
597:                 });
598:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
599:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
600:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
601:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
602:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
603:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
604:                     });
605:                 }
606:             }
607:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
608:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
609:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
610:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
611:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
612:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
613:                 });
614:             }
615:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
619:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
620:                 });
621:             }
622:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
623:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
624:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
625:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
626:             }
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
628:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
629:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
630:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
631:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
632:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
633:                     Schedule([this]() {
634:                         Reboot();
635:                     });
636:                 } else {
637:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
638:                 }
639:             }
640:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
641:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
642:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
643:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
644:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
645:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
646:             } else {
647:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
648:             }
649: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
650:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
651:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
652:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
653:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
654:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
655:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
656:                 });
657:             } else {
658:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
659:             }
660: #endif
661:         } else {
662:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
663:         } });
664: 
665:     protocol_->Start();
666: }
667: 
668: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
669: {
670:     struct digit_sound
671:     {
672:         char digit;
673:         const std::string_view &sound;
674:     };
675:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
676:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
677:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
678:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
679:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
680:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
681:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
682:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
683:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
684:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
685: 
686:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
687:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
688: 
689:     for (const auto &digit : code)
690:     {
691:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
692:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
693:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
694:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
695:         {
696:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
697:         }
698:     }
699: }

701: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)
702: {
703:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert [%s] %s: %s", emotion, status, message);
704:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
705:     display->SetStatus(status);
706:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
707:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
708:     if (!sound.empty())
709:     {
710:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
711:     }
712: }

740: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
741: {
742:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
743: 
744:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
745:     {
746:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
747:         return;
748:     }
749:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
750:     {
751:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
752:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
753:         return;
754:     }
755:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
756:     {
757:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
758:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
759:         return;
760:     }
761: 
762:     if (!protocol_)
763:     {
764:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
765:         return;
766:     }
767: 
768:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
769:     {
770:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
771:         {
772:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
773:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
774:             {
775:                 return;
776:             }
777:         }
778: 
779:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
780:     }
781:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
782:     {
783:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
784:     }
785:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
786:     {
787:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
788:     }
789: }

791: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
792: {
793:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
794: 
795:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
796:     {
797:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
798:         return;
799:     }
800:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
801:     {
802:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
803:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
804:         return;
805:     }
806: 
807:     if (!protocol_)
808:     {
809:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
810:         return;
811:     }
812: 
813:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
814:     {
815:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
816:         {
817:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
818:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
819:             {
820:                 return;
821:             }
822:         }
823: 
824:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
825:     }
826:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
827:     {
828:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
829:         SetListeningMode(kListeningModeManualStop);
830:     }
831: }

833: void Application::HandleStopListeningEvent()
834: {

837:     if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
838:     {
839:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
840:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
841:         return;
842:     }
843:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
844:     {
845:         if (protocol_)
846:         {
847:             protocol_->SendStopListening();
848:         }
849:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
850:     }

853: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
854: {
855:     if (!protocol_)
856:     {
857:         return;
858:     }
859: 
860:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
861: 
862:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
863:     {
864:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
865: 
866:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
867:         {
868:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
869:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
870:             {
871:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
872:                 return;
873:             }
874:         }
875: 
876:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
877:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
878: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
879:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
880:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
881:         {
