345:     // Release OTA object after activation is complete
346:     ota_.reset();
347:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
348:     board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
349: }
350: 
351: void Application::ActivationTask()
352: {
353:     // Create OTA object for activation process
354:     ota_ = std::make_unique<Ota>();
355: 
356:     // Check for new assets version
357:     CheckAssetsVersion();
358: 
359:     // Check for new firmware version
360:     CheckNewVersion();
361: 
362:     // Initialize the protocol
363:     InitializeProtocol();
364: 
365:     // Signal completion to main loop
366:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ACTIVATION_DONE);
367: }
368: 
369: void Application::CheckAssetsVersion()
370: {
371:     // Only allow CheckAssetsVersion to be called once
372:     if (assets_version_checked_)
373:     {
374:         return;
375:     }
376:     assets_version_checked_ = true;
377: 
378:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
379:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
380:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
381: 
382:     if (!assets.partition_valid())
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Assets partition is disabled for board %s", BOARD_NAME);
385:         return;
386:     }
387: 
388:     Settings settings("assets", true);
389:     // Check if there is a new assets need to be downloaded
390:     std::string download_url = settings.GetString("download_url");
391: 
392:     if (!download_url.empty())
393:     {
394:         settings.EraseKey("download_url");
395: 
396:         char message[256];
397:         snprintf(message, sizeof(message), Lang::Strings::FOUND_NEW_ASSETS, download_url.c_str());
398:         Alert(Lang::Strings::LOADING_ASSETS, message, "cloud_arrow_down", Lang::Sounds::OGG_UPGRADE);
399: 
400:         // Wait for the audio service to be idle for 3 seconds
401:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
402:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);
403:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
404:         display->SetChatMessage("system", Lang::Strings::PLEASE_WAIT);
405: 
406:         bool success = assets.Download(download_url, [display](int progress, size_t speed) -> void
407:                                        { std::thread([display, progress, speed]()
408:                                                      {
409:                 char buffer[32];
410:                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%d%% %uKB/s", progress, speed / 1024);
411:                 display->SetChatMessage("system", buffer); })
412:                                              .detach(); });
413: 
414:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
415:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
416: 
417:         if (!success)
418:         {
419:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::DOWNLOAD_ASSETS_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
420:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(2000));
421:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateActivating);
422:             return;
423:         }
424:     }
425: 
426:     // Apply assets
427:     assets.Apply();
428:     display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
429:     display->SetEmotion("microchip_ai");
430: }
431: 
432: void Application::CheckNewVersion()
433: {
434:     const int MAX_RETRY = 10;
435:     int retry_count = 0;
436:     int retry_delay = 10; // Initial retry delay in seconds
437: 
438:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
439:     while (true)
440:     {
441:         auto display = board.GetDisplay();
442:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CHECKING_NEW_VERSION);
443: 
444:         esp_err_t err = ota_->CheckVersion();
445:         if (err != ESP_OK)
446:         {
447:             retry_count++;
448:             if (retry_count >= MAX_RETRY)
449:             {
450:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Too many retries, exit version check");
451:                 return;
452:             }
453: 
454:             char error_message[128];
455:             snprintf(error_message, sizeof(error_message), "code=%d, url=%s", err, ota_->GetCheckVersionUrl().c_str());
456:             char buffer[256];
457:             snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), Lang::Strings::CHECK_NEW_VERSION_FAILED, retry_delay, error_message);
458:             Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, buffer, "cloud_slash", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);
459: 
460:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Check new version failed, retry in %d seconds (%d/%d)", retry_delay, retry_count, MAX_RETRY);
461:             for (int i = 0; i < retry_delay; i++)
462:             {
463:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
464:                 if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
465:                 {
466:                     break;
467:                 }
468:             }
469:             retry_delay *= 2; // Double the retry delay
470:             continue;
471:         }
472:         retry_count = 0;
473:         retry_delay = 10; // Reset retry delay
474: 
475:         if (ota_->HasNewVersion())
476:         {
477:             if (UpgradeFirmware(ota_->GetFirmwareUrl(), ota_->GetFirmwareVersion()))
478:             {
479:                 return; // This line will never be reached after reboot
480:             }
481:             // If upgrade failed, continue to normal operation
482:         }
483: 
484:         // No new version, mark the current version as valid
485:         ota_->MarkCurrentVersionValid();
486:         if (!ota_->HasActivationCode() && !ota_->HasActivationChallenge())
487:         {
488:             // Exit the loop if done checking new version
489:             break;
490:         }
491: 
492:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION);
493:         // Activation code is shown to the user and waiting for the user to input
494:         if (ota_->HasActivationCode())
495:         {
496:             ShowActivationCode(ota_->GetActivationCode(), ota_->GetActivationMessage());
497:         }
498: 
499:         // This will block the loop until the activation is done or timeout
500:         for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i)
501:         {
502:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activating... %d/%d", i + 1, 10);
503:             esp_err_t err = ota_->Activate();
504:             if (err == ESP_OK)
505:             {
506:                 break;
507:             }
508:             else if (err == ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT)
509:             {
510:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(3000));
511:             }
512:             else
513:             {
514:                 vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
515:             }
516:             if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
517:             {
518:                 break;
519:             }
520:         }
521:     }
522: }
523: 
524: void Application::InitializeProtocol()
525: {
526:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
527:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
528:     auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec();
529: 
530:     display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LOADING_PROTOCOL);
531: 
532:     if (ota_->HasMqttConfig())
533:     {
534:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
535:     }
536:     else if (ota_->HasWebsocketConfig())
537:     {
538:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<WebsocketProtocol>();
539:     }
540:     else
541:     {
542:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No protocol specified in the OTA config, using MQTT");
543:         protocol_ = std::make_unique<MqttProtocol>();
544:     }
545: 
546:     protocol_->OnConnected([this]()
547:                            { DismissAlert(); });
548: 
549:     protocol_->OnNetworkError([this](const std::string &message)
550:                               {
551:         last_error_message_ = message;
552:         xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_ERROR); });
553: 
554:     protocol_->OnIncomingAudio([this](std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
555:                                {
556:         if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
557:             audio_service_.PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet));
558:         } });
559: 
560:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelOpened([this, codec, &board]()
561:                                     {
562:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::PERFORMANCE);
563:         if (protocol_->server_sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate()) {
564:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Server sample rate %d does not match device output sample rate %d, resampling may cause distortion",
565:                 protocol_->server_sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
566:         } });
567: 
568:     protocol_->OnAudioChannelClosed([this, &board]()
569:                                     {
570:         board.SetPowerSaveLevel(PowerSaveLevel::LOW_POWER);
571:         Schedule([this]() {
572:             auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
573:             display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
574:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
575:         }); });
576: 
577:     protocol_->OnIncomingJson([this, display](const cJSON *root)
578:                               {
579:         // Parse JSON data
580:         auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
581:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "tts") == 0) {
582:             auto state = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "state");
583:             if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "start") == 0) {
584:                 Schedule([this]() {
585:                     aborted_ = false;
586:                     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateSpeaking);
587:                 });
588:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "stop") == 0) {
589:                 Schedule([this]() {
590:                     if (GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateSpeaking) {
591:                         if (listening_mode_ == kListeningModeManualStop) {
592:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
593:                         } else {
594:                             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateListening);
595:                         }
596:                     }
597:                 });
598:             } else if (strcmp(state->valuestring, "sentence_start") == 0) {
599:                 auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
600:                 if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
601:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "<< %s", text->valuestring);
602:                     Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
603:                         display->SetChatMessage("assistant", message.c_str());
604:                     });
605:                 }
606:             }
607:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "stt") == 0) {
608:             auto text = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "text");
609:             if (cJSON_IsString(text)) {
610:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, ">> %s", text->valuestring);
611:                 Schedule([this, display, message = std::string(text->valuestring)]() {
612:                     display->SetChatMessage("user", message.c_str());
613:                 });
614:             }
615:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "llm") == 0) {
616:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
617:             if (cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
618:                 Schedule([this, display, emotion_str = std::string(emotion->valuestring)]() {
619:                     display->SetEmotion(emotion_str.c_str());
620:                 });
621:             }
622:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "mcp") == 0) {
623:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
624:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
625:                 McpServer::GetInstance().ParseMessage(payload);
626:             }
627:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "system") == 0) {
628:             auto command = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "command");
629:             if (cJSON_IsString(command)) {
630:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "System command: %s", command->valuestring);
631:                 if (strcmp(command->valuestring, "reboot") == 0) {
632:                     // Do a reboot if user requests a OTA update
633:                     Schedule([this]() {
634:                         Reboot();
635:                     });
636:                 } else {
637:                     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown system command: %s", command->valuestring);
638:                 }
639:             }
640:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "alert") == 0) {
641:             auto status = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "status");
642:             auto message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "message");
643:             auto emotion = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "emotion");
644:             if (cJSON_IsString(status) && cJSON_IsString(message) && cJSON_IsString(emotion)) {
645:                 Alert(status->valuestring, message->valuestring, emotion->valuestring, Lang::Sounds::OGG_VIBRATION);
646:             } else {
647:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Alert command requires status, message and emotion");
648:             }
649: #if CONFIG_RECEIVE_CUSTOM_MESSAGE
650:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "custom") == 0) {
651:             auto payload = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "payload");
652:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received custom message: %s", cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root));
653:             if (cJSON_IsObject(payload)) {
654:                 Schedule([this, display, payload_str = std::string(cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload))]() {
655:                     display->SetChatMessage("system", payload_str.c_str());
656:                 });
657:             } else {
658:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid custom message format: missing payload");
659:             }
660: #endif
661:         } else {
662:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Unknown message type: %s", type->valuestring);
663:         } });
664: 
665:     protocol_->Start();
666: }
667: 
668: void Application::ShowActivationCode(const std::string &code, const std::string &message)
669: {
670:     struct digit_sound
671:     {
672:         char digit;
673:         const std::string_view &sound;
674:     };
675:     static const std::array<digit_sound, 10> digit_sounds{{digit_sound{'0', Lang::Sounds::OGG_0},
676:                                                            digit_sound{'1', Lang::Sounds::OGG_1},
677:                                                            digit_sound{'2', Lang::Sounds::OGG_2},
678:                                                            digit_sound{'3', Lang::Sounds::OGG_3},
679:                                                            digit_sound{'4', Lang::Sounds::OGG_4},
680:                                                            digit_sound{'5', Lang::Sounds::OGG_5},
681:                                                            digit_sound{'6', Lang::Sounds::OGG_6},
682:                                                            digit_sound{'7', Lang::Sounds::OGG_7},
683:                                                            digit_sound{'8', Lang::Sounds::OGG_8},
684:                                                            digit_sound{'9', Lang::Sounds::OGG_9}}};
685: 
686:     // This sentence uses 9KB of SRAM, so we need to wait for it to finish
687:     Alert(Lang::Strings::ACTIVATION, message.c_str(), "link", Lang::Sounds::OGG_ACTIVATION);
688: 
689:     for (const auto &digit : code)
690:     {
691:         auto it = std::find_if(digit_sounds.begin(), digit_sounds.end(),
692:                                [digit](const digit_sound &ds)
693:                                { return ds.digit == digit; });
694:         if (it != digit_sounds.end())
695:         {
696:             audio_service_.PlaySound(it->sound);
697:         }
698:     }
699: }

701: void Application::Alert(const char *status, const char *message, const char *emotion, const std::string_view &sound)

704:     auto display = Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay();
705:     display->SetStatus(status);
706:     display->SetEmotion(emotion);
707:     display->SetChatMessage("system", message);
708:     if (!sound.empty())
709:     {
710:         audio_service_.PlaySound(sound);
711:     }

740: void Application::HandleToggleChatEvent()
741: {
742:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
743: 
744:     if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
745:     {
746:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
747:         return;
748:     }
749:     else if (state == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring)
750:     {
751:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(true);
752:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateAudioTesting);
753:         return;
754:     }
755:     else if (state == kDeviceStateAudioTesting)
756:     {
757:         audio_service_.EnableAudioTesting(false);
758:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring);
759:         return;
760:     }
761: 
762:     if (!protocol_)
763:     {
764:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Protocol not initialized");
765:         return;
766:     }
767: 
768:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
769:     {
770:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
771:         {
772:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
773:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
774:             {
775:                 return;
776:             }
777:         }
778: 
779:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
780:     }
781:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
782:     {
783:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone);
784:     }
785:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)
786:     {
787:         protocol_->CloseAudioChannel();
788:     }
789: }

791: void Application::HandleStartListeningEvent()
792: {

801:     {

853: void Application::HandleWakeWordDetectedEvent()
854: {
855:     if (!protocol_)
856:     {
857:         return;
858:     }
859: 
860:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
861: 
862:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
863:     {
864:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
865: 
866:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
867:         {
868:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
869:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
870:             {
871:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
872:                 return;
873:             }
874:         }
875: 
876:         auto wake_word = audio_service_.GetLastWakeWord();
877:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
878: #if CONFIG_SEND_WAKE_WORD_DATA
879:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
880:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
881:         {
882:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
883:         }
884:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
885:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
886:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
887: #else
888:         // Set flag to play popup sound after state changes to listening
889:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
890:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
891:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
892: #endif
893:     }
894:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
895:     {
896:         AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonWakeWordDetected);
897:     }
898:     else if (state == kDeviceStateActivating)
899:     {
900:         // Restart the activation check if the wake word is detected during activation
901:         SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateIdle);
902:     }
903: }
904: 
905: void Application::HandleStateChangedEvent()
906: {
907:     DeviceState new_state = state_machine_.GetState();
908:     clock_ticks_ = 0;
909: 
910:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
911:     auto display = board.GetDisplay();
912:     auto led = board.GetLed();
913:     led->OnStateChanged();
914: 
915:     switch (new_state)
916:     {
917:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
918:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
919:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::STANDBY);
920:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
921:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
922:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
923:         break;
924:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
925:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::CONNECTING);
926:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
927:         display->SetChatMessage("system", "");
928:         break;
929:     case kDeviceStateListening:
930:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::LISTENING);
931:         display->SetEmotion("neutral");
932: 
933:         // Make sure the audio processor is running
934:         if (!audio_service_.IsAudioProcessorRunning())
935:         {
936:             // Send the start listening command
937:             protocol_->SendStartListening(listening_mode_);
938:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(true);
939:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
940:         }
941: 
942:         // Play popup sound after ResetDecoder (in EnableVoiceProcessing) has been called
943:         if (play_popup_on_listening_)
944:         {
945:             play_popup_on_listening_ = false;
946:             audio_service_.PlaySound(Lang::Sounds::OGG_POPUP);
947:         }
948:         break;
949:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
950:         display->SetStatus(Lang::Strings::SPEAKING);
951: 
952:         if (listening_mode_ != kListeningModeRealtime)
953:         {
954:             audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
955:             // Only AFE wake word can be detected in speaking mode
956:             audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(audio_service_.IsAfeWakeWord());
957:         }
958:         audio_service_.ResetDecoder();
959:         break;
960:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
961:         audio_service_.EnableVoiceProcessing(false);
962:         audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(false);
963:         break;
964:     default:
965:         // Do nothing
966:         break;
967:     }
968: }

972:     {

975:     }
976:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_, MAIN_EVENT_SCHEDULE);
977: }

995: void Application::Reboot()
996: {
997:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Rebooting...");
998:     // Disconnect the audio channel

1017:

1029:     SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateUpgrading);

1031:     std::string message = std::string(Lang::Strings::NEW_VERSION) + version_info;

1046:     if (!upgrade_success)

1049:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Firmware upgrade failed, restarting audio service and continuing operation...");
1050:         audio_service_.Start();                             // Restart audio service

1052:         Alert(Lang::Strings::ERROR, Lang::Strings::UPGRADE_FAILED, "circle_xmark", Lang::Sounds::OGG_EXCLAMATION);

1067: void Application::WakeWordInvoke(const std::string &wake_word)
1068: {
1069:     if (!protocol_)
1070:     {
1071:         return;
1072:     }
1073: 
1074:     auto state = GetDeviceState();
1075: 
1076:     if (state == kDeviceStateIdle)
1077:     {
1078:         audio_service_.EncodeWakeWord();
1079: 
1080:         if (!protocol_->IsAudioChannelOpened())
1081:         {
1082:             SetDeviceState(kDeviceStateConnecting);
1083:             if (!protocol_->OpenAudioChannel())
1084:             {
1085:                 audio_service_.EnableWakeWordDetection(true);
1086:                 return;
1087:             }
1088:         }
1089: 
1090:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Wake word detected: %s", wake_word.c_str());
1091: #if CONFIG_USE_AFE_WAKE_WORD || CONFIG_USE_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
1092:         // Encode and send the wake word data to the server
1093:         while (auto packet = audio_service_.PopWakeWordPacket())
1094:         {
1095:             protocol_->SendAudio(std::move(packet));
1096:         }
1097:         // Set the chat state to wake word detected
1098:         protocol_->SendWakeWordDetected(wake_word);
1099:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1100: #else

1102:         // (PlaySound here would be cleared by ResetDecoder in EnableVoiceProcessing)
1103:         play_popup_on_listening_ = true;
1104:         SetListeningMode(aec_mode_ == kAecOff ? kListeningModeAutoStop : kListeningModeRealtime);
1105: #endif
1106:     }
1107:     else if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking)
1108:     {
1109:         Schedule([this]()
1110:                  { AbortSpeaking(kAbortReasonNone); });
1111:     }
1112:     else if (state == kDeviceStateListening)

The function initializes and validates an assets partition in flash memory, ensuring it exists, has sufficient space, and passes checksum verification. It maps the partition into memory and builds an index of assets with their sizes and offsets for efficient access. Returns true if the partition is successfully initialized and validated, false otherwise.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to initialize a partition and validate its contents, which aligns with the purpose of the ground truth.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  - Uses a magic number check ("ASET") not present in the ground truth.
  - Implements a different checksum mechanism (header-based vs. calculated over data).
  - Assumes a different data layout (with table offset/size fields) compared to the ground truth's direct file count and length approach.
  - Handles asset parsing differently with additional validation steps not in the original.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a general flow of finding the partition, mapping it, validating content, and building an asset index.
- Major structural differences include:
  - Different partition search parameters (DATA/SPIFFS vs. ANY/ANY).
  - Different error handling approaches (some cleanup in generated code that isn't in ground truth).
  - Different logging strategies and levels.
  - Different assumptions about memory layout and data structures.
  - Function call differences (e.g., no flash free page checking in generated code).

Functional Completeness:
- Missing key functionalities from ground truth:
  - No check for available flash memory pages before mapping.
  - No timing measurement for checksum calculation.
  - No proper handling of stored file counts and lengths as done in ground truth.
  - Lacks some validation checks like stored_len boundary verification.
- Additional functionality in generated code not present in ground truth:
  - Magic number verification.
  - Width/height fields in asset info.
  - More aggressive bounds checking for individual assets.
- Core functionality of partition initialization and asset indexing is present but implemented differently.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (45/100):
- The generated code attempts to implement asset loading functionality but uses a completely different approach than the ground truth
- Major semantic differences include:
  * Uses partition validation checks not present in ground truth
  * Parses JSON differently (using string conversion vs direct parsing with length)
  * Handles version checking differently (string vs number comparison)
  * Completely different theme/application logic structure
  * Missing critical error handling patterns from the original
- Some basic concepts are preserved like loading index.json and applying assets, but implementation diverges significantly

Structure Similarity (30/100):
- Function signature matches but internal structure is vastly different
- Different JSON parsing approach (converts to string vs direct binary parsing)
- No equivalent conditional compilation blocks (#ifdef HAVE_LVGL)
- Different error logging patterns and message formats
- Missing key API calls like esp_srmodel_load/deinit, LvglThemeManager usage
- Different control flow with early returns vs comprehensive error handling
- No equivalent of dynamic casting for display types or theme management

Functional Completeness (40/100):
- Missing critical functionalities:
  * Speech recognition model loading with esp_srmodel API
  * LVGL theme management and font handling
  * Emoji collection processing with detailed item parsing
  * Skin/color configuration handling
  * Display theme refreshing
  * Hide subtitle configuration
  * Platform-specific implementations (LVGL vs Emote display styles)
- Partially covers basic asset loading concepts but misses domain-specific functionality
- Lacks proper integration with Application, Board, and Display systems
- No equivalent resource cleanup or initialization patterns
- Missing specific file type handling (srmodels.bin, fonts.bin, etc.)

Overall, while the generated code shows understanding of general asset loading concepts, it fails to capture the specific domain requirements, platform integrations, and detailed functionality present in the ground truth implementation.

61:         return false;
62:     }
63: 
64:     int free_pages = spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages(SPI_FLASH_MMAP_DATA);
65:     uint32_t storage_size = free_pages * 64 * 1024;
66:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The storage free size is %ld KB", storage_size / 1024);
67:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The partition size is %ld KB", partition_->size / 1024);
68:     if (storage_size < partition_->size)
69:     {
70:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The free size %ld KB is less than assets partition required %ld KB", storage_size / 1024, partition_->size / 1024);
71:         return false;
72:     }
73: 
74:     esp_err_t err = esp_partition_mmap(partition_, 0, partition_->size, ESP_PARTITION_MMAP_DATA, (const void **)&mmap_root_, &mmap_handle_);
75:     if (err != ESP_OK)
76:     {
77:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to mmap assets partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
78:         return false;
79:     }
80: 
81:     partition_valid_ = true;
82: 
83:     uint32_t stored_files = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 0);
84:     uint32_t stored_chksum = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 4);
85:     uint32_t stored_len = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 8);
86: 
87:     if (stored_len > partition_->size - 12)
88:     {
89:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "The stored_len (0x%lx) is greater than the partition size (0x%lx) - 12", stored_len, partition_->size);
90:         return false;
91:     }
92: 
93:     auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
94:     uint32_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(mmap_root_ + 12, stored_len);
95:     auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
96:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The checksum calculation time is %d ms", int((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
97: 
98:     if (calculated_checksum != stored_chksum)
99:     {
100:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The calculated checksum (0x%lx) does not match the stored checksum (0x%lx)", calculated_checksum, stored_chksum);
101:         return false;
102:     }
103: 
104:     checksum_valid_ = true;
105: 
106:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < stored_files; i++)
107:     {
108:         auto item = (const mmap_assets_table *)(mmap_root_ + 12 + i * sizeof(mmap_assets_table));
109:         auto asset = Asset{
110:             .size = static_cast<size_t>(item->asset_size),
111:             .offset = static_cast<size_t>(12 + sizeof(mmap_assets_table) * stored_files + item->asset_offset)};
112:         assets_[item->asset_name] = asset;
113:     }
114:     return checksum_valid_;
115: }

469: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
470: {
471:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
472:     if (asset == assets_.end())
473:     {
474:         return false;
475:     }
476:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
477:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
480:         return false;
481:     }
482: 
483:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
484:     size = asset->second.size;
485:     return true;
486: }

The `Assets::Download` function downloads a new version of assets from a given URL and stores it in the device's flash memory partition. It handles HTTP communication, validates the content size against the partition size, and manages flash memory by erasing and writing data in sectors. The function also provides progress updates during the download process.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely mirrors the intended behavior of downloading a resource from a URL and writing it to an ESP32 partition with progress updates. Both versions handle HTTP communication, validate content size against partition size, erase flash before writing, and report progress via callbacks. However, there are differences in implementation details such as how partitions are accessed (the ground truth uses member variables like `partition_`, while the generated code searches for the partition by name) and error handling granularity.

Structure Similarity:
While both functions aim to accomplish the same task, their structural approaches diverge significantly. The ground truth integrates tightly with class members (`mmap_handle_`, `assets_`, `Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork()`), uses custom HTTP abstractions (`network->CreateHttp`), performs sector-by-sector erasure during writes, and includes detailed logging around timing and performance metrics. In contrast, the generated code uses direct ESP-IDF APIs (`esp_http_client_*`) and simplifies the process by erasing the entire partition upfront. These architectural differences lower the similarity score despite functional overlap.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionalities—such as downloading over HTTP, validating sizes, erasing flash, writing data, and invoking progress callbacks—are covered in the generated code. It also attempts to compute transfer speed based on elapsed time. However, some advanced features present in the ground truth are missing or simplified: per-sector erase logic during streaming write, precise per-second speed calculation, use of existing network stack abstraction, unmapping old memory regions prior to update, and checksum validation flags. Thus, while mostly complete, it omits several nuanced operational aspects.

results.query: complete the function IsValidTransition(DeviceState, DeviceState) const()
results.description: The `IsValidTransition` function determines whether a state change is allowed in the device's state machine by checking if the transition from the current state to the target state is valid according to predefined rules. It ensures that only permitted state transitions occur, maintaining system stability and preventing invalid states. The function returns `true` for valid transitions and `false` otherwise, including all self-transitions and transitions that follow the defined state flow.

45: bool DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo(DeviceState new_state)
46: {
47:     DeviceState old_state = current_state_.load();

49:     // No-op if already in the target state

64:     current_state_.store(new_state);
65:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "State: %s -> %s",

The `IsValidTransition` function determines whether a state change is allowed in the device's state machine by checking if the transition from the current state to the target state is valid according to predefined rules. It ensures that only permitted state transitions occur, maintaining system stability and preventing invalid states. The function returns `true` for valid transitions and `false` otherwise, including all self-transitions and transitions that follow the defined state flow.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (40/100):
The generated code fails to capture most of the intended state transitions defined in the ground truth. For example:
- It incorrectly allows transitions from `kDeviceStateStarting` to `kDeviceStateFatalError`, which is not part of the original logic.
- Many specific and meaningful transitions like from `kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring` to `kDeviceStateActivating` or `kDeviceStateAudioTesting` are missing.
- Several intermediate states such as `kDeviceStateAudioTesting` do not have correct onward transitions per the specification.
While it handles some basic cases like self-transitions and terminal behavior for `kDeviceStateFatalError`, the overall semantics deviate significantly from the expected device lifecycle.

Structure Similarity (85/100):
Structurally, both implementations follow a similar approach using a `switch` statement over the `from` state and returning boolean values based on valid transitions. Both also handle default cases and include comments where appropriate. The function signature matches exactly, including the method name, parameters, return type, and const qualifier. 45: bool DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo(DeviceState new_state)
46: {
47:     DeviceState old_state = current_state_.load();
48: 
49:     // No-op if already in the target state
50:     if (old_state == new_state)
51:     {
52:         return true;
53:     }
54: 
55:     // Validate transition
56:     if (!IsValidTransition(old_state, new_state))
57:     {
58:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid state transition: %s -> %s",
59:                  GetStateName(old_state), GetStateName(new_state));
60:         return false;
61:     }
62: 
63:     // Perform transition
64:     current_state_.store(new_state);
65:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "State: %s -> %s",
66:              GetStateName(old_state), GetStateName(new_state));
67: 
68:     // Notify callback
69:     NotifyStateChange(old_state, new_state);
70:     return true;
71: }

100:         }
101:     }
102: 
103:     for (const auto &cb : callbacks_copy)

107: }

291: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const cJSON *json)
292: {
293:     // Check JSONRPC version
294:     auto version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "jsonrpc");
295:     if (version == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(version) || strcmp(version->valuestring, "2.0") != 0)
296:     {
297:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid JSONRPC version: %s", version ? version->valuestring : "null");
298:         return;
299:     }
300: 
301:     // Check method
302:     auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
303:     if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
304:     {
305:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
306:         return;
307:     }
308: 
309:     auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
310:     if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
311:     {
312:         return;
313:     }
314: 
315:     // Check params
316:     auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
317:     if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
318:     {
319:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
320:         return;
321:     }
322: 
323:     auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
324:     if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
325:     {
326:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
327:         return;
328:     }
329:     auto id_int = id->valueint;
330: 
331:     if (method_str == "initialize")
332:     {
333:         if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
334:         {
335:             auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
336:             if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
337:             {
338:                 ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
339:             }
340:         }
341:         auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
342:         std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
343:         message += app_desc->version;
344:         message += "\"}}";
345:         ReplyResult(id_int, message);
346:     }
347:     else if (method_str == "tools/list")
348:     {
349:         std::string cursor_str = "";
350:         bool list_user_only_tools = false;
351:         if (params != nullptr)
352:         {
353:             auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
354:             if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
355:             {
356:                 cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
357:             }
358:             auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
359:             if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
360:             {
361:                 list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
362:             }
363:         }
364:         GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
365:     }
366:     else if (method_str == "tools/call")
367:     {
368:         if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
369:         {
370:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
371:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
372:             return;
373:         }
374:         auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
375:         if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
376:         {
377:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
378:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
379:             return;
380:         }
381:         auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
382:         if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
383:         {
384:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
385:             ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
386:             return;
387:         }
388:         DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
389:     }
390:     else
391:     {
392:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
393:         ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
394:     }
395: }

416: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
417: {
418:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
419:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
420: 
421:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
422:     auto it = tools_.begin();
423:     std::string next_cursor = "";
424: 
425:     while (it != tools_.end())
426:     {
427:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
428:         if (!found_cursor)
429:         {
430:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
431:             {
432:                 found_cursor = true;
433:             }
434:             else
435:             {
436:                 ++it;
437:                 continue;
438:             }
439:         }
440: 
441:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
442:         {
443:             ++it;
444:             continue;
445:         }
446: 
447:         // 添加tool前检查大小
448:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
449:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
450:         {
451:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
452:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
453:             break;
454:         }
455: 
456:         json += tool_json;
457:         ++it;
458:     }
459: 
460:     if (json.back() == ',')
461:     {
462:         json.pop_back();
463:     }
464: 
465:     if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
466:     {
467:         // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
468:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
469:         ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
470:         return;
471:     }
472: 
473:     if (next_cursor.empty())
474:     {
475:         json += "]}";
476:     }
477:     else
478:     {
479:         json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
480:     }
481: 
482:     ReplyResult(id, json);
483: }

485: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)

The `McpServer::AddCommonTools` function registers a set of predefined tools for device control and status retrieval, including functions to get device status, set audio volume, adjust screen brightness, and change the display theme. These tools are added at the beginning of the tool list to optimize response times by leveraging prompt caching. Custom tools are explicitly excluded and must be added elsewhere during board initialization.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement similar functionality but lacks several important semantic details from the ground truth:
  * Tool names differ significantly (e.g., "get_device_status" vs "self.get_device_status")
  * Missing critical comments about performance optimization and prompt caching
  * No backup/restore mechanism for the original tools list
  * Different approach to conditional tool addition (uses direct null checks instead of preprocessor directives appropriately)
  * Missing the important note about custom tools placement

Structure Similarity:
- Major structural differences:
  * Function signature is missing the class scope resolution (`McpServer::`)
  * Missing preprocessor directives handling (#ifdef HAVE_LVGL)
  * No proper tool ordering strategy (adding common tools at beginning)
  * Different parameter structures in Property definitions
  * Missing lambda capture semantics ([&board] vs direct access)
  * Absence of priority management for camera operations
  * No error handling mechanisms like in the camera capture section

Functional Completeness:
- Partially covers core functions but misses key aspects:
  * Device status retrieval implemented but with simplified description
  * Audio volume control exists but uses different APIs (GetAudioOutput vs GetAudioCodec)
  * Screen brightness adjustment implemented but with different method calls
  * Theme switching present but hardcoded enum values instead of dynamic lookup
  * Missing camera functionality entirely
  * No restoration of original tools list
  * Lacks proper validation and error checking found in ground truth
  * Missing integration with settings persistence in some cases
  * Does not respect the separation between common tools and custom tools as specified in comments

244: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
245: {
246:             const int max_payload_size = 8000;
247:             std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
248: 
249:             bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
250:             auto it = tools_.begin();
251:             std::string next_cursor = "";
252: 
253:             while (it != tools_.end())
254:             {
255:                 // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
256:                 if (!found_cursor)
257:                 {
258:                     if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
259:                     {
260:                         found_cursor = true;
261:                     }
262:                     else
263:                     {
264:                         ++it;
265:                         continue;
266:                     }
267:                 }
268: 
269:                 if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
270:                 {
271:                     ++it;
272:                     continue;
273:                 }
274: 
275:                 // 添加tool前检查大小
276:                 std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
277:                 if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
278:                 {
279:                     // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
280:                     next_cursor = (*it)->name();
281:                     break;
282:                 }
283: 
284:                 json += tool_json;
285:                 ++it;
286:             }
287: 
288:             if (json.back() == ',')
289:             {
290:                 json.pop_back();
291:             }
292: 
293:             if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
294:             {
295:                 // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
296:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
297:                 ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
298:                 return;
299:             }
300: 
301:             if (next_cursor.empty())
302:             {
303:                 json += "]}";
304:             }
305:             else
306:             {
307:                 json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
308:             }
309: 
310:             ReplyResult(id, json);
311: }

The `McpServer::AddUserOnlyTools` function registers a set of user-accessible tools for system management, including retrieving system information, rebooting the device, upgrading firmware from a given URL, and capturing/displaying screen snapshots. These tools are exposed through the MCP server interface and can be invoked remotely by users with appropriate permissions. The function ensures that critical operations like reboot and firmware upgrade are executed safely within the application's event loop.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely preserves the intended behavior of the original functions such as `self.get_system_info`, `self.reboot`, and `self.upgrade_firmware`. However, there are notable deviations in the implementation of `self.screen.snapshot` where the manual construction of multipart body differs from the ground truth's stream-based writing approach. While both aim to upload a JPEG screenshot, differences in how HTTP headers and payload are handled may lead to divergent runtime behaviors or compatibility issues depending on server expectations.

Structure Similarity:
The overall structure including lambda captures, use of `PropertyList`, tool registration via `AddUserOnlyTool`, conditional compilation (`#ifdef HAVE_LVGL`, `#if CONFIG_LV_USE_SNAPSHOT`) is preserved well. Function signatures match exactly with consistent usage of `[this]` capture for member access. Minor stylistic variations exist (e.g., spacing, indentation), but these do not affect structural integrity.

Functional Completeness:
Several features present in the ground truth are missing in the generated version:
- The `self.screen.preview_image` functionality under `CONFIG_LV_USE_SNAPSHOT` is entirely absent.
- Asset management functionality related to `self.assets.set_download_url` has been omitted.
These omissions significantly reduce functional coverage despite accurate reproduction of other parts.

It supports methods like "initialize", "tools/list", and "tools/call", ensuring proper parameter validation and error handling. The function also manages capability parsing and tool listing or execution based on the request.

221: 
222:                             auto http = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork()->CreateHttp(3);
223:                             http->SetHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);

228:                             {

241:                             {

249:                             if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)

251:                                 throw std::runtime_error("Unexpected status code: " + std::to_string(http->GetStatusCode()));
252:                             }
253:                             std::string result = http->ReadAll();
254:                             http->Close();

256:                             return true;

274:                             }
275: 
276:                             size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
277:                             char *data = (char *)heap_caps_malloc(content_length, MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
278:                             if (data == nullptr)
279:                             {
280:                                 throw std::runtime_error("Failed to allocate memory for image: " + url);
281:                             }
282:                             size_t total_read = 0;
283:                             while (total_read < content_length)
284:                             {
285:                                 int ret = http->Read(data + total_read, content_length - total_read);
286:                                 if (ret < 0)
287:                                 {
288:                                     heap_caps_free(data);
289:                                     throw std::runtime_error("Failed to download image: " + url);
290:                                 }
291:                                 if (ret == 0)
292:                                 {
293:                                     break;
294:                                 }
295:                                 total_read += ret;
296:                             }
297:                             http->Close();
298: 
299:                             auto image = std::make_unique<LvglAllocatedImage>(data, content_length);
300:                             display->SetPreviewImage(std::move(image));
301:                             return true;
302:                         });
303: #endif // CONFIG_LV_USE_SNAPSHOT

389: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
390: {
391:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
392:     payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
393:     payload += result;
394:     payload += "}";
395:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
396: }

399: {

401:     payload += std::to_string(id);
402:     payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
403:     payload += message;
404:     payload += "\"}}";
405:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
406: }

408: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
409: {
410:     const int max_payload_size = 8000;
411:     std::string json = "{\"tools\":[";
412: 
413:     bool found_cursor = cursor.empty();
414:     auto it = tools_.begin();
415:     std::string next_cursor = "";
416: 
417:     while (it != tools_.end())
418:     {
419:         // 如果我们还没有找到起始位置，继续搜索
420:         if (!found_cursor)
421:         {
422:             if ((*it)->name() == cursor)
423:             {
424:                 found_cursor = true;
425:             }
426:             else
427:             {
428:                 ++it;
429:                 continue;
430:             }
431:         }
432: 
433:         if (!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only())
434:         {
435:             ++it;
436:             continue;
437:         }
438: 
439:         // 添加tool前检查大小
440:         std::string tool_json = (*it)->to_json() + ",";
441:         if (json.length() + tool_json.length() + 30 > max_payload_size)
442:         {
443:             // 如果添加这个tool会超出大小限制，设置next_cursor并退出循环
444:             next_cursor = (*it)->name();
445:             break;
446:         }
447: 
448:         json += tool_json;
449:         ++it;
450:     }
451: 
452:     if (json.back() == ',')
453:     {
454:         json.pop_back();
455:     }
456: 
457:     if (json.back() == '[' && !tools_.empty())
458:     {
459:         // 如果没有添加任何tool，返回错误
460:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/list: Failed to add tool %s because of payload size limit", next_cursor.c_str());
461:         ReplyError(id, "Failed to add tool " + next_cursor + " because of payload size limit");
462:         return;
463:     }
464: 
465:     if (next_cursor.empty())
466:     {
467:         json += "]}";
468:     }
469:     else
470:     {
471:         json += "],\"nextCursor\":\"" + next_cursor + "\"}";
472:     }
473: 
474:     ReplyResult(id, json);
475: }

477: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)
478: {
479:     auto tool_iter = std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(),
480:                                   [&tool_name](const McpTool *tool)
481:                                   {
482:                                       return tool->name() == tool_name;
483:                                   });
484: 
485:     if (tool_iter == tools_.end())
486:     {
487:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Unknown tool: %s", tool_name.c_str());
488:         ReplyError(id, "Unknown tool: " + tool_name);
489:         return;
490:     }
491: 
492:     PropertyList arguments = (*tool_iter)->properties();
493:     try
494:     {
495:         for (auto &argument : arguments)
496:         {
497:             bool found = false;
498:             if (cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
499:             {
500:                 auto value = cJSON_GetObjectItem(tool_arguments, argument.name().c_str());
501:                 if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeBoolean && cJSON_IsBool(value))
502:                 {
503:                     argument.set_value<bool>(value->valueint == 1);
504:                     found = true;
505:                 }
506:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeInteger && cJSON_IsNumber(value))
507:                 {
508:                     argument.set_value<int>(value->valueint);
509:                     found = true;
510:                 }
511:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
512:                 {
513:                     argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
514:                     found = true;
515:                 }
516:             }
517: 
518:             if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
519:             {
520:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
521:                 ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
522:                 return;
523:             }
524:         }
525:     }
526:     catch (const std::exception &e)
527:     {
528:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
529:         ReplyError(id, e.what());
530:         return;
531:     }
532: 
533:     // Use main thread to call the tool
534:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
535:     app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
536:                  {
537:         try {
538:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
539:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
540:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
541:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
542:         } });
543: }

The `McpServer::ParseMessage` function processes incoming JSON-RPC 2.0 messages, validates their structure, and routes them to appropriate handlers based on the method specified. It supports methods like "initialize", "tools/list", and "tools/call", ensuring proper parameter validation and error handling. The function also manages capability parsing and tool listing or execution based on the request.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly parses the JSON-RPC message and handles core fields like `jsonrpc`, `method`, `id`, and `params`.
- It supports the same set of methods (`initialize`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`) with mostly correct logic.
- However, there are some semantic discrepancies:
  - In `tools/list`, the parameter name is `userOnly` instead of `withUserTools`. This is a functional mismatch that changes behavior.
  - The handling of missing or invalid IDs differs slightly; the ground truth checks for numeric type while generated assumes presence suffices.
  - The generated code does not filter out notification messages (methods starting with "notifications"), which could lead to unintended processing.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes follow a similar high-level structure: validate version → extract method → process based on method.
- Function call patterns and use of cJSON APIs are largely consistent.
- Differences include:
  - Order of validation steps varies (e.g., ID checking happens later in generated).
  - Use of intermediate variables differs (e.g., `method_str` vs `method`).
  - Handling of responses shows structural differences in how JSON objects are built and returned (manual string concatenation vs cJSON object construction).

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality such as parsing capabilities during initialization and routing method calls is preserved.
- Missing features:
  - No explicit filtering of notifications.
  - Incorrect field name used in `tools/list` (`userOnly` vs `withUserTools`), affecting compatibility.
  - Slight difference in error reporting details (less verbose logging in generated code).
  - Does not check if `id` is a number specifically, only checks existence.

Overall, the generated code captures most intended behaviors but misses certain protocol nuances and has minor behavioral divergences that affect correctness and completeness.

results.query: complete the function GetToolsList(int, const std::string &, bool)()
results.description: The `GetToolsList` function retrieves a paginated list of available tools from the MCP server, filtering them based on user permissions and payload size limits. It supports cursor-based pagination to handle large tool lists efficiently and returns the result as a JSON-formatted response. If a tool cannot be added due to size constraints, an error is returned instead.

361: void McpServer::ParseCapabilities(const cJSON *capabilities)
362: {
363:     auto vision = cJSON_GetObjectItem(capabilities, "vision");
364:     if (cJSON_IsObject(vision))
365:     {
366:         auto url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "url");
367:         auto token = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "token");
368:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
369:         {

371:             if (camera)
372:             {
373:                 std::string url_str = std::string(url->valuestring);
374:                 std::string token_str;
375:                 if (cJSON_IsString(token))
376:                 {
377:                     token_str = std::string(token->valuestring);
378:                 }
379:                 camera->SetExplainUrl(url_str, token_str);
380:             }
381:         }
382:     }
383: }

385: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const cJSON *json)
386: {
387:     // Check JSONRPC version
388:     auto version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "jsonrpc");
389:     if (version == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(version) || strcmp(version->valuestring, "2.0") != 0)
390:     {
391:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid JSONRPC version: %s", version ? version->valuestring : "null");
392:         return;
393:     }
394: 
395:     // Check method
396:     auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
397:     if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
398:     {
399:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
400:         return;
401:     }
402: 
403:     auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
404:     if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
405:     {
406:         return;
407:     }
408: 
409:     // Check params
410:     auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
411:     if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
412:     {
413:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
414:         return;
415:     }
416: 
417:     auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
418:     if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
419:     {
420:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
421:         return;
422:     }
423:     auto id_int = id->valueint;
424: 
425:     if (method_str == "initialize")
426:     {
427:         if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
428:         {
429:             auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
430:             if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
431:             {
432:                 ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
433:             }
434:         }
435:         auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
436:         std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
437:         message += app_desc->version;
438:         message += "\"}}";
439:         ReplyResult(id_int, message);
440:     }
441:     else if (method_str == "tools/list")
442:     {
443:         std::string cursor_str = "";
444:         bool list_user_only_tools = false;
445:         if (params != nullptr)
446:         {
447:             auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
448:             if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
449:             {
450:                 cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
451:             }
452:             auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
453:             if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
454:             {
455:                 list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
456:             }
457:         }
458:         GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
459:     }
460:     else if (method_str == "tools/call")
461:     {
462:         if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
463:         {
464:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
465:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
466:             return;
467:         }
468:         auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
469:         if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
470:         {
471:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
472:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
473:             return;
474:         }
475:         auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
476:         if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
477:         {
478:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
479:             ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
480:             return;
481:         }
482:         DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
483:     }
484:     else
485:     {
486:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
487:         ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
488:     }
489: }

491: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
492: {
493:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
494:     payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
495:     payload += result;
496:     payload += "}";
497:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
498: }
499: 
500: void McpServer::ReplyError(int id, const std::string &message)
501: {
502:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
503:     payload += std::to_string(id);
504:     payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
505:     payload += message;
506:     payload += "\"}}";
507:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
508: }

514: void McpServer::DoToolCall(int id, const std::string &tool_name, const cJSON *tool_arguments)
515: {
516:     auto tool_iter = std::find_if(tools_.begin(), tools_.end(),
517:                                   [&tool_name](const McpTool *tool)
518:                                   {
519:                                       return tool->name() == tool_name;
520:                                   });
521: 
522:     if (tool_iter == tools_.end())
523:     {
524:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Unknown tool: %s", tool_name.c_str());
525:         ReplyError(id, "Unknown tool: " + tool_name);
526:         return;
527:     }
528: 
529:     PropertyList arguments = (*tool_iter)->properties();
530:     try
531:     {
532:         for (auto &argument : arguments)
533:         {
534:             bool found = false;
535:             if (cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
536:             {
537:                 auto value = cJSON_GetObjectItem(tool_arguments, argument.name().c_str());
538:                 if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeBoolean && cJSON_IsBool(value))
539:                 {
540:                     argument.set_value<bool>(value->valueint == 1);
541:                     found = true;
542:                 }
543:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeInteger && cJSON_IsNumber(value))
544:                 {
545:                     argument.set_value<int>(value->valueint);
546:                     found = true;
547:                 }
548:                 else if (argument.type() == kPropertyTypeString && cJSON_IsString(value))
549:                 {
550:                     argument.set_value<std::string>(value->valuestring);
551:                     found = true;
552:                 }
553:             }
554: 
555:             if (!argument.has_default_value() && !found)
556:             {
557:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing valid argument: %s", argument.name().c_str());
558:                 ReplyError(id, "Missing valid argument: " + argument.name());
559:                 return;
560:             }
561:         }
562:     }
563:     catch (const std::exception &e)
564:     {
565:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
566:         ReplyError(id, e.what());
567:         return;
568:     }
569: 
570:     // Use main thread to call the tool
571:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
572:     app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
573:                  {
574:         try {
575:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
576:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
577:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
578:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
579:         } });
580: }
581: 
</file>
The `GetToolsList` function retrieves a paginated list of available tools from the MCP server, filtering them based on user permissions and payload size limits. It supports cursor-based pagination to handle large tool lists efficiently and returns the result as a JSON-formatted response. If a tool cannot be added due to size constraints, an error is returned instead.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core functionality of listing tools with pagination and filtering is preserved.
- However, the method of handling the cursor differs significantly: ground truth uses tool name as cursor, while generated code interprets cursor as numeric index.
- Filtering logic is inverted in the generated code (`tool->user_only() && !list_user_only_tools`) compared to ground truth (`!list_user_only_tools && (*it)->user_only()`), leading to different behavior.
- Payload size checking mechanism differs: ground truth estimates size before adding, while generated code adds then removes if exceeded.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow diverges: ground truth uses while loop with iterator, generated code uses for-loop with index.
- Different JSON libraries used (manual string construction vs cJSON).
- Pagination implementation is fundamentally different (name-based cursor vs index-based cursor).

Functional Completeness:
- Both handle tool listing, filtering, pagination, and payload limiting.
- Generated code includes additional detailed input schema generation missing in ground truth.
- Error handling for serialization failure is present in generated but not in ground truth.
- Cursor handling and size limitation approaches are implemented but differently than ground truth.
- Missing exact payload size limit configuration (1024 vs 8000) and specific error logging from ground truth.

results.description: The `McpServer::DoToolCall` function processes tool invocation requests by validating the requested tool name and its arguments against registered tools. It parses and type-checks the provided arguments, ensuring required parameters are present and correctly formatted. If validation passes, it schedules the tool execution on the main application thread and sends the result or any encountered errors back to the caller.

349: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const std::string &message)
350: {
351:     cJSON *json = cJSON_Parse(message.c_str());
352:     if (json == nullptr)
353:     {
354:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse MCP message: %s", message.c_str());
355:         return;
356:     }
357:     ParseMessage(json);
358:     cJSON_Delete(json);
359: }
