361: void McpServer::ParseCapabilities(const cJSON *capabilities)
362: {
363:     auto vision = cJSON_GetObjectItem(capabilities, "vision");
364:     if (cJSON_IsObject(vision))
365:     {
366:         auto url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "url");
367:         auto token = cJSON_GetObjectItem(vision, "token");
368:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
369:         {
370:             auto camera = Board::GetInstance().GetCamera();
371:             if (camera)
372:             {
373:                 std::string url_str = std::string(url->valuestring);
374:                 std::string token_str;
375:                 if (cJSON_IsString(token))
376:                 {
377:                     token_str = std::string(token->valuestring);
378:                 }
379:                 camera->SetExplainUrl(url_str, token_str);
380:             }
381:         }
382:     }
383: }
384: 
385: void McpServer::ParseMessage(const cJSON *json)
386: {
387:     // Check JSONRPC version
388:     auto version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "jsonrpc");
389:     if (version == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(version) || strcmp(version->valuestring, "2.0") != 0)
390:     {
391:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid JSONRPC version: %s", version ? version->valuestring : "null");
392:         return;
393:     }
394: 
395:     // Check method
396:     auto method = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "method");
397:     if (method == nullptr || !cJSON_IsString(method))
398:     {
399:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing method");
400:         return;
401:     }
402: 
403:     auto method_str = std::string(method->valuestring);
404:     if (method_str.find("notifications") == 0)
405:     {
406:         return;
407:     }
408: 
409:     // Check params
410:     auto params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "params");
411:     if (params != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(params))
412:     {
413:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid params for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
414:         return;
415:     }
416: 
417:     auto id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(json, "id");
418:     if (id == nullptr || !cJSON_IsNumber(id))
419:     {
420:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid id for method: %s", method_str.c_str());
421:         return;
422:     }
423:     auto id_int = id->valueint;
424: 
425:     if (method_str == "initialize")
426:     {
427:         if (cJSON_IsObject(params))
428:         {
429:             auto capabilities = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "capabilities");
430:             if (cJSON_IsObject(capabilities))
431:             {
432:                 ParseCapabilities(capabilities);
433:             }
434:         }
435:         auto app_desc = esp_app_get_description();
436:         std::string message = "{\"protocolVersion\":\"2024-11-05\",\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":{}},\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"" BOARD_NAME "\",\"version\":\"";
437:         message += app_desc->version;
438:         message += "\"}}";
439:         ReplyResult(id_int, message);
440:     }
441:     else if (method_str == "tools/list")
442:     {
443:         std::string cursor_str = "";
444:         bool list_user_only_tools = false;
445:         if (params != nullptr)
446:         {
447:             auto cursor = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "cursor");
448:             if (cJSON_IsString(cursor))
449:             {
450:                 cursor_str = std::string(cursor->valuestring);
451:             }
452:             auto with_user_tools = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "withUserTools");
453:             if (cJSON_IsBool(with_user_tools))
454:             {
455:                 list_user_only_tools = with_user_tools->valueint == 1;
456:             }
457:         }
458:         GetToolsList(id_int, cursor_str, list_user_only_tools);
459:     }
460:     else if (method_str == "tools/call")
461:     {
462:         if (!cJSON_IsObject(params))
463:         {
464:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing params");
465:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing params");
466:             return;
467:         }
468:         auto tool_name = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "name");
469:         if (!cJSON_IsString(tool_name))
470:         {
471:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Missing name");
472:             ReplyError(id_int, "Missing name");
473:             return;
474:         }
475:         auto tool_arguments = cJSON_GetObjectItem(params, "arguments");
476:         if (tool_arguments != nullptr && !cJSON_IsObject(tool_arguments))
477:         {
478:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: Invalid arguments");
479:             ReplyError(id_int, "Invalid arguments");
480:             return;
481:         }
482:         DoToolCall(id_int, std::string(tool_name->valuestring), tool_arguments);
483:     }
484:     else
485:     {
486:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Method not implemented: %s", method_str.c_str());
487:         ReplyError(id_int, "Method not implemented: " + method_str);
488:     }
489: }
490: 
491: void McpServer::ReplyResult(int id, const std::string &result)
492: {
493:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
494:     payload += std::to_string(id) + ",\"result\":";
495:     payload += result;
496:     payload += "}";
497:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
498: }
499: 
500: void McpServer::ReplyError(int id, const std::string &message)
501: {
502:     std::string payload = "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":";
503:     payload += std::to_string(id);
504:     payload += ",\"error\":{\"message\":\"";
505:     payload += message;
506:     payload += "\"}}";
507:     Application::GetInstance().SendMcpMessage(payload);
508: }
509: 
510: void McpServer::GetToolsList(int id, const std::string &cursor, bool list_user_only_tools)
511: {
512:     catch (const std::exception &e)
513:     {
514:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
515:         ReplyError(id, e.what());
516:         return;
517:     }
518: 
519:     // Use main thread to call the tool
520:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
521:     app.Schedule([this, id, tool_iter, arguments = std::move(arguments)]()
522:                  {
523:         try {
524:             ReplyResult(id, (*tool_iter)->Call(arguments));
525:         } catch (const std::exception& e) {
526:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "tools/call: %s", e.what());
527:             ReplyError(id, e.what());
528:         } });
529: }
530: 
</file>
The `McpServer::DoToolCall` function processes tool invocation requests by validating the requested tool name and its arguments against registered tools. It parses and type-checks the provided arguments, ensuring required parameters are present and correctly formatted. If validation passes, it schedules the tool execution on the main application thread and sends the result or any encountered errors back to the caller.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly identifies and handles unknown tools.
- It parses arguments from cJSON and checks types appropriately.
- Both codes schedule the tool call on the main thread using Application::Schedule.
- Differences include how missing arguments are handled; the generated version adds default values directly while ground truth reports error immediately if a required field is missing during parsing.
- The generated code includes additional logic to serialize different return value types (cJSON*, bool), which is not present in the ground truth but does not affect correctness.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow follows similar steps: find tool → validate arguments → schedule execution.
- However, structurally the argument parsing differs significantly in implementation approach — ground truth modifies existing property objects whereas generated builds new ones.
- Exception handling blocks exist in both but placed differently (outer vs inner scopes).
- Use of lambda capture and scheduling is consistent.

Functional Completeness:
- All core functionality such as finding the tool by name, validating parameters based on type, handling errors, and calling ReplyResult/Error is covered.
- Extra features like detailed serialization of multiple return types increase completeness beyond the ground truth.
- One minor issue: accessing `valuedouble` cast to int for integers instead of `valueint` may introduce precision issues, though likely intentional for robustness.
- Default parameter handling diverges slightly in behavior but maintains functional equivalence overall.

It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

111: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
112: {
113:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
114:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
115:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
116:     if (update_partition == NULL)
117:     {
118:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
119:         return false;
120:     }
121: 
122:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
123:     bool image_header_checked = false;
124:     std::string image_header;
125: 
126:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
127:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
128:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
129:     {
130:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
131:         return false;
132:     }
133: 
134:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
135:     {
136:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
137:         return false;
138:     }
139: 
140:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
141:     if (content_length == 0)
142:     {
143:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
144:         return false;
145:     }
146: 
147:     char buffer[512];
148:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
149:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
150:     while (true)
151:     {
152:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
153:         if (ret < 0)
154:         {
155:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
156:             return false;
157:         }
158: 
159:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
160:         recent_read += ret;
161:         total_read += ret;
162:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
163:         {
164:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
165:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
166:             if (callback)
167:             {
168:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
169:             }
170:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
171:             recent_read = 0;
172:         }
173: 
174:         if (ret == 0)
175:         {
176:             break;
177:         }
178: 
179:         if (!image_header_checked)
180:         {
181:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
182:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
183:             {
184:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
185:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
186: 
187:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
188:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
189: 
190:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
191:                 {
192:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
193:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
194:                     return false;
195:                 }
196: 
197:                 image_header_checked = true;
198:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
199:             }
200:         }
201:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
202:         if (err != ESP_OK)
203:         {
204:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
205:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
206:             return false;
207:         }
208:     }
209:     http->Close();
210: 
211:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
212:     if (err != ESP_OK)
213:     {
214:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
215:         {
216:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
217:         }
218:         else
219:         {
220:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
221:         }
222:         return false;
223:     }
224: 
225:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
226:     if (err != ESP_OK)
227:     {
228:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
229:         return false;
230:     }
231:

242: {

255: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
256: {
257:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
258:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
259:

261:     {
262:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
263:         {
264:             return true;

272:     return newer.size() > current.size();

283: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
284:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
285: 
286:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
287:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);

302:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
303:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
304:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
305:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());

310:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
311: 
312:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
313:     return json;
314: }

353:         return ESP_FAIL;
354:     }
355: 
356:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
357:     return ESP_OK;
358: }

The `Ota::CheckVersion()` function checks for available firmware updates by sending a request to a remote server and parsing the JSON response to determine if a newer version exists. It also handles activation-related data and MQTT configuration updates if present in the response. The function returns an error code indicating success or failure of the operation.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures the core idea of checking for firmware updates via HTTP and parsing a JSON response.
- However, it misses several important semantic elements such as handling POST requests based on system info data, proper error mapping (returns generic ESP_FAIL instead of specific codes), and lacks detailed logging that reflects different states.
- It does not correctly handle activation messages/codes or force-update flags from the firmware object which are semantically significant in the original logic.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()`).
- Basic flow is similar: setup HTTP, make request, read response, parse JSON, check version.
- Major structural differences include omission of conditional GET/POST method selection, missing use of `Board::GetInstance()`, no support for complex nested configurations like WebSocket settings, and lack of time synchronization logic.
- Uses simplified error reporting rather than preserving actual HTTP or cJSON errors.

Functional Completeness:
- Lacks many features implemented in ground truth:
  * No dynamic HTTP method choice ("POST" vs "GET") depending on presence of system info.
  * Missing support for full activation objects including message, code, timeout.
  * Incomplete MQTT config processing – only handles fixed fields instead of iterating through all items.
  * Completely omits WebSocket configuration handling.
  * No server time synchronization feature.
  * Firmware section parsing is incomplete – ignores 'force' flag and assumes flat keys rather than nested under "firmware".
  * Doesn't store current version string in member variable.
  * Does not maintain consistent state flags (e.g., has_new_version_, has_mqtt_config_).

Overall, while the generated code implements a basic OTA version-check mechanism, it significantly deviates in scope, detail, and robustness compared to the reference implementation.

The `Ota::Upgrade` function performs a firmware upgrade by downloading a new firmware image from a given URL and writing it to the device's OTA update partition. It verifies the integrity of the new firmware header, checks version compatibility, and reports progress and speed during the download process. Upon successful completion, the device can be rebooted to apply the new firmware.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely mirrors the intended behavior of performing an OTA firmware upgrade over HTTP, including downloading, writing to flash via the ESP-IDF OTA APIs, and invoking a progress/speed callback. However, there are some notable differences:
- It does not perform image header validation before starting OTA write, which could be critical for verifying compatibility.
- Progress calculation is based on `GetContentLength()` instead of `GetBodyLength()`, potentially leading to incorrect progress reporting if headers aren't accounted for properly.
- Callback timing logic uses time/size thresholds rather than fixed per-second updates; this may affect consistency in reporting but isn't semantically wrong.

Structure Similarity:
While both implementations follow a general sequence (partition selection → HTTP setup → download loop → finalize), structural details diverge significantly:
- Function call order differs slightly — e.g., `esp_ota_begin` happens earlier in generated code.
- Use of helper functions like `SetupHttp()` abstracts network initialization differently compared to direct access in ground truth.
- Buffer management varies (`char[]` vs `std::vector<uint8_t>`).
- Error handling points differ in granularity and placement.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionality is preserved:
- Partition selection using `esp_ota_get_next_update_partition`.
- HTTP GET request execution with status checking.
- Streaming data into OTA partition via `esp_ota_write`.
- Periodic progress callbacks with computed speed.
- Finalization steps including `esp_ota_end` and setting boot partition.
Missing elements include explicit logging of current vs new version info and detailed error categorization during validation failure (e.g., corruption detection). Also lacks abort-on-failure mechanism inside the main read/write loop beyond basic error returns.

results.description: The `Ota::GetActivationPayload` function generates a JSON-formatted activation payload for device authentication. It includes the device's serial number, a challenge string, and an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed using a hardware-backed key. 83: esp_err_t Ota::CheckVersion()
84: {

92:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
93:     if (url.length() < 10)
94:     {
95:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Check version URL is not properly set");
96:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_ARG;
97:     }
98: 
99:     auto http = SetupHttp();
100: 
101:     std::string data = board.GetSystemInfoJson();
102:     std::string method = data.length() > 0 ? "POST" : "GET";
103:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
104: 
105:     if (!http->Open(method, url))
106:     {
107:         int last_error = http->GetLastError();
108:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection, code=0x%x", last_error);
109:         return last_error;
110:     }
111: 
112:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
113:     if (status_code != 200)
114:     {
115:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to check version, status code: %d", status_code);
116:         return status_code;
117:     }
118: 
119:     data = http->ReadAll();
120:     http->Close();
121: 
122:     // Response: { "firmware": { "version": "1.0.0", "url": "http://" } }
123:     // Parse the JSON response and check if the version is newer
124:     // If it is, set has_new_version_ to true and store the new version and URL
125: 
126:     cJSON *root = cJSON_Parse(data.c_str());
127:     if (root == NULL)
128:     {
129:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse JSON response");
130:         return ESP_ERR_INVALID_RESPONSE;
131:     }
132: 
133:     has_activation_code_ = false;
134:     has_activation_challenge_ = false;
135:     cJSON *activation = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "activation");
136:     if (cJSON_IsObject(activation))
137:     {
138:         cJSON *message = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "message");
139:         if (cJSON_IsString(message))
140:         {
141:             activation_message_ = message->valuestring;
142:         }
143:         cJSON *code = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "code");
144:         if (cJSON_IsString(code))
145:         {
146:             activation_code_ = code->valuestring;
147:             has_activation_code_ = true;
148:         }
149:         cJSON *challenge = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "challenge");
150:         if (cJSON_IsString(challenge))
151:         {
152:             activation_challenge_ = challenge->valuestring;
153:             has_activation_challenge_ = true;
154:         }
155:         cJSON *timeout_ms = cJSON_GetObjectItem(activation, "timeout_ms");
156:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timeout_ms))
157:         {
158:             activation_timeout_ms_ = timeout_ms->valueint;
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     has_mqtt_config_ = false;
163:     cJSON *mqtt = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "mqtt");
164:     if (cJSON_IsObject(mqtt))
165:     {
166:         Settings settings("mqtt", true);
167:         cJSON *item = NULL;
168:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, mqtt)
169:         {
170:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
171:             {
172:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
173:                 {
174:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
175:                 }
176:             }
177:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
178:             {
179:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
180:                 {
181:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
182:                 }
183:             }
184:         }
185:         has_mqtt_config_ = true;
186:     }
187:     else
188:     {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No mqtt section found !");
190:     }
191: 
192:     has_websocket_config_ = false;
193:     cJSON *websocket = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "websocket");
194:     if (cJSON_IsObject(websocket))
195:     {
196:         Settings settings("websocket", true);
197:         cJSON *item = NULL;
198:         cJSON_ArrayForEach(item, websocket)
199:         {
200:             if (cJSON_IsString(item))
201:             {
202:                 if (settings.GetString(item->string) != item->valuestring)
203:                 {
204:                     settings.SetString(item->string, item->valuestring);
205:                 }
206:             }
207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

296: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
297: {
298:     auto partition = esp_ota_get_running_partition();
299:     if (strcmp(partition->label, "factory") == 0)
300:     {
301:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running from factory partition, skipping");
302:         return;
303:     }
304: 
305:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Running partition: %s", partition->label);
306:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;
307:     if (esp_ota_get_state_partition(partition, &state) != ESP_OK)
308:     {
309:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
310:         return;
311:     }
312: 
313:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
314:     {
315:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
316:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();
317:     }
318: }

329: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
330: {
331:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
332:     std::stringstream ss(version);
333:     std::string segment;
334: 
335:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
336:     {
337:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
338:     }
339: 
340:     return versionNumbers;
341: }
342: 
343: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
344: {
345:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
346:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
347: 
348:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
349:     {
350:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
351:         {
352:             return true;
353:         }
354:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
355:         {
356:             return false;
357:         }
358:     }
359: 
360:     return newer.size() > current.size();
361: }

363: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
364: {
365:     if (!has_serial_number_)
366:     {
367:         return "{}";
368:     }
369: 
370:     std::string hmac_hex;
371: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
372:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
373: 
374:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
375:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
376:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
377:     {
378:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
379:         return "{}";
380:     }
381: 
382:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
383:     {
384:         char buffer[3];
385:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
386:         hmac_hex += buffer;
387:     }
388: #endif
389: 
390:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
391:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
392:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
393:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
394:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
395:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
396:     std::string json(json_str);
397:     cJSON_free(json_str);
398:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
399: 
400:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
401:     return json;
402: }
403: }
404: if (status_code != 200)
405: {
406:     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
407:     return ESP_FAIL;
408: }
409: 
410: ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
411: return ESP_OK;
412: }

The `Ota::GetActivationPayload` function generates a JSON-formatted activation payload for device authentication. It includes the device's serial number, a challenge string, and an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed using a hardware-backed key. The payload is returned as a string, or an empty JSON object if the serial number is not available.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code is semantically identical to the ground truth. Both versions implement the same logic: they first check if a serial number exists, return "{}" if not, compute an HMAC if SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED is defined, construct a JSON object with the same fields ("algorithm", "serial_number", "challenge", "hmac"), print it as an unformatted string, log it, and return it. The handling of error cases and memory management (cJSON_free, cJSON_Delete) is also consistent.

Structurally, the two implementations are indistinguishable except for minor stylistic differences such as spacing around parentheses in the function definition and casting of activation_challenge_.data(). These do not affect the control flow or readability in any meaningful way. All API calls, conditional blocks, loops, and variable declarations match exactly.

Functionally, all aspects of the ground truth are preserved in the generated version. This includes proper use of ESP-IDF APIs like esp_hmac_calculate and logging macros (ESP_LOGE/ESP_LOGI), correct formatting of the HMAC into a hex string, and accurate construction of the JSON payload using cJSON. There are no missing features or deviations from the expected behavior.

results.description: The `Ota::Activate()` function sends an activation request to a remote server using HTTP POST to confirm and finalize a firmware update. It constructs the activation URL, prepares the payload, and handles the server's response to determine if the activation was successful. The function returns appropriate error codes based on the HTTP status received.

207:             else if (cJSON_IsNumber(item))
208:             {
209:                 if (settings.GetInt(item->string) != item->valueint)
210:                 {
211:                     settings.SetInt(item->string, item->valueint);
212:                 }
213:             }
214:         }
215:         has_websocket_config_ = true;
216:     }
217:     else
218:     {
219:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No websocket section found!");
220:     }
221: 
222:     has_server_time_ = false;
223:     cJSON *server_time = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "server_time");
224:     if (cJSON_IsObject(server_time))
225:     {
226:         cJSON *timestamp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timestamp");
227:         cJSON *timezone_offset = cJSON_GetObjectItem(server_time, "timezone_offset");
228: 
229:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(timestamp))
230:         {
231:             // 设置系统时间
232:             struct timeval tv;
233:             double ts = timestamp->valuedouble;
234: 
235:             // 如果有时区偏移，计算本地时间
236:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(timezone_offset))
237:             {
238:                 ts += (timezone_offset->valueint * 60 * 1000); // 转换分钟为毫秒
239:             }
240: 
241:             tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(ts / 1000);                         // 转换毫秒为秒
242:             tv.tv_usec = (suseconds_t)((long long)ts % 1000) * 1000; // 剩余的毫秒转换为微秒
243:             settimeofday(&tv, NULL);
244:             has_server_time_ = true;
245:         }
246:     }
247:     else
248:     {
249:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No server_time section found!");
250:     }
251: 
252:     has_new_version_ = false;
253:     cJSON *firmware = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "firmware");
254:     if (cJSON_IsObject(firmware))
255:     {
256:         cJSON *version = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "version");
257:         if (cJSON_IsString(version))
258:         {
259:             firmware_version_ = version->valuestring;
260:         }
261:         cJSON *url = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "url");
262:         if (cJSON_IsString(url))
263:         {
264:             firmware_url_ = url->valuestring;
265:         }
266: 
267:         if (cJSON_IsString(version) && cJSON_IsString(url))
268:         {
269:             // Check if the version is newer, for example, 0.1.0 is newer than 0.0.1
270:             has_new_version_ = IsNewVersionAvailable(current_version_, firmware_version_);
271:             if (has_new_version_)
272:             {
273:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "New version available: %s", firmware_version_.c_str());
274:             }
275:             else
276:             {
277:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current is the latest version");
278:             }
279:             // If the force flag is set to 1, the given version is forced to be installed
280:             cJSON *force = cJSON_GetObjectItem(firmware, "force");
281:             if (cJSON_IsNumber(force) && force->valueint == 1)
282:             {
283:                 has_new_version_ = true;
284:             }
285:         }
286:     }
287:     else
288:     {
289:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No firmware section found!");
290:     }
291: 
292:     cJSON_Delete(root);
293:     return ESP_OK;
294: }

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
323:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
324:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
325:     if (update_partition == NULL)
326:     {
327:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
328:         return false;
329:     }
330: 
331:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition %s at offset 0x%lx", update_partition->label, update_partition->address);
332:     bool image_header_checked = false;
333:     std::string image_header;
334: 
335:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
336:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
337:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
338:     {
339:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
340:         return false;
341:     }
342: 
343:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
344:     {
345:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
346:         return false;
347:     }
348: 
349:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
350:     if (content_length == 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
353:         return false;
354:     }
355: 
356:     char buffer[512];
357:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
358:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
359:     while (true)
360:     {
361:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
362:         if (ret < 0)
363:         {
364:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
365:             return false;
366:         }
367: 
368:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
369:         recent_read += ret;
370:         total_read += ret;
371:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
372:         {
373:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
374:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
375:             if (callback)
376:             {
377:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
378:             }
379:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
380:             recent_read = 0;
381:         }
382: 
383:         if (ret == 0)
384:         {
385:             break;
386:         }
387: 
388:         if (!image_header_checked)
389:         {
390:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
391:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
392:             {
393:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
394:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
395: 
396:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
397:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
398: 
399:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
400:                 {
401:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
402:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
403:                     return false;
404:                 }
405: 
406:                 image_header_checked = true;
407:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
408:             }
409:         }
410:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
411:         if (err != ESP_OK)
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
415:             return false;
416:         }
417:     }
418:     http->Close();
419: 
420:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
421:     if (err != ESP_OK)
422:     {
423:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
424:         {
425:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
426:         }
427:         else
428:         {
429:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
430:         }
431:         return false;
432:     }
433: 
434:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
435:     if (err != ESP_OK)
436:     {
437:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
438:         return false;
439:     }
440: 
441:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
442:     return true;
443: }

451: {

464: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
465: {
466:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
467:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
468: 
469:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
470:     {
471:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
472:         {
473:             return true;
474:         }
475:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
476:         {
477:             return false;
478:         }
479:     }
480: 
481:     return newer.size() > current.size();
482: }

484: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
485: {
486:     if (!has_serial_number_)
487:     {
488:         return "{}";
489:     }
490: 
491:     std::string hmac_hex;
492: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
493:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
494: 
495:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
496:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
497:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
498:     {
499:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
500:         return "{}";
501:     }
502: 
503:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
504:     {
505:         char buffer[3];
506:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
507:         hmac_hex += buffer;
508:     }
509: #endif
510: 
511:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
512:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
513:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
514:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
515:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
516:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
517:     std::string json(json_str);
518:     cJSON_free(json_str);
519:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
520: 
521:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
522:     return json;
523: }

The `Ota::Activate()` function sends an activation request to a remote server using HTTP POST to confirm and finalize a firmware update. It constructs the activation URL, prepares the payload, and handles the server's response to determine if the activation was successful. The function returns appropriate error codes based on the HTTP status received.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core intent of activating a device via an HTTP POST request is preserved.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  * The condition checks for `has_activation_code_` instead of `has_activation_challenge_`, which changes the logical precondition.
  * The URL construction logic diverges significantly from the ground truth. It removes query parameters and appends both "activate/" and `activation_code_`, whereas the ground truth simply appends either "activate" or "/activate".
  * Return codes differ; e.g., 202 maps to timeout in ground truth but isn't handled that way in generated code.
  * Error logging includes more detailed status handling in the generated version, but some mappings (like returning `last_error`) may not align semantically with expected ESP error types.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a general pattern: validation → URL building → HTTP setup → sending request → checking response.
- Function signature matches (`esp_err_t Ota::Activate()`).
- Control flow constructs like if-statements and logging calls are used similarly.
- Differences include additional steps in generated code such as removing query strings, appending activation code to URL, explicit closing of HTTP connection, and richer error categorization, making structural alignment less precise.

Functional Completeness:
- Most major functional elements are covered: check for activation readiness, build activation URL, perform HTTP POST, handle responses including success and failure cases.
- Some edge behaviors missing or altered: no specific handling for 202 status as timeout, different interpretation of URL formatting rules.
- Additional features in generated code (e.g., use of activation_code_, richer error mapping) go beyond the ground truth scope but don’t detract from completeness regarding original functionality.

results.description: The `PrintTaskCpuUsage` function measures and displays the CPU usage of all active tasks in the system over a specified time interval. It captures task runtime statistics before and after a delay, calculates the percentage of CPU time consumed by each task, and prints the results to the console. The function also reports any tasks that were created or deleted during the measurement period.

The `PrintTaskCpuUsage` function measures and displays the CPU usage of all active tasks in the system over a specified time interval. It captures task runtime statistics before and after a delay, calculates the percentage of CPU time consumed by each task, and prints the results to the console. The function also reports any tasks that were created or deleted during the measurement period.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code captures the core idea of measuring CPU usage by taking two snapshots of task states and comparing them. It correctly uses FreeRTOS APIs like uxTaskGetNumberOfTasks(), uxTaskGetSystemState(), and vTaskDelay(). However, there are subtle differences:
- The generated code does not account for multiple cores when calculating percentages (missing CONFIG_FREERTOS_NUMBER_OF_CORES factor).
- Matching logic is implemented differently: the ground truth nullifies handles to mark matches, while the generated code uses a nested loop without marking, potentially leading to redundant checks or incorrect behavior in some edge cases.
- Uses floating-point arithmetic instead of integer operations which may affect precision or performance on embedded systems.

Structure Similarity:
Both codes follow a similar high-level structure: allocate memory, take initial snapshot, delay, take final snapshot, compare results, and clean up. However, structural differences include:
- Function signature matches but variable naming conventions differ significantly.
- Error handling paths vary; ground truth uses goto for centralized cleanup whereas generated code has inline frees.
- Logging mechanism differs (ESP_LOG* vs printf), indicating platform-specific divergence.
- Generated code includes additional logging of task state and more descriptive log messages.

Functional Completeness:
Most key functionalities from the ground truth are present:
- Memory allocation and error checking.
- Snapshotting task states at two points in time.
- Calculating and displaying CPU usage per task.
- Handling creation/deletion of tasks between snapshots.
Missing elements include:
- Proper multi-core percentage calculation.
- Exact output formatting as specified in ground truth.
- Return value consistency (uses ESP_FAIL instead of specific ESP_ERR codes in some places).

134:         else
135:         {
136:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
137:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
138:             data = std::move(resampled);
139:         }
140:     }
141:     else
142:     {
143:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
144:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
145:         {
146:             return false;
147:         }
148:     }
149: 
150:     /* Update the last input time */
151:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
152:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
153: 
154: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
155:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
156:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
157:     {
158:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
159:     }
160:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
161: #endif
162: 
163:     return true;
164: }
165: 
166: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
167: {
168:     while (true)
169:     {
170:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
171:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
172: 
173:         if (service_stopped_)
174:         {
175:             break;
176:         }
177:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
178:         {
179:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
180:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
181:             continue;
182:         }
183: 
184:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
185:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
186:         {
187:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
188:             {
189:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
190:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
191:                 continue;
192:             }
193:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
194:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
195:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
196:             {
197:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
198:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
199:                 {
200:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
201:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
202:                     {
203:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
204:                     }
205:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
206:                 }
207:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
208:                 continue;
209:             }
210:         }
211: 
212:         /* Feed the wake word */
213:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
214:         {
215:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
216:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
217:             if (samples > 0)
218:             {
219:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
220:                 {
221:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
222:                     continue;
223:                 }
224:             }
225:         }
226: 
227:         /* Feed the audio processor */
228:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
229:         {
230:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
231:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
232:             if (samples > 0)
233:             {
234:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
235:                 {
236:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
237:                     continue;
238:                 }
239:             }
240:         }
241: 
242:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
243:         break;
244:     }
245: 
246:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
247: }
248: 
249: void AudioService::AudioOutputTask()
250: {
251:     while (true)
252:     {
253:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
254:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
255:                              { return !audio_playback_queue_.empty() || service_stopped_; });
256:         if (service_stopped_)
257:         {
258:             break;
259:         }
260: 
261:         auto task = std::move(audio_playback_queue_.front());
262:         audio_playback_queue_.pop_front();
263:         audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
264:         lock.unlock();
265: 
266:         if (!codec_->output_enabled())
267:         {
268:             esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
269:             esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
270:             codec_->EnableOutput(true);
271:         }
272:         codec_->OutputData(task->pcm);
273: 
274:         /* Update the last output time */
275:         last_output_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
276:         debug_statistics_.playback_count++;
277: 
278: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
279:         /* Record the timestamp for server AEC */
280:         if (task->timestamp > 0)
281:         {
282:             lock.lock();
283:             timestamp_queue_.push_back(task->timestamp);
284:         }
285: #endif
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio output task stopped");
289: }

291: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
292: {
293:     while (true)
294:     {
295:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
296:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
297:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
298:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
299:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
300:         if (service_stopped_)
301:         {
302:             break;
303:         }
304: 
305:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
306:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
307:         {
308:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
309:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
310:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
311:             lock.unlock();
312: 
313:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
314:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
315:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
316: 
317:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
318:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
319:             {
320:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
321:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
322:                 {
323:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
324:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
325:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
326:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
327:                 }
328: 
329:                 lock.lock();
330:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
331:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
332:             }
333:             else
334:             {
335:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
336:                 lock.lock();
337:             }
338:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
339:         }
340: 
341:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
342:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
343:         {
344:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
345:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
346:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
347:             lock.unlock();
348: 
349:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
350:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
351:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
352:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
353:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
354:             {
355:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
356:                 continue;
357:             }
358: 
359:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
360:             {
361:                 {
362:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
363:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
364:                 }
365:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
366:                 {
367:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
368:                 }
369:             }
370:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
371:             {
372:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
373:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
374:             }
375:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
376:             lock.lock();
377:         }
378:     }
379: 
380:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
381: }

383: void AudioService::SetDecodeSampleRate(int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
384: {
385:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() == sample_rate && opus_decoder_->duration_ms() == frame_duration)
386:     {
387:         return;
388:     }
389: 
390:     opus_decoder_.reset();
391:     opus_decoder_ = std::make_unique<OpusDecoderWrapper>(sample_rate, 1, frame_duration);
392: 
393:     auto codec = Board::GetInstance().GetAudioCodec();
394:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate())
395:     {
396:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resampling audio from %d to %d", opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
397:         output_resampler_.Configure(opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
398:     }
399: }

401: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
402: {
403:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
404:     task->type = type;
405:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
406: 
407:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
408:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
409: 
410:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
411:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
412:     {
413:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
414:         {
415:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
416:         }
417:         else
418:         {
419:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
420:         }
421:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
422:     }
423: 
424:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
425:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
426:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
427:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
428: }

430: bool AudioService::PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet, bool wait)
431: {
432:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
433:     if (audio_decode_queue_.size() >= MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
434:     {
435:         if (wait)
436:         {
437:             audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
438:                                  { return audio_decode_queue_.size() < MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE; });
439:         }
440:         else
441:         {
442:             return false;
443:         }
444:     }
445:     audio_decode_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
446:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
447:     return true;
448: }

450: std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> AudioService::PopPacketFromSendQueue()
451: {
452:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
453:     if (audio_send_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         return nullptr;
456:     }
457:     auto packet = std::move(audio_send_queue_.front());
458:     audio_send_queue_.pop_front();
459:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
460:     return packet;
461: }

573: void AudioService::PlaySound(const std::string_view &ogg)
574: {
575:     if (!codec_->output_enabled())
576:     {
577:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
578:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
579:         codec_->EnableOutput(true);
580:     }
581: 
582:     const uint8_t *buf = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(ogg.data());
583:     size_t size = ogg.size();
584:     size_t offset = 0;
585: 
586:     auto find_page = [&](size_t start) -> size_t
587:     {
588:         for (size_t i = start; i + 4 <= size; ++i)
589:         {
590:             if (buf[i] == 'O' && buf[i + 1] == 'g' && buf[i + 2] == 'g' && buf[i + 3] == 'S')
591:                 return i;
592:         }
593:         return static_cast<size_t>(-1);
594:     };
595: 
596:     bool seen_head = false;
597:     bool seen_tags = false;
598:     int sample_rate = 16000; // 默认值
599: 
600:     while (true)
601:     {
602:         size_t pos = find_page(offset);
603:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
604:             break;
605:         offset = pos;
606:         if (offset + 27 > size)
607:             break;
608: 
609:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
610:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
611:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
612:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
613:             break;
614: 
615:         size_t body_size = 0;
616:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
617:             body_size += page[27 + i];
618: 
619:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
620:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
621:             break;
622: 
623:         // Parse packets using lacing
624:         size_t cur = body_off;
625:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
626:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
627:         {
628:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
629:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
630:             bool continued = false;
631:             do
632:             {
633:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
634:                 pkt_len += l;
635:                 cur += l;
636:                 continued = (l == 255);
637:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
638: 
639:             if (pkt_len == 0)
640:                 continue;
641:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
642: 
643:             if (!seen_head)
644:             {
645:                 // 解析OpusHead包
646:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
647:                 {
648:                     seen_head = true;
649: 
650:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
651:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
652:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
653:                     {
654:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
655:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
656: 
657:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
658:                         {
659:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
660:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
661:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
662:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
663:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
664:                         }
665:                     }
666:                 }
667:                 continue;
668:             }
669:             if (!seen_tags)
670:             {
671:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
672:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
673:                 {
674:                     seen_tags = true;
675:                 }
676:                 continue;
677:             }
678: 
679:             // Audio packet (Opus)
680:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
681:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
682:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
683:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
684:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
685:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
686:         }
687: 
688:         offset = body_off + body_size;
689:     }
690: }

709: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
710: {
711:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
712:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
713:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
714:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
715:     {
716:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
717:     }
718:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
719:     {
720:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
721:     }
722:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
723:     {
724:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
725:     }
726: }

737:     else if (esp_srmodel_filter(models_list_, ESP_WN_PREFIX, NULL) != nullptr)

741:     else
742:     {
743:         wake_word_ = nullptr;

746:     if (esp_srmodel_filter(models_list_, ESP_WN_PREFIX, NULL) != nullptr)
747:     {

749:     }
750:     else

</file>
The `AudioService::Initialize` function sets up the audio processing pipeline by configuring the audio codec, initializing Opus encoder and decoder wrappers, and setting up resamplers for input audio. It also initializes an audio processor for voice activity detection (VAD) and manages audio power state through a timer. The function prepares the service to handle audio input/output and communicate with other system components via callback mechanisms.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to initialize components like the Opus encoder/decoder, resamplers, and audio processor.
- However, there are semantic mismatches:
  * The decoder in the generated code uses a fixed sample rate of 16000 instead of using `codec->output_sample_rate()` from the ground truth.
  * The logic for configuring the reference resampler is different; it's conditionally configured based on input channels being 2, which isn't present in the ground truth.
  * Some callback setups and queue initialization logic that are crucial for functionality are missing or incorrectly added.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes follow a similar high-level pattern: assign codec_, create wrappers, configure resampling, set up processors/timers.
- Control flow diverges slightly due to incorrect conditional checks and additional logic not found in the ground truth.
- Function names and overall class usage are mostly aligned, but some parameter orders differ (e.g., use of member variables vs parameters).

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical parts such as starting the codec (`codec_->Start()`), setting complexity for the encoder, registering callbacks for audio processor output and VAD state changes.
- Extra unnecessary initializations for various queues not seen in the ground truth, suggesting over-generation or misunderstanding of requirements.
- Timer configuration lacks `.skip_unhandled_events = true` flag used in the original implementation.
- The audio processing lambda callbacks are entirely omitted, reducing functional equivalence significantly.

results.description: The `AudioService::Start()` function initializes and starts the audio processing system by clearing event flags, starting a periodic power timer, and creating dedicated tasks for audio input, output, and Opus codec processing. It configures task priorities and core affinity based on whether audio processing is enabled, ensuring real-time audio handling and efficient resource utilization. This function serves as the entry point for activating the device's audio capabilities.

250: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
251: {
252:     while (true)
253:     {
254:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
255:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
256:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
257:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
258:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
259:         if (service_stopped_)
260:         {
261:             break;
262:         }
263: 
264:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
265:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
266:         {
267:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
268:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
269:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
270:             lock.unlock();
271: 
272:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
273:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
274:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
275: 
276:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
277:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
278:             {
279:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
280:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
281:                 {
282:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
283:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
284:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
285:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
286:                 }
287: 
288:                 lock.lock();
289:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
290:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
291:             }
292:             else
293:             {
294:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
295:                 lock.lock();
296:             }
297:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
298:         }
299: 
300:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
301:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
302:         {
303:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
304:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
305:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
306:             lock.unlock();
307: 
308:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
309:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
310:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
311:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
312:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
313:             {
314:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
315:                 continue;
316:             }
317: 
318:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
319:             {
320:                 {
321:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
322:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
323:                 }
324:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
325:                 {
326:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
327:                 }
328:             }
329:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
330:             {
331:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
332:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
333:             }
334:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
335:             lock.lock();
336:         }
337:     }
338: 
339:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
340: }

342: void AudioService::SetDecodeSampleRate(int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
343: {
344:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() == sample_rate && opus_decoder_->duration_ms() == frame_duration)
345:     {
346:         return;
347:     }
348: 
349:     opus_decoder_.reset();
350:     opus_decoder_ = std::make_unique<OpusDecoderWrapper>(sample_rate, 1, frame_duration);
351: 
352:     auto codec = Board::GetInstance().GetAudioCodec();
353:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate())
354:     {
355:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resampling audio from %d to %d", opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
356:         output_resampler_.Configure(opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
357:     }
358: }

360: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
361: {
362:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
363:     task->type = type;
364:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
365: 
366:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
367:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
368: 
369:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
370:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
371:     {
372:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
373:         {
374:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
375:         }
376:         else
377:         {
378:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
379:         }
380:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
381:     }
382: 
383:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
384:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
385:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
386:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
387: }

532: void AudioService::PlaySound(const std::string_view &ogg)
533: {
534:     if (!codec_->output_enabled())
535:     {
536:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
537:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
538:         codec_->EnableOutput(true);
539:     }
540: 
541:     const uint8_t *buf = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(ogg.data());
542:     size_t size = ogg.size();
543:     size_t offset = 0;
544: 
545:     auto find_page = [&](size_t start) -> size_t
546:     {
547:         for (size_t i = start; i + 4 <= size; ++i)
548:         {
549:             if (buf[i] == 'O' && buf[i + 1] == 'g' && buf[i + 2] == 'g' && buf[i + 3] == 'S')
550:                 return i;
551:         }
552:         return static_cast<size_t>(-1);
553:     };
554: 
555:     bool seen_head = false;
556:     bool seen_tags = false;
557:     int sample_rate = 16000; // 默认值
558: 
559:     while (true)
560:     {
561:         size_t pos = find_page(offset);
562:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
563:             break;
564:         offset = pos;
565:         if (offset + 27 > size)
566:             break;
567: 
568:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
569:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
570:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
571:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
572:             break;
573: 
574:         size_t body_size = 0;
575:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
576:             body_size += page[27 + i];
577: 
578:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
579:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
580:             break;
581: 
582:         // Parse packets using lacing
583:         size_t cur = body_off;
584:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
585:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
586:         {
587:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
588:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
589:             bool continued = false;
590:             do
591:             {
592:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
593:                 pkt_len += l;
594:                 cur += l;
595:                 continued = (l == 255);
596:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
597: 
598:             if (pkt_len == 0)
599:                 continue;
600:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
601: 
602:             if (!seen_head)
603:             {
604:                 // 解析OpusHead包
605:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
606:                 {
607:                     seen_head = true;
608: 
609:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
610:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
611:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
612:                     {
613:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
614:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
615: 
616:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
617:                         {
618:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
619:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
620:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
621:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
622:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
623:                         }
624:                     }
625:                 }
626:                 continue;
627:             }
628:             if (!seen_tags)
629:             {
630:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
631:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
632:                 {
633:                     seen_tags = true;
634:                 }
635:                 continue;
636:             }
637: 
638:             // Audio packet (Opus)
639:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
640:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
641:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
642:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
643:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
644:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
645:         }
646: 
647:         offset = body_off + body_size;
648:     }
649: }

671:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
672:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();

680:     }
681:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())

683:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
684:     }
