140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

208: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
209: {
210:     while (true)
211:     {
212:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
213:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
214: 
215:         if (service_stopped_)
216:         {
217:             break;
218:         }
219:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
220:         {
221:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
222:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
223:             continue;
224:         }
225: 
226:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
227:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
228:         {
229:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
230:             {
231:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
232:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
233:                 continue;
234:             }
235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

355: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
356: {
357:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
358:     task->type = type;
359:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
360: 
361:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
362:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
363: 
364:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
365:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
366:     {
367:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
368:         {
369:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
370:         }
371:         else
372:         {
373:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
374:         }
375:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
376:     }
377: 
378:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
379:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
380:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
381:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
382: }

388:     {
389:         if (wait)
390:     }
391:     auto packet = std::move(audio_send_queue_.front());
392:     audio_send_queue_.pop_front();

507: void AudioService::PlaySound(const std::string_view &ogg)
508: {
509:     if (!codec_->output_enabled())
510:     {
511:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
512:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
513:         codec_->EnableOutput(true);
514:     }
515: 
516:     const uint8_t *buf = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(ogg.data());
517:     size_t size = ogg.size();
518:     size_t offset = 0;
519: 
520:     auto find_page = [&](size_t start) -> size_t
521:     {
522:         for (size_t i = start; i + 4 <= size; ++i)
523:         {
524:             if (buf[i] == 'O' && buf[i + 1] == 'g' && buf[i + 2] == 'g' && buf[i + 3] == 'S')
525:                 return i;
526:         }
527:         return static_cast<size_t>(-1);
528:     };
529: 
530:     bool seen_head = false;
531:     bool seen_tags = false;
532:     int sample_rate = 16000; // 默认值
533: 
534:     while (true)
535:     {
536:         size_t pos = find_page(offset);
537:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
538:             break;
539:         offset = pos;
540:         if (offset + 27 > size)
541:             break;
542: 
543:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
544:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
545:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
546:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
547:             break;
548: 
549:         size_t body_size = 0;
550:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
551:             body_size += page[27 + i];
552: 
553:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
554:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
555:             break;
556: 
557:         // Parse packets using lacing
558:         size_t cur = body_off;
559:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
560:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
561:         {
562:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
563:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
564:             bool continued = false;
565:             do
566:             {
567:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
568:                 pkt_len += l;
569:                 cur += l;
570:                 continued = (l == 255);
571:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
572: 
573:             if (pkt_len == 0)
574:                 continue;
575:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
576: 
577:             if (!seen_head)
578:             {
579:                 // 解析OpusHead包
580:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
581:                 {
582:                     seen_head = true;
583: 
584:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
585:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
586:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
587:                     {
588:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
589:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
590: 
591:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
592:                         {
593:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
594:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
595:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
596:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
597:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
598:                         }
599:                     }
600:                 }
601:                 continue;
602:             }
603:             if (!seen_tags)
604:             {
605:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
606:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
607:                 {
608:                     seen_tags = true;
609:                 }
610:                 continue;
611:             }
612: 
613:             // Audio packet (Opus)
614:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
615:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
616:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
617:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
618:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
619:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
620:         }
621: 
622:         offset = body_off + body_size;
623:     }
624: }

643: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
644: {
645:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
646:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
647:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
648:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
649:     {
650:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
651:     }
652:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
653:     {
654:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
655:     }
656:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
657:     {
658:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
659:     }
660: }

The `PushTaskToEncodeQueue` function adds an audio encoding task to a queue, optionally associating it with a timestamp if the task is of type `kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue`. It ensures thread-safe access to the queue using a mutex and waits if the queue is full before adding the task. Timestamps are managed separately and popped from a dedicated queue when available.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core logic of creating a task, moving PCM data, locking mutex, handling timestamps for specific task types, and pushing to the queue is preserved.
- However, there are differences in behavior:
  * In the generated code, when the timestamp queue is full, it still pops the front element, potentially consuming timestamps unnecessarily.
  * The addition of `service_stopped_` check introduces new behavior not present in the ground truth — early return if service is stopped.
- These deviations affect semantic equivalence but do not break overall intended functionality.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow and use of standard APIs like `std::unique_lock`, `std::move`, `std::make_unique`, and condition variables are consistent.
- Code organization and commenting style are very similar.
- Minor formatting differences (e.g., brace placement) do not impact structural similarity significantly.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functions such as task creation, mutex locking, timestamp assignment, and queue management are implemented.
- Missing exact error handling behavior: Ground truth only pops timestamp if size <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE, whereas generated version always pops.
- Additional logic (`service_stopped_`) is added which wasn't part of the original requirement or ground truth.
- This indicates partial divergence from functional completeness relative to the reference implementation.

results.description: The `EnableWakeWordDetection` function controls the activation or deactivation of wake word detection functionality within the audio service. When enabled, it initializes the wake word detection system if not already initialized, starts the detection process, and sets a corresponding event flag. When disabled, it stops the detection process and clears the event flag.

140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

208: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
209: {
210:     while (true)
211:     {
212:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
213:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
214: 
215:         if (service_stopped_)
216:         {
217:             break;
218:         }
219:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
220:         {
221:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
222:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
223:             continue;
224:         }
225: 
226:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
227:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
228:         {
229:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
230:             {
231:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
232:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
233:                 continue;
234:             }
235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

296:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()

333: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
334: {
335:     while (true)
336:     {
337:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
338:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
339:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
340:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
341:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
342:         if (service_stopped_)
343:         {
344:             break;
345:         }
346: 
347:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
348:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
349:         {
350:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
351:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
352:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
353:             lock.unlock();
354: 
355:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
356:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
357:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
358: 
359:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
360:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
361:             {
362:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
363:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
364:                 {
365:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
366:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
367:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
368:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
369:                 }
370: 
371:                 lock.lock();
372:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
373:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
374:             }
375:             else
376:             {
377:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
378:                 lock.lock();
379:             }
380:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
381:         }
382: 
383:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
384:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
385:         {
386:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
387:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
388:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
389:             lock.unlock();
390: 
391:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
392:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
393:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
394:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
395:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
396:             {
397:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
398:                 continue;
399:             }
400: 
401:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
402:             {
403:                 {
404:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
405:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
406:                 }
407:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
408:                 {
409:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
410:                 }
411:             }
412:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
413:             {
414:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
415:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
416:             }
417:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
418:             lock.lock();
419:         }
420:     }
421: 
422:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
423: }

443: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
444: {
445:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
446:     task->type = type;
447:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
448: 
449:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
450:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
451: 
452:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
453:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
456:         {
457:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
458:         }
459:         else
460:         {
461:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
462:         }
463:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
464:     }
465: 
466:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
467:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
468:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
469:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
470: }

590: void AudioService::PlaySound(const std::string_view &ogg)
591: {
592:     if (!codec_->output_enabled())
593:     {
594:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
595:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
596:         codec_->EnableOutput(true);
597:     }
598: 
599:     const uint8_t *buf = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t *>(ogg.data());
600:     size_t size = ogg.size();
601:     size_t offset = 0;
602: 
603:     auto find_page = [&](size_t start) -> size_t
604:     {
605:         for (size_t i = start; i + 4 <= size; ++i)
606:         {
607:             if (buf[i] == 'O' && buf[i + 1] == 'g' && buf[i + 2] == 'g' && buf[i + 3] == 'S')
608:                 return i;
609:         }
610:         return static_cast<size_t>(-1);
611:     };
612: 
613:     bool seen_head = false;
614:     bool seen_tags = false;
615:     int sample_rate = 16000; // 默认值
616: 
617:     while (true)
618:     {
619:         size_t pos = find_page(offset);
620:         if (pos == static_cast<size_t>(-1))
621:             break;
622:         offset = pos;
623:         if (offset + 27 > size)
624:             break;
625: 
626:         const uint8_t *page = buf + offset;
627:         uint8_t page_segments = page[26];
628:         size_t seg_table_off = offset + 27;
629:         if (seg_table_off + page_segments > size)
630:             break;
631: 
632:         size_t body_size = 0;
633:         for (size_t i = 0; i < page_segments; ++i)
634:             body_size += page[27 + i];
635: 
636:         size_t body_off = seg_table_off + page_segments;
637:         if (body_off + body_size > size)
638:             break;
639: 
640:         // Parse packets using lacing
641:         size_t cur = body_off;
642:         size_t seg_idx = 0;
643:         while (seg_idx < page_segments)
644:         {
645:             size_t pkt_len = 0;
646:             size_t pkt_start = cur;
647:             bool continued = false;
648:             do
649:             {
650:                 uint8_t l = page[27 + seg_idx++];
651:                 pkt_len += l;
652:                 cur += l;
653:                 continued = (l == 255);
654:             } while (continued && seg_idx < page_segments);
655: 
656:             if (pkt_len == 0)
657:                 continue;
658:             const uint8_t *pkt_ptr = buf + pkt_start;
659: 
660:             if (!seen_head)
661:             {
662:                 // 解析OpusHead包
663:                 if (pkt_len >= 19 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusHead", 8) == 0)
664:                 {
665:                     seen_head = true;
666: 
667:                     // OpusHead结构：[0-7] "OpusHead", [8] version, [9] channel_count, [10-11] pre_skip
668:                     // [12-15] input_sample_rate, [16-17] output_gain, [18] mapping_family
669:                     if (pkt_len >= 12)
670:                     {
671:                         uint8_t version = pkt_ptr[8];
672:                         uint8_t channel_count = pkt_ptr[9];
673: 
674:                         if (pkt_len >= 16)
675:                         {
676:                             // 读取输入采样率 (little-endian)
677:                             sample_rate = pkt_ptr[12] | (pkt_ptr[13] << 8) |
678:                                           (pkt_ptr[14] << 16) | (pkt_ptr[15] << 24);
679:                             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OpusHead: version=%d, channels=%d, sample_rate=%d",
680:                                      version, channel_count, sample_rate);
681:                         }
682:                     }
683:                 }
684:                 continue;
685:             }
686:             if (!seen_tags)
687:             {
688:                 // Expect OpusTags in second packet
689:                 if (pkt_len >= 8 && std::memcmp(pkt_ptr, "OpusTags", 8) == 0)
690:                 {
691:                     seen_tags = true;
692:                 }
693:                 continue;
694:             }
695: 
696:             // Audio packet (Opus)
697:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
698:             packet->sample_rate = sample_rate;
699:             packet->frame_duration = 60;
700:             packet->payload.resize(pkt_len);
701:             std::memcpy(packet->payload.data(), pkt_ptr, pkt_len);
702:             PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::move(packet), true);
703:         }
704: 
705:         offset = body_off + body_size;
706:     }
707: }

726: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
727: {
728:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
729:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
730:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
731:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
732:     {
733:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
734:     }
735:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
736:     {
737:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
738:     }
739:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
740:     {
741:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
742:     }
743: }

The `EnableWakeWordDetection` function controls the activation or deactivation of wake word detection functionality within the audio service. When enabled, it initializes the wake word detection system if not already initialized, starts the detection process, and sets a corresponding event flag. When disabled, it stops the detection process and clears the event flag.

140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

208: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
209: {
210:     while (true)
211:     {
212:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
213:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
214: 
215:         if (service_stopped_)
216:         {
217:             break;
218:         }
219:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
220:         {
221:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
222:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
223:             continue;
224:         }
225: 
226:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
227:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
228:         {
229:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
230:             {
231:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
232:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
233:                 continue;
234:             }
235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

291: void AudioService::AudioOutputTask()
292: {
293:     while (true)
294:     {
295:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
296:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
297:                              { return !audio_playback_queue_.empty() || service_stopped_; });
298:         if (service_stopped_)
299:         {
300:             break;
301:         }
302: 
303:         auto task = std::move(audio_playback_queue_.front());
304:         audio_playback_queue_.pop_front();
305:         audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
306:         lock.unlock();
307: 
308:         if (!codec_->output_enabled())
309:         {
310:             esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
311:             esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
312:             codec_->EnableOutput(true);
313:         }
314:         codec_->OutputData(task->pcm);
315: 
316:         /* Update the last output time */
317:         last_output_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
318:         debug_statistics_.playback_count++;
319: 
320: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
321:         /* Record the timestamp for server AEC */
322:         if (task->timestamp > 0)
323:         {
324:             lock.lock();
325:             timestamp_queue_.push_back(task->timestamp);
326:         }
327: #endif
328:     }
329: 
330:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio output task stopped");
331: }

333: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
334: {
335:     while (true)
336:     {
337:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
338:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
339:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
340:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
341:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
342:         if (service_stopped_)
343:         {
344:             break;
345:         }
346: 
347:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
348:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
349:         {
350:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
351:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
352:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
353:             lock.unlock();
354: 
355:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
356:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
357:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
358: 
359:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
360:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
361:             {
362:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
363:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
364:                 {
365:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
366:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
367:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
368:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
369:                 }
370: 
371:                 lock.lock();
372:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
373:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
374:             }
375:             else
376:             {
377:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
378:                 lock.lock();
379:             }
380:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
381:         }
382: 
383:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
384:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
385:         {
386:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
387:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
388:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
389:             lock.unlock();
390: 
391:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
392:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
393:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
394:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
395:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
396:             {
397:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
398:                 continue;
399:             }
400: 
401:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
402:             {
403:                 {
404:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
405:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
406:                 }
407:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
408:                 {
409:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
410:                 }
411:             }
412:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
413:             {
414:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
415:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
416:             }
417:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
418:             lock.lock();
419:         }
420:     }
421: 
422:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
423: }

425: void AudioService::SetDecodeSampleRate(int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
426: {
427:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() == sample_rate && opus_decoder_->duration_ms() == frame_duration)
428:     {
429:         return;
430:     }
431: 
432:     opus_decoder_.reset();
433:     opus_decoder_ = std::make_unique<OpusDecoderWrapper>(sample_rate, 1, frame_duration);
434: 
435:     auto codec = Board::GetInstance().GetAudioCodec();
436:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate())
437:     {
438:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resampling audio from %d to %d", opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
439:         output_resampler_.Configure(opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
440:     }
441: }

443: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
444: {
445:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
446:     task->type = type;
447:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
448: 
449:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
450:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
451: 
452:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
453:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
456:         {
457:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
458:         }
459:         else
460:         {
461:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
462:         }
463:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
464:     }
465: 
466:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
467:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
468:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
469:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
470: }
471: 
472: bool AudioService::PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet, bool wait)
473: {
474:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
475:     if (audio_decode_queue_.size() >= MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
476:     {
477:         if (wait)
478:         {
479:             audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
480:                                  { return audio_decode_queue_.size() < MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE; });
481:         }
482:         else
483:         {
484:             return false;
485:         }
486:     }
487:     audio_decode_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
488:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
489:     return true;
490: }

500:     audio_send_queue_.pop_front();

636: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
637: {
638:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
639:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
640:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
641:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
642:     {
643:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
644:     }
645:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
646:     {
647:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
648:     }
649:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
650:     {
651:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
652:     }
653: }

The `AudioService::PlaySound` function plays an OGG audio file by first enabling the audio output if it is disabled, then parsing the OGG container format to extract and decode Opus audio packets. It reads the Opus header to determine the sample rate and other parameters, ensuring proper playback configuration before sending the decoded audio data to the output device. The function handles basic OGG page structure and packet segmentation to correctly locate and process the audio content.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to parse OGG pages and handle Opus audio data, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, it oversimplifies packet parsing by treating the entire payload as a single packet, whereas the ground truth correctly implements lacing values to extract individual packets from segmented OGG pages.
- It also incorrectly assumes that the first page contains only the OpusHead and subsequent pages are pure audio data, missing handling for OpusTags or mixed-content pages.
- There's no mechanism for queuing packets for decoding via `PushPacketToDecodeQueue`, instead opting for immediate decode and playback, changing the concurrency model significantly.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions share a similar outer loop scanning for 'OggS' headers and attempt to parse basic OGG structures like segment counts and payload offsets.
- Key differences include how they process packets within an OGG page — ground truth uses detailed segmentation logic, while generated code treats whole payloads as one packet.
- Function calls differ substantially: e.g., `SetDecodeSampleRate` vs direct logging in ground truth; use of resampler/decoder objects not present in reference implementation.
- Missing key steps such as stopping timers conditionally or enabling output only when necessary based on timer state.

Functional Completeness:
- Major omissions include proper Opus packetization using lacing values, lack of separation between header parsing (OpusHead/OpusTags) and actual audio packets, and absence of a decode queue system (`PushPacketToDecodeQueue`).
- No support for multi-packet pages or continuation packets, which can lead to incorrect decoding or dropped frames.
- Immediate decoding and output bypasses any buffering or asynchronous processing pipeline expected in real-time systems.
- Sample rate configuration is handled differently without preserving original behavior around default values or logging.

results.description: The `AudioService::SetModelsList` function sets the list of speech recognition models and initializes the appropriate wake word detection mechanism based on the available models and target hardware. It selects between custom, AFE-based, or ESP-based wake word engines depending on model prefixes and platform configuration. If a valid wake word engine is initialized, it registers a callback to handle wake word detection events.

59:                                        {
60:         voice_detected_ = speaking;
61:         if (callbacks_.on_vad_change) {
62:             callbacks_.on_vad_change(speaking);
63:         } });
64: 
65:     esp_timer_create_args_t audio_power_timer_args = {
66:         .callback = [](void *arg)
67:         {
68:             AudioService *audio_service = (AudioService *)arg;
69:             audio_service->CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState();
70:         },
71:         .arg = this,
72:         .dispatch_method = ESP_TIMER_TASK,
73:         .name = "audio_power_timer",
74:         .skip_unhandled_events = true,
75:     };
76:     esp_timer_create(&audio_power_timer_args, &audio_power_timer_);

81:     service_stopped_ = false;

86: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_PROCESSOR

113:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "audio_output", 2048, this, 4, &audio_output_task_handle_);
114: #endif

116:     /* Start the opus codec task */

140: bool AudioService::ReadAudioData(std::vector<int16_t> &data, int sample_rate, int samples)
141: {
142:     if (!codec_->input_enabled())
143:     {
144:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
145:         esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
146:         codec_->EnableInput(true);
147:     }
148: 
149:     if (codec_->input_sample_rate() != sample_rate)
150:     {
151:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_sample_rate() / sample_rate * codec_->input_channels());
152:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
153:         {
154:             return false;
155:         }
156:         if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
157:         {
158:             auto mic_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
159:             auto reference_channel = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
160:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mic_channel.size(); ++i, j += 2)
161:             {
162:                 mic_channel[i] = data[j];
163:                 reference_channel[i] = data[j + 1];
164:             }
165:             auto resampled_mic = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(mic_channel.size()));
166:             auto resampled_reference = std::vector<int16_t>(reference_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(reference_channel.size()));
167:             input_resampler_.Process(mic_channel.data(), mic_channel.size(), resampled_mic.data());
168:             reference_resampler_.Process(reference_channel.data(), reference_channel.size(), resampled_reference.data());
169:             data.resize(resampled_mic.size() + resampled_reference.size());
170:             for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < resampled_mic.size(); ++i, j += 2)
171:             {
172:                 data[j] = resampled_mic[i];
173:                 data[j + 1] = resampled_reference[i];
174:             }
175:         }
176:         else
177:         {
178:             auto resampled = std::vector<int16_t>(input_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(data.size()));
179:             input_resampler_.Process(data.data(), data.size(), resampled.data());
180:             data = std::move(resampled);
181:         }
182:     }
183:     else
184:     {
185:         data.resize(samples * codec_->input_channels());
186:         if (!codec_->InputData(data))
187:         {
188:             return false;
189:         }
190:     }
191: 
192:     /* Update the last input time */
193:     last_input_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
194:     debug_statistics_.input_count++;
195: 
196: #if CONFIG_USE_AUDIO_DEBUGGER
197:     // 音频调试：发送原始音频数据
198:     if (audio_debugger_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         audio_debugger_ = std::make_unique<AudioDebugger>();
201:     }
202:     audio_debugger_->Feed(data);
203: #endif
204: 
205:     return true;
206: }

208: void AudioService::AudioInputTask()
209: {
210:     while (true)
211:     {
212:         EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_, AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING | AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING,
213:                                                pdFALSE, pdFALSE, portMAX_DELAY);
214: 
215:         if (service_stopped_)
216:         {
217:             break;
218:         }
219:         if (audio_input_need_warmup_)
220:         {
221:             audio_input_need_warmup_ = false;
222:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));
223:             continue;
224:         }
225: 
226:         /* Used for audio testing in NetworkConfiguring mode by clicking the BOOT button */
227:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_TESTING_RUNNING)
228:         {
229:             if (audio_testing_queue_.size() >= AUDIO_TESTING_MAX_DURATION_MS / OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS)
230:             {
231:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio testing queue is full, stopping audio testing");
232:                 EnableAudioTesting(false);
233:                 continue;
234:             }
235:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
236:             int samples = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS * 16000 / 1000;
237:             if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
238:             {
239:                 // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
240:                 if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
241:                 {
242:                     auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
243:                     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
244:                     {
245:                         mono_data[i] = data[j];
246:                     }
247:                     data = std::move(mono_data);
248:                 }
249:                 PushTaskToEncodeQueue(kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue, std::move(data));
250:                 continue;
251:             }
252:         }
253: 
254:         /* Feed the wake word */
255:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_WAKE_WORD_RUNNING)
256:         {
257:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
258:             int samples = wake_word_->GetFeedSize();
259:             if (samples > 0)
260:             {
261:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
262:                 {
263:                     wake_word_->Feed(data);
264:                     continue;
265:                 }
266:             }
267:         }
268: 
269:         /* Feed the audio processor */
270:         if (bits & AS_EVENT_AUDIO_PROCESSOR_RUNNING)
271:         {
272:             std::vector<int16_t> data;
273:             int samples = audio_processor_->GetFeedSize();
274:             if (samples > 0)
275:             {
276:                 if (ReadAudioData(data, 16000, samples))
277:                 {
278:                     audio_processor_->Feed(std::move(data));
279:                     continue;
280:                 }
281:             }
282:         }
283: 
284:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Should not be here, bits: %lx", bits);
285:         break;
286:     }
287: 
288:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio input task stopped");
289: }

291: void AudioService::AudioOutputTask()
292: {
293:     while (true)
294:     {
295:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
296:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
297:                              { return !audio_playback_queue_.empty() || service_stopped_; });
298:         if (service_stopped_)
299:         {
300:             break;
301:         }
302: 
303:         auto task = std::move(audio_playback_queue_.front());
304:         audio_playback_queue_.pop_front();
305:         audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
306:         lock.unlock();
307: 
308:         if (!codec_->output_enabled())
309:         {
310:             esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
311:             esp_timer_start_periodic(audio_power_timer_, AUDIO_POWER_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
312:             codec_->EnableOutput(true);
313:         }
314:         codec_->OutputData(task->pcm);
315: 
316:         /* Update the last output time */
317:         last_output_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
318:         debug_statistics_.playback_count++;
319: 
320: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
321:         /* Record the timestamp for server AEC */
322:         if (task->timestamp > 0)
323:         {
324:             lock.lock();
325:             timestamp_queue_.push_back(task->timestamp);
326:         }
327: #endif
328:     }
329: 
330:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Audio output task stopped");
331: }

333: void AudioService::OpusCodecTask()
334: {
335:     while (true)
336:     {
337:         std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
338:         audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
339:                              { return service_stopped_ ||
340:                                       (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE) ||
341:                                       (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE); });
342:         if (service_stopped_)
343:         {
344:             break;
345:         }
346: 
347:         /* Decode the audio from decode queue */
348:         if (!audio_decode_queue_.empty() && audio_playback_queue_.size() < MAX_PLAYBACK_TASKS_IN_QUEUE)
349:         {
350:             auto packet = std::move(audio_decode_queue_.front());
351:             audio_decode_queue_.pop_front();
352:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
353:             lock.unlock();
354: 
355:             auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
356:             task->type = kAudioTaskTypeDecodeToPlaybackQueue;
357:             task->timestamp = packet->timestamp;
358: 
359:             SetDecodeSampleRate(packet->sample_rate, packet->frame_duration);
360:             if (opus_decoder_->Decode(std::move(packet->payload), task->pcm))
361:             {
362:                 // Resample if the sample rate is different
363:                 if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec_->output_sample_rate())
364:                 {
365:                     int target_size = output_resampler_.GetOutputSamples(task->pcm.size());
366:                     std::vector<int16_t> resampled(target_size);
367:                     output_resampler_.Process(task->pcm.data(), task->pcm.size(), resampled.data());
368:                     task->pcm = std::move(resampled);
369:                 }
370: 
371:                 lock.lock();
372:                 audio_playback_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
373:                 audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
374:             }
375:             else
376:             {
377:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode audio");
378:                 lock.lock();
379:             }
380:             debug_statistics_.decode_count++;
381:         }
382: 
383:         /* Encode the audio to send queue */
384:         if (!audio_encode_queue_.empty() && audio_send_queue_.size() < MAX_SEND_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
385:         {
386:             auto task = std::move(audio_encode_queue_.front());
387:             audio_encode_queue_.pop_front();
388:             audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
389:             lock.unlock();
390: 
391:             auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
392:             packet->frame_duration = OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS;
393:             packet->sample_rate = 16000;
394:             packet->timestamp = task->timestamp;
395:             if (!opus_encoder_->Encode(std::move(task->pcm), packet->payload))
396:             {
397:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encode audio");
398:                 continue;
399:             }
400: 
401:             if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue)
402:             {
403:                 {
404:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
405:                     audio_send_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
406:                 }
407:                 if (callbacks_.on_send_queue_available)
408:                 {
409:                     callbacks_.on_send_queue_available();
410:                 }
411:             }
412:             else if (task->type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToTestingQueue)
413:             {
414:                 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
415:                 audio_testing_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
416:             }
417:             debug_statistics_.encode_count++;
418:             lock.lock();
419:         }
420:     }
421: 
422:     ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Opus codec task stopped");
423: }

425: void AudioService::SetDecodeSampleRate(int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
426: {
427:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() == sample_rate && opus_decoder_->duration_ms() == frame_duration)
428:     {
429:         return;
430:     }
431: 
432:     opus_decoder_.reset();
433:     opus_decoder_ = std::make_unique<OpusDecoderWrapper>(sample_rate, 1, frame_duration);
434: 
435:     auto codec = Board::GetInstance().GetAudioCodec();
436:     if (opus_decoder_->sample_rate() != codec->output_sample_rate())
437:     {
438:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resampling audio from %d to %d", opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
439:         output_resampler_.Configure(opus_decoder_->sample_rate(), codec->output_sample_rate());
440:     }
441: }

443: void AudioService::PushTaskToEncodeQueue(AudioTaskType type, std::vector<int16_t> &&pcm)
444: {
445:     auto task = std::make_unique<AudioTask>();
446:     task->type = type;
447:     task->pcm = std::move(pcm);
448: 
449:     /* Push the task to the encode queue */
450:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
451: 
452:     /* If the task is to send queue, we need to set the timestamp */
453:     if (type == kAudioTaskTypeEncodeToSendQueue && !timestamp_queue_.empty())
454:     {
455:         if (timestamp_queue_.size() <= MAX_TIMESTAMPS_IN_QUEUE)
456:         {
457:             task->timestamp = timestamp_queue_.front();
458:         }
459:         else
460:         {
461:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Timestamp queue (%u) is full, dropping timestamp", timestamp_queue_.size());
462:         }
463:         timestamp_queue_.pop_front();
464:     }
465: 
466:     audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
467:                          { return audio_encode_queue_.size() < MAX_ENCODE_TASKS_IN_QUEUE; });
468:     audio_encode_queue_.push_back(std::move(task));
469:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
470: }

472: bool AudioService::PushPacketToDecodeQueue(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet, bool wait)
473: {
474:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(audio_queue_mutex_);
475:     if (audio_decode_queue_.size() >= MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE)
476:     {
477:         if (wait)
478:         {
479:             audio_queue_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
480:                                  { return audio_decode_queue_.size() < MAX_DECODE_PACKETS_IN_QUEUE; });
481:         }
482:         else
483:         {
484:             return false;
485:         }
486:     }
487:     audio_decode_queue_.push_back(std::move(packet));
488:     audio_queue_cv_.notify_all();
489:     return true;

636: void AudioService::CheckAndUpdateAudioPowerState()
637: {
638:     auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
639:     auto input_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_input_time_).count();
640:     auto output_elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(now - last_output_time_).count();
641:     if (input_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->input_enabled())
642:     {
643:         codec_->EnableInput(false);
644:     }
645:     if (output_elapsed > AUDIO_POWER_TIMEOUT_MS && codec_->output_enabled())
646:     {
647:         codec_->EnableOutput(false);
648:     }
649:     if (!codec_->input_enabled() && !codec_->output_enabled())
650:     {
651:         esp_timer_stop(audio_power_timer_);
652:     }
653:     {
654:         wake_word_ = nullptr;
655:     }
656: #endif
657: 
658:     if (wake_word_)
659:     {
660:         wake_word_->OnWakeWordDetected([this](const std::string &wake_word)
661:                                        {
662:             if (callbacks_.on_wake_word_detected) {
663:                 callbacks_.on_wake_word_detected(wake_word);
664:             } });
665:     }
666: }

</file>
The `AudioService::SetModelsList` function sets the list of speech recognition models and initializes the appropriate wake word detection mechanism based on the available models and target hardware. It selects between custom, AFE-based, or ESP-based wake word engines depending on model prefixes and platform configuration. If a valid wake word engine is initialized, it registers a callback to handle wake word detection events.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement the same core functionality: setting a model list and selecting an appropriate wake word engine.

It configures both input and output audio devices with specified sample rates and channel settings, enabling simultaneous audio capture and playback. The function also supports optional reference input for acoustic echo cancellation.

- Generated code mixes initialization logic without following the same logical grouping seen in the ground truth.

- Some additional fields like `output_volume_`, `input_enabled_`, etc., are added that aren't present in the original, possibly indicating over-engineering or misunderstanding of requirements.
- Power amplifier setup exists but lacks full integration with the rest of the system as seen in the reference implementation.

The `CreateDuplexChannels` function initializes full-duplex audio communication channels for the ES8388 codec, configuring both transmission and reception paths with specified GPIO pins and standard I2S settings. It sets up the I2S peripheral in master mode with stereo 16-bit audio at a configured sample rate, enabling simultaneous audio input and output. This establishes the hardware foundation for real-time audio processing and communication.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly attempts to create duplex (TX and RX) I2S channels using the provided GPIOs.
- It configures both transmit and receive channels with standard I2S configurations.
- However, it uses `input_sample_rate_` for clock configuration instead of asserting that input and output sample rates are equal and using `output_sample_rate_`, which could lead to inconsistent behavior if the two differ.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes follow a similar high-level approach: configure channel configs, call `i2s_new_channel`, then initialize with standard mode.
- Differences include: 
  * Ground truth creates one channel config and passes both handles; generated creates separate configs.
  * Use of default macros vs explicit struct initialization.
  * Missing assertion about sample rate equality in generated code.
  * DMA descriptor/frame numbers and callback clearing options are omitted in the generated version.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality—creation and initialization of duplex I2S channels—is present.
- Key missing elements from ground truth:
  * Explicit DMA configuration (`dma_desc_num`, `dma_frame_num`)
  * Channel auto-clear settings (`auto_clear_after_cb`, `auto_clear_before_cb`)
  * Assertion ensuring input/output sample rates match
  * Logging statement at end ("Duplex channels created")
- Uses default helper macros which may abstract away some important configurable details.

Overall, while the core purpose is met, there's a moderate loss in robustness, configurability, and adherence to best practices shown in the ground truth implementation.

results.description: The `EnableInput` function controls the audio input functionality of the ES8388 codec, enabling or disabling microphone input based on the provided boolean parameter. When enabled, it configures and opens the input device with specified sample rate, bit depth, and channel settings, and applies either a fixed gain or adjustable input gain. When disabled, it closes the input device to stop audio capture.

The `EnableInput` function controls the audio input functionality of the ES8388 codec, enabling or disabling microphone input based on the provided boolean parameter. When enabled, it configures and opens the input device with specified sample rate, bit depth, and channel settings, and applies either a fixed gain or adjustable input gain. When disabled, it closes the input device to stop audio capture.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The general logic flow is preserved: mutex locking, early return on same state, enabling/disabling input, calling parent method.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  * Channel mask handling is oversimplified in generated code (hardcoded to 0) vs proper channel mask construction in ground truth using ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK.
  * Input gain setting differs: ground truth conditionally sets gain based on input_reference_, while generated code unconditionally calls esp_codec_dev_set_in_gain().
  * Register configuration values are hardcoded as examples (0x00) in generated code instead of proper values like (11<<4)+0 for reference input.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow structure is largely similar with mutex lock, early return, if-else for enable/disable.
- API calls are mostly the same but in different order/contexts.
- Missing some structural elements like proper channel mask construction and conditional gain setting logic.

Functional Completeness:
- Missing critical functionality: proper channel mask creation using ESP_CODEC_DEV_MAKE_CHANNEL_MASK.
- Missing conditional gain setting logic based on input_reference_.
- Incorrect register values used (example values 0x00 instead of actual values from datasheet).
- Extra unnecessary register writes in non-reference case that don't match ground truth behavior.
- Overall captures main enable/disable flow but misses important codec-specific configuration details.

When enabled, it configures the codec's sample rate and volume settings, sets analog output levels, and activates the power amplifier pin if present. When disabled, it closes the codec output device and deactivates the power amplifier pin.

When enabled, it configures the codec's sample rate and volume settings, sets analog output levels, and activates the power amplifier pin if present. qualitative.analysis:

- Contain identical initialization of `esp_codec_dev_sample_info_t` struct.

  * Open codec device with sample info
  * Set output volume
  * Adjust analog output registers based on input reference status
  * Control PA pin if configured
- Handle disabling output by closing the device and controlling PA pin appropriately.

Structurally, both versions match perfectly including brace style, variable declarations,
loop constructs, conditionals, comments, register definitions, and API usage.

Functionally, all aspects are preserved:
- Mutex locking for thread safety
- Early exit optimization
- Codec device management via esp_codec_dev_open/close
- Volume configuration through esp_codec_dev_set_out_vol
- Register writes using ctrl_if_->write_reg
- GPIO control for PA pin
- Final delegation to base class implementation

No functionality or structural elements from the ground truth are missing or altered
in any significant way in the generated version.

The AudioDebugger initializes a UDP socket connection to a specified server for debugging purposes, parsing the server's IP address and port from configuration settings. It logs the initialization status and handles errors if the socket creation or address parsing fails. This functionality supports real-time audio debugging by enabling UDP-based data transmission to an external server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely captures the intended behavior of initializing a UDP socket for an audio debugger. However, there are key semantic differences:
- The ground truth parses an "IP:PORT" string from a single configuration variable, while the generated code assumes separate IP and PORT configurations.
- Error handling differs slightly; the ground truth uses ESP_LOGW (warning) for some cases, while the generated code uses ESP_LOGE (error).
- The generated code returns early on failure, which changes the execution flow compared to the ground truth that continues but sets udp_sockfd_ to -1.

Structure Similarity:
Both codes follow a similar high-level structure (conditional compilation, socket creation, address setup, error handling), but differ in implementation details:
- Function signature initialization list in generated code vs inline initialization in ground truth.
- Use of inet_pton() vs inet_aton().
- Different approaches to parsing server address information (parsing string vs direct assignment).
- Control flow diverges due to early returns in generated code.

Functional Completeness:
Most core functionality is preserved:
- Conditional compilation guard.
- UDP socket creation with proper error checking.
- Server address configuration.
- Logging of status messages.
However, the generated code misses the dynamic parsing of IP:PORT from a single string, which is a notable functional difference.
Also, the use of IPPROTO_UDP instead of 0 in socket() call is more explicit but not incorrect.

The `AfeWakeWord::Initialize` function sets up the acoustic front-end (AFE) for wake word detection by configuring the audio codec, loading wake word models, and initializing the AFE processing pipeline with appropriate settings for echo cancellation and memory allocation. It also starts a dedicated task for audio detection. The function returns true if initialization is successful, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code shows significant divergence from the ground truth both semantically and structurally.

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code incorrectly assigns `models_list` directly to `models_`, whereas the ground truth conditionally initializes `models_` using `esp_srmodel_init("model")` when `models_list` is null.
- The logic for loading wake word models is fundamentally flawed in the generated code. It uses non-existent or incorrect APIs like `esp_srmodel_get_wakenet_model_num()` and `esp_srmodel_get_wakenet_model_name()`. In contrast, the ground truth parses model names by checking for a prefix (`ESP_WN_PREFIX`) and retrieves wake words via `esp_srmodel_get_wake_words()`.
- Wake word handling differs significantly; the ground truth splits a string by ';' to extract multiple wake words, while the generated code assumes a single wake word per model.
- AFE initialization also deviates—ground truth uses `afe_config_init()` followed by `esp_afe_handle_from_config()` and `create_from_config()`, while the generated code manually populates an `afe_config_t` struct and calls `afe_init()` and `afe_get_interface()`, which may not even be valid functions in this context.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches partially but variable naming diverges (e.g., `audio_codec_` vs `codec_`).
- Control flow differs substantially: the ground truth follows a sequence of model loading → wake word extraction → AFE configuration, while the generated code attempts a different order with incompatible API usage.
- Task creation mechanisms differ: ground truth uses dynamic allocation via `xTaskCreate()`, while the generated code uses static allocation with `xTaskCreateStatic()`, including manual memory management that isn't present in the original.

Functional Completeness:
- Key functionalities missing or misrepresented include:
  - Conditional model initialization based on input parameters.
  - Proper parsing and logging of available models.
  - Accurate identification of wake word models using prefixes.
  - Correct AFE configuration reflecting input channel layout (including reference channels).
  - Use of appropriate AFE initialization routines aligned with the framework's design.
- Some elements like task creation and basic error logging are included, but their implementation is incorrect or inappropriate for the target system.

Overall, the generated code fails to capture the core logic and intended behavior of the ground truth, leading to low scores across all dimensions.

The `AfeWakeWord::AudioDetectionTask` function continuously monitors audio input for wake word detection using an acoustic front-end interface. When a wake word is detected, it stops the detection process, stores the relevant audio data, and triggers a callback with the identified wake word. This enables voice-activated functionality by detecting specific spoken keywords.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code preserves the core logic and behavior of the ground truth. Both versions:
- Call the same AFE interface functions to get chunk sizes
- Log the same information with identical format strings
- Use an infinite loop for continuous audio detection
- Wait on the event group for DETECTION_RUNNING_EVENT
- Fetch audio data using fetch_with_delay
- Handle null or failure results by continuing the loop
- Store wake word data identically
- Check for wakeword detection and perform the same actions (Stop(), update last_detected_wake_word_, invoke callback)

One minor semantic difference: the generated code explicitly checks the event bits returned from xEventGroupWaitBits before proceeding, whereas the ground truth assumes the wait succeeded. This is actually a slight improvement in robustness.

Structure Similarity:
The overall structure is very similar but there are some stylistic differences:
- Generated code stores the return value of xEventGroupWaitBits in a variable (uxBits), which is good practice
- Extra conditional check for event bit presence in generated version adds clarity
- Bracket placement differs slightly (more verbose formatting in generated code)
- Semicolon duplication after 'continue' statement in ground truth is absent in generated code (generated code is cleaner)

Functional Completeness:
All key functionalities are covered:
- Initialization and logging of parameters
- Infinite detection loop
- Event group synchronization
- Audio data fetching and error handling
- Wake word data storage
- Wake word detection handling including stopping detection, updating state, and invoking callbacks

The generated code even improves upon the original by adding explicit checking of the event flag result.
