47: bool CustomWakeWord::Initialize(AudioCodec *codec, srmodel_list_t *models_list)
48: {
49:     codec_ = codec;
50:     commands_.clear();
51: 
52:     if (models_list == nullptr)
53:     {
54:         language_ = "cn";
55:         models_ = esp_srmodel_init("model");
56: #ifdef CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD
57:         threshold_ = CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_THRESHOLD / 100.0f;
58:         commands_.push_back({CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD, CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD_DISPLAY, "wake"});
59: #endif
60:     }
61:     else
62:     {
63:         models_ = models_list;
64:         ParseWakenetModelConfig();
65:     }
66: 
67:     if (models_ == nullptr || models_->num == -1)
68:     {
69:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to initialize wakenet model");
70:         return false;
71:     }
72: 
73:     // 初始化 multinet (命令词识别)
74:     mn_name_ = esp_srmodel_filter(models_, ESP_MN_PREFIX, language_.c_str());
75:     if (mn_name_ == nullptr)
76:     {
77:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Language '%s' multinet not found, falling back to any multinet model", language_.c_str());
78:         mn_name_ = esp_srmodel_filter(models_, ESP_MN_PREFIX, NULL);
79:     }
80:     if (mn_name_ == nullptr)
81:     {
82:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to initialize multinet, mn_name is nullptr");
83:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Please refer to https://pcn7cs20v8cr.feishu.cn/wiki/CpQjwQsCJiQSWSkYEvrcxcbVnwh to add custom wake word");
84:         return false;
85:     }
86: 
87:     multinet_ = esp_mn_handle_from_name(mn_name_);
88:     multinet_model_data_ = multinet_->create(mn_name_, duration_);
89:     multinet_->set_det_threshold(multinet_model_data_, threshold_);
90:     esp_mn_commands_clear();
91:     for (int i = 0; i < commands_.size(); i++)
92:     {
93:         esp_mn_commands_add(i + 1, commands_[i].command.c_str());
94:     }
95:     esp_mn_commands_update();
96: 
97:     multinet_->print_active_speech_commands(multinet_model_data_);
98:     return true;
99: }

103:     wake_word_detected_callback_ = callback;

116: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
117: {
118:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
119:     {
120:         return;
121:     }
122: 
123:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
124:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
125:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
126:     {
127:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
128:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
129:         {
130:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
131:         }
132: 
133:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
134:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
135:     }
136:     else
137:     {
138:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
139:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
140:     }
141: 
142:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
143:     {
144:         return;
145:     }
146:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
147:     {
148:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
149:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
150:         {
151:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
152:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
153:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
154:             if (command.action == "wake")
155:             {
156:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
157:                 running_ = false;
158: 
159:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
160:                 {
161:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
162:                 }
163:             }
164:         }
165:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
166:     }
167:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
168:     {
169:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
170:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
171:     }
172: }
173: 
174: size_t CustomWakeWord::GetFeedSize()
175: {
176:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr)
177:     {
178:         return 0;
179:     }
180:     return multinet_->get_samp_chunksize(multinet_model_data_);
181: }
182: 
183: void CustomWakeWord::StoreWakeWordData(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
184: {
185:     // store audio data to wake_word_pcm_
186:     wake_word_pcm_.push_back(data);
187:     // keep about 2 seconds of data, detect duration is 30ms (sample_rate == 16000, chunksize == 512)
188:     while (wake_word_pcm_.size() > 2000 / 30)
189:     {
190:         wake_word_pcm_.pop_front();
191:     }
192: }
193: 
194: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
195: {
196:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
197:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
198:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
201:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
202:     }
203:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
204:     {
205:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
206:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
207:     }
208: 
209:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
210:                                                {
211:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
212:         {
213:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
214:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
215:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
216: 
217:             int packets = 0;
218:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
219:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
220:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
221:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
222:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
223:                 });
224:                 packets++;
225:             }
226:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
227: 
228:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
229:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
230: 
231:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
232:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
233:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
234:         }
235:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
236: }
237: 
238: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
239: {
240:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
241:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
242:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
243:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
244:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
245:     return !opus.empty();
246: }
247: 
</file>
The `CustomWakeWord::ParseWakenetModelConfig` function reads and parses the `index.json` configuration file to extract wake word detection settings, including language, duration, threshold, and command definitions. It populates internal variables with these settings and logs each parsed command for debugging purposes. This enables the system to configure and respond to specific voice commands based on the model's defined parameters.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly parses the index.json and extracts language, duration, threshold, and commands.
- It uses cJSON library for parsing, which matches the intent of the ground truth.
- However, it incorrectly assumes a flat structure for these fields, while the ground truth expects them under a "multinet_model" object. This leads to semantic deviation.
- The handling of default values for text and action in commands shows added robustness but deviates from the original behavior.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches.
- Control flow is similar with sequential JSON parsing and value extraction.
- Uses different APIs for accessing assets (`esp_srmodel_get_buffer` vs `Assets::GetInstance().GetAssetData`) – a significant structural difference.
- Error logging and cJSON usage patterns are consistent, though placement differs slightly.
- The way commands are parsed and stored differs structurally due to assumptions about data hierarchy.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers main functionality: reading index.json, parsing fields (language, duration, threshold, commands).
- Lacks correct traversal of nested JSON structure ("multinet_model" wrapper), leading to incomplete or incorrect parsing per the actual schema.
- Adds extra logic like fallback defaults for 'text' and 'action', which aren't present in the reference but do not harm functional completeness directly.
- Properly logs parsed information and cleans up cJSON resources.

The `CustomWakeWord::Initialize` function sets up the wake word detection system by configuring the audio codec, loading voice recognition models, and registering custom voice commands. It initializes the multinet model for speech command recognition based on the specified language and threshold settings. The function returns true if initialization succeeds, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
### Semantic Correctness (Score: 85)
The generated code largely preserves the semantic meaning and logical flow of the ground truth. It correctly initializes `codec_`, clears commands, handles model initialization based on whether `models_list` is null or not, parses configuration when needed, filters for multinet models, and sets thresholds. However, there are some deviations:

- In the ground truth, `multinet_->create()` is called with two arguments (`mn_name_` and `duration_`), whereas in the generated code it's only passed one argument (`mn_name_`). This could lead to incorrect behavior if `duration_` plays a critical role.
- The generated version omits command registration logic using `esp_mn_commands_add`, which may affect functionality related to speech command handling.
- Some logging messages differ slightly but do not change core semantics.

### Structure Similarity (Score: 90)
The overall structure closely mirrors that of the ground truth:

- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow including conditional blocks, error checks, and model loading sequence remains intact.
- Use of macros like `#ifdef CONFIG_CUSTOM_WAKE_WORD` is preserved.
- Calls to ESP SR library functions such as `esp_srmodel_init`, `esp_srmodel_filter`, and `esp_mn_handle_from_name` appear in the same order.
- Differences include minor structural additions like extra null-checks for `multinet_` and `multinet_model_data_`, and removal of certain sections like command updating logic.

### Functional Completeness (Score: 70)
While most core functionality is retained, several important features from the ground truth are missing in the generated code:

- Command list population via `esp_mn_commands_add` and subsequent update through `esp_mn_commands_update()` is entirely omitted. These steps are crucial for enabling multi-command recognition after model creation.
- Logging details about active speech commands using `multinet_->print_active_speech_commands()` has been removed.
- Threshold setting condition changed from unconditional assignment to a guarded block checking `if (threshold_ > 0)`—this might be an improvement or deviation depending on intended default behaviors.
- Argument mismatch in `multinet_->create(mn_name_)` vs `multinet_->create(mn_name_, duration_)`.

Overall, while foundational setup is covered well, advanced integration aspects involving dynamic command registration and debugging output are absent, reducing completeness significantly.

It handles both mono and stereo input by extracting the left channel if necessary, stores the processed audio data, and runs the wake word detection algorithm. Upon successful detection, it triggers a callback with the identified wake word and stops further processing until reactivated.

43: void CustomWakeWord::ParseWakenetModelConfig()
44: {
45:     // Read index.json
46:     auto &assets = Assets::GetInstance();
47:     void *ptr = nullptr;
48:     size_t size = 0;
49:     if (!assets.GetAssetData("index.json", ptr, size))
50:     {
51:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read index.json");
52:         return;
53:     }
54:     cJSON *root = cJSON_ParseWithLength(static_cast<char *>(ptr), size);
55:     if (root == nullptr)
56:     {
57:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse index.json");
58:         return;
59:     }
60:     cJSON *multinet_model = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "multinet_model");
61:     if (cJSON_IsObject(multinet_model))
62:     {
63:         cJSON *language = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "language");
64:         cJSON *duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "duration");
65:         cJSON *threshold = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "threshold");
66:         cJSON *commands = cJSON_GetObjectItem(multinet_model, "commands");

194: void CustomWakeWord::EncodeWakeWordData()
195: {
196:     const size_t stack_size = 4096 * 7;
197:     wake_word_opus_.clear();
198:     if (wake_word_encode_task_stack_ == nullptr)
199:     {
200:         wake_word_encode_task_stack_ = (StackType_t *)heap_caps_malloc(stack_size, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM);
201:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_stack_ != nullptr);
202:     }
203:     if (wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ == nullptr)
204:     {
205:         wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ = (StaticTask_t *)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(StaticTask_t), MALLOC_CAP_INTERNAL);
206:         assert(wake_word_encode_task_buffer_ != nullptr);
207:     }
208: 
209:     wake_word_encode_task_ = xTaskCreateStatic([](void *arg)
210:                                                {
211:         auto this_ = (CustomWakeWord*)arg;
212:         {
213:             auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
214:             auto encoder = std::make_unique<OpusEncoderWrapper>(16000, 1, OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
215:             encoder->SetComplexity(0); // 0 is the fastest
216: 
217:             int packets = 0;
218:             for (auto& pcm: this_->wake_word_pcm_) {
219:                 encoder->Encode(std::move(pcm), [this_](std::vector<uint8_t>&& opus) {
220:                     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
221:                     this_->wake_word_opus_.emplace_back(std::move(opus));
222:                     this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
223:                 });
224:                 packets++;
225:             }
226:             this_->wake_word_pcm_.clear();
227: 
228:             auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
229:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Encode wake word opus %d packets in %ld ms", packets, (long)((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
230: 
231:             std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(this_->wake_word_mutex_);
232:             this_->wake_word_opus_.push_back(std::vector<uint8_t>());
233:             this_->wake_word_cv_.notify_all();
234:         }
235:         vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "encode_wake_word", stack_size, this, 2, wake_word_encode_task_stack_, wake_word_encode_task_buffer_);
236: }
237: 
238: bool CustomWakeWord::GetWakeWordOpus(std::vector<uint8_t> &opus)
239: {
240:     std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(wake_word_mutex_);
241:     wake_word_cv_.wait(lock, [this]()
242:                        { return !wake_word_opus_.empty(); });
243:     opus.swap(wake_word_opus_.front());
244:     wake_word_opus_.pop_front();
245:     return !opus.empty();
246: }

The `CustomWakeWord::Feed` function processes audio data to detect predefined wake words using a multinet model. It handles both mono and stereo input by extracting the left channel if necessary, stores the processed audio data, and runs the wake word detection algorithm. Upon successful detection, it triggers a callback with the identified wake word and stops further processing until reactivated.

data (used but incorrectly assumed to be raw pointer instead of vector)

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement wake word detection but diverges significantly in logic.
- It incorrectly assumes stereo based on even-sized input rather than checking codec input channels.
- Uses `esp_mn_state_t*` and accesses `state->is_waked` and `state->command_id`, which doesn't match the ground truth's use of `ESP_MN_STATE_*` enum values.
- Processes audio in chunks using a loop that isn't present in the original, changing the semantic behavior.
- Incorrectly handles detection results by accessing struct members instead of calling `get_results()`.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Control flow differs substantially due to chunked processing and different stereo handling logic.
- API calls like `multinet_->detect()` are used differently — pointer vs enum return type.
- Missing proper state handling (`ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT`, `ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING`) from the ground truth.
- Callback invocation exists but placed within a conditional block not aligned with original logic.

Functional Completeness:
- Partially covers core functionality such as feeding data to the model and invoking callbacks.
- Lacks correct state management for detection states (timeout, detecting).
- Misses `multinet_->get_results()` and result iteration over multiple commands.
- No cleanup via `multinet_->clean()` after detection or timeout.
- Improperly handles stereo conversion without checking actual codec configuration.
- Introduces new behaviors like chunk-based processing and encoding not found in ground truth.

170: void CustomWakeWord::Feed(const std::vector<int16_t> &data)
171: {
172:     if (multinet_model_data_ == nullptr || !running_)
173:     {
174:         return;
175:     }
176: 
177:     esp_mn_state_t mn_state;
178:     // If input channels is 2, we need to fetch the left channel data
179:     if (codec_->input_channels() == 2)
180:     {
181:         auto mono_data = std::vector<int16_t>(data.size() / 2);
182:         for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < mono_data.size(); ++i, j += 2)
183:         {
184:             mono_data[i] = data[j];
185:         }
186: 
187:         StoreWakeWordData(mono_data);
188:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(mono_data.data()));
189:     }
190:     else
191:     {
192:         StoreWakeWordData(data);
193:         mn_state = multinet_->detect(multinet_model_data_, const_cast<int16_t *>(data.data()));
194:     }
195: 
196:     if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTING)
197:     {
198:         return;
199:     }
200:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_DETECTED)
201:     {
202:         esp_mn_results_t *mn_result = multinet_->get_results(multinet_model_data_);
203:         for (int i = 0; i < mn_result->num && running_; i++)
204:         {
205:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Custom wake word detected: command_id=%d, string=%s, prob=%f",
206:                      mn_result->command_id[i], mn_result->string, mn_result->prob[i]);
207:             auto &command = commands_[mn_result->command_id[i] - 1];
208:             if (command.action == "wake")
209:             {
210:                 last_detected_wake_word_ = command.text;
211:                 running_ = false;
212: 
213:                 if (wake_word_detected_callback_)
214:                 {
215:                     wake_word_detected_callback_(last_detected_wake_word_);
216:                 }
217:             }
218:         }
219:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
220:     }
221:     else if (mn_state == ESP_MN_STATE_TIMEOUT)
222:     {
223:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Command word detection timeout, cleaning state");
224:         multinet_->clean(multinet_model_data_);
225:     }
226: }

The `EncodeWakeWordData` function initiates a background task to encode stored PCM audio data into Opus format for efficient transmission. It manages memory allocation for the task stack and buffer, then processes each PCM segment using an Opus encoder with low complexity settings. Upon completion, it signals the availability of encoded data and appends a termination marker to indicate the end of the wake word audio stream.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
- The generated code attempts to perform the same core function: encoding wake word PCM data using Opus.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  * Uses raw C-style Opus API instead of a C++ wrapper (OpusEncoderWrapper vs opus_encoder_create/opus_encode).
  * Processes all PCM data within a single critical section instead of using move semantics and callbacks per packet.
  * Lacks proper timing measurements for performance logging.
  * Incorrectly checks wake_word_encode_task_stack_ for early return instead of checking if task is already created.

Structure Similarity (55/100):
- Major structural differences:
  * Uses xTaskCreatePinnedToCore instead of xTaskCreateStatic, which changes memory management approach.
  * Different stack size allocation (4KB vs 4096*7 bytes).
  * Memory allocation flags differ (adds MALLOC_CAP_8BIT).
  * Task priority differs (5 vs 2).
  * Missing StaticTask_t allocation for task buffer.
  * Introduces unnecessary helper function CleanUpEncodeTaskResources not present in ground truth.
  * Different error handling approach with explicit cleanup on failure.

Functional Completeness (65/100):
- Covers main functionality but misses several key aspects:
  * Missing use of OpusEncoderWrapper class which likely handles frame duration and complexity settings properly.
  * No support for asynchronous callback-based encoding per packet.
  * Lacks proper frame duration configuration (OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS).
  * Missing complexity setting of 0 for fastest encoding.
  * Doesn't clear wake_word_pcm_ after processing.
  * No performance timing measurements.
  * Termination marker handling is simplified and not thread-safe.
  * Missing proper task handle management.
- Positive aspects: includes proper mutex protection, condition variable notification, and resource cleanup.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly follows the general intent of initializing an ST7789 LCD display using SPI.
- It configures and initializes both the panel I/O and the panel itself with appropriate parameters.
- However, there are differences in configuration values (e.g., SPI clock speed, queue depth), which may affect performance or compatibility.
- Some specific hardware interactions like xl9555_ calls and custom offset/mirror configurations from the ground truth are missing.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes follow a step-by-step approach for initialization but differ in how steps are grouped and documented.
- Function signature differs slightly; the generated version uses a class method (`atk_dnesp32s3::InitializeSt7789Display`) while the ground truth is a plain C-style function.
- Use of ESP_ERROR_CHECK is more consistent in the generated code, whereas the ground truth omits error checking in some places.
- Different naming conventions and struct initialization styles reduce structural similarity.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality such as setting up SPI interface, creating panel IO, initializing ST7789 driver, resetting, and enabling the display is covered.
- Missing elements include interaction with external GPIO expanders (xl9555_), use of specific display flags/configurations (like data endian setting), and detailed positioning parameters used in constructing the final display object.
- The generated code introduces some additional features not found in the reference (e.g., turning on display explicitly), which can be seen as enhancements rather than deficiencies.

The `InitializeCamera` function initializes the camera hardware by configuring power and reset signals through an I/O expander, setting up the parallel data interface (DVP) with appropriate pin mappings, and initializing the SCCB/I2C control interface for camera communication. It ensures proper sequencing of power-up and reset operations with sufficient delays to stabilize the camera module before creating and configuring the camera driver instance.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The general intent of initializing a camera via GPIO control and subsequent configuration is preserved.
- However, there are significant deviations in timing (e.g., 10ms vs 50ms delays), incorrect use of pin constants (`CAMERA_PIN_*` instead of `OV_PWDN_IO`, `OV_RESET_IO`), and improper sequencing (power-up occurs after reset in generated code).
- The generated code assumes a different camera abstraction layer (`camera_config_t`, `Esp32Camera(config)` + `Init()`) compared to the ground truth's direct hardware initialization using `esp_video_init_*` structures.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches but internal logic diverges significantly.
- Ground truth uses structured config objects (`esp_cam_ctlr_dvp_pin_config_t`, `esp_video_init_sccb_config_t`, etc.), while generated code manually populates a flat struct.
- Control flow differs notably — e.g., order of PWDN/RESET operations, delay durations, and handling of XL9555-controlled pins.
- Generated code includes additional error-checking and logging not found in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality like controlling PWDN/RESET through XL9555 and creating a camera instance is present.
- Missing elements include proper usage of specific I/O definitions (`OV_PWDN_IO`, `OV_RESET_IO`), correct delay values (50ms), and precise configuration expected by the ESP-IDF video subsystem used in the ground truth.
- Additional features such as frame size, pixel format, buffer count, and JPEG quality in the generated code indicate over-completion or assumption of a higher-level API that wasn't implied in the ground truth.

results.query: complete the function ReceiveWifiCredentialsFromAudio(Application *, WifiManager *, Display *, size_t)()
results.description: The function `ReceiveWifiCredentialsFromAudio` captures and processes audio input to extract embedded WiFi credentials when the device is in WiFi configuration mode. It reads audio data, converts stereo to mono if needed, downsamples the audio, and analyzes it to detect and decode WiFi credentials encoded as audio signals. Upon successful extraction, the credentials are passed to the WiFi manager for connection setup.

191: bool AudioDataBuffer::ProcessProbabilityData(const std::vector<float> &probabilities, float threshold)
192: {
193:     for (float probability : probabilities)
194:     {
195:         uint8_t bit = (probability > threshold) ? 1 : 0;
196: 
197:         if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= identifier_buffer_size_)
198:         {
199:             identifier_buffer_.pop_front(); // Maintain buffer size
200:         }
201:         identifier_buffer_.push_back(bit);
202: 
203:         // Process received bit based on state machine
204:         switch (current_state_)
205:         {
206:         case DataReceptionState::kInactive:
207:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
208:             {
209:                 current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kWaiting; // Enter waiting state
210:                 ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Waiting state");
211:             }
212:             break;
213: 
214:         case DataReceptionState::kWaiting:
215:             // Waiting state, possibly waiting for transmission end
216:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
217:             {
218:                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
219:                 if (identifier_snapshot == start_of_transmission_)
220:                 {
221:                     ClearBuffers();                                  // Clear buffers
222:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kReceiving; // Enter receiving state
223:                     ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Receiving state");
224:                 }
225:             }
226:             break;
227: 
228:         case DataReceptionState::kReceiving:
229:             bit_buffer_.push_back(bit);
230:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= end_of_transmission_.size())
231:             {
232:                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
233:                 if (identifier_snapshot == end_of_transmission_)
234:                 {
235:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Enter inactive state
236: 
237:                     // Convert bits to bytes
238:                     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes = ConvertBitsToBytes(bit_buffer_);
239: 
240:                     uint8_t received_checksum = 0;
241:                     size_t minimum_length = 0;
242: 
243:                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
244:                     {
245:                         // If checksum is required, last byte is checksum
246:                         minimum_length = 1 + start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
247:                         if (bytes.size() >= minimum_length)
248:                         {
249:                             received_checksum = bytes[bytes.size() - start_of_transmission_.size() / 8 - 1];
250:                         }
251:                     }
252:                     else
253:                     {
254:                         minimum_length = start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
255:                     }
256: 
257:                     if (bytes.size() < minimum_length)
258:                     {
259:                         ClearBuffers();
260:                         ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Data too short, clearing buffer");
261:                         return false; // Data too short, return failure
262:                     }
263: 
264:                     // Extract text data (remove trailing identifier part)
265:                     std::vector<uint8_t> text_bytes(
266:                         bytes.begin(), bytes.begin() + bytes.size() - minimum_length);
267: 
268:                     std::string result(text_bytes.begin(), text_bytes.end());
269: 
270:                     // Validate checksum if required
271:                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
272:                     {
273:                         uint8_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(result);
274:                         if (calculated_checksum != received_checksum)
275:                         {
276:                             // Checksum mismatch
277:                             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Checksum mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
278:                                      received_checksum, calculated_checksum);
279:                             ClearBuffers();
280:                             return false;
281:                         }
282:                     }
283: 
284:                     ClearBuffers();
285:                     decoded_text = result;
286:                     return true; // Return success
287:                 }
288:                 else if (bit_buffer_.size() >= max_bit_buffer_size_)
289:                 {
290:                     // If not end identifier and bit buffer is full, reset
291:                     ClearBuffers();
292:                     ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Buffer overflow, clearing buffer");
293:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Reset state machine
294:                 }
295:             }
296:             break;
297:         }
298:     }
299: 
300:     return false;
301: }
302: 
303: std::vector<uint8_t> AudioDataBuffer::ConvertBitsToBytes(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bits) const
304: {
305:     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
306: 
307:     // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
308:     size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
309:     bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
310: 
311:     for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
312:     {
313:         uint8_t byte_value = 0;
314:         for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
315:         {
316:             byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
317:         }
318:         bytes.push_back(byte_value);
319:     }
320: 
321:     return bytes;
322: }
323: }

The function `ReceiveWifiCredentialsFromAudio` captures and processes audio input to extract embedded WiFi credentials when the device is in WiFi configuration mode. It reads audio data, converts stereo to mono if needed, downsamples the audio, and analyzes it to detect and decode WiFi credentials encoded as audio signals. Upon successful extraction, the credentials are passed to the WiFi manager for connection setup.

wifi_password (missing, used directly from parsed string)

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to implement the core functionality of receiving Wi-Fi credentials via audio, but there are significant deviations in logic and assumptions:
- It assumes a fixed format ("SSID:PASSWORD") whereas the ground truth uses newline-separated values.
- The parameter `sample_rate` is used directly instead of downsampling from a fixed high-rate input (16kHz).
- There's no handling for dual-channel audio conversion to mono as seen in the ground truth.
- Placeholder comments indicate missing real-world audio capture implementation which undermines semantic equivalence.

Structure Similarity:
While both functions aim to process audio for Wi-Fi credentials, structural differences include:
- Function signature mismatch: the fourth parameter represents 'input_channels' in ground truth vs 'sample_rate' in generated code.
- Different constant definitions and lack of proper downsampling mechanism in generated code.
- Missing critical elements like checking application state (`kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring`) in a loop.
- Absence of integration with `SsidManager`, use of `vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1))` vs `pdMS_TO_TICKS(10)`, and other timing/logic discrepancies.

Functional Completeness:
Several important features from the ground truth are either omitted or incorrectly implemented:
- No support for stereo-to-mono conversion based on channel count.
- Lack of correct downsampling logic according to specified rates.
- Incomplete parsing of received text data (uses ':' instead of '\n').
- Missing integration with system components such as `SsidManager::AddSsid()` and `WifiManager::StopConfigAp()`.
- Early exit through `break` rather than returning after successful credential processing.
Overall, while conceptually aligned, the generated version lacks fidelity to the detailed behavior expected by the reference implementation.

results.description: The `ProcessAudioSamples` function processes incoming audio samples by buffering them and applying the Goertzel algorithm to detect specific frequency components (mark and space tones). It calculates the probability of a mark tone being present in each buffered window and returns these probabilities as output. This enables frequency-based audio signal detection and classification.

135:         : frequency_(frequency), window_size_(window_size)
136:     {

138:         angular_frequency_ = 2.0f * M_PI * frequency_;
139:         cos_coefficient_ = std::cos(angular_frequency_);

141:         filter_coefficient_ = 2.0f * cos_coefficient_;

155:     void FrequencyDetector::ProcessSample(float sample)
156:     {
157:         if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
158:         {
159:             return;
160:         }
161: 
162:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-2]
163:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
164:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-1]
165:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
166: 
167:         float s_current = sample + filter_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2;
168: 
169:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_minus_1); // Put S[-1] back
170:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_current); // Add new S[0]

179: 
180:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_[1];                         // S[-1]
181:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_[0];                         // S[-2]
182:         float real_part = cos_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2; // Real part
183:         float imaginary_part = sin_coefficient_ * s_minus_1;        // Imaginary part
184: 
185:         return std::sqrt(real_part * real_part + imaginary_part * imaginary_part) /

The `ProcessAudioSamples` function processes incoming audio samples by buffering them and applying the Goertzel algorithm to detect specific frequency components (mark and space tones). It calculates the probability of a mark tone being present in each buffered window and returns these probabilities as output. This enables frequency-based audio signal detection and classification.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code attempts to implement audio signal processing but diverges significantly in logic and behavior from the ground truth:

Semantic Differences:
- The ground truth uses a sliding window approach where old samples are removed with pop_front(), while the generated code clears the entire buffer after each window.
- The ground truth has an output_sample_count_ mechanism that controls when processing occurs (every samples_per_bit_ samples), whereas the generated code processes every time the buffer fills.
- The generated code resets detectors before processing rather than after, changing the temporal relationship between detections.

Structural Differences:
- Function signature matches but internal variable names differ (probabilities vs result).
- Different control flow patterns - ground truth uses explicit size checking and conditional buffering, generated code uses clear-based windowing.
- Missing several member variables used in ground truth: output_sample_count_, samples_per_bit_, and epsilon handling.

Functional Incompleteness:
- Lacks output_sample_count_ throttling mechanism entirely.
- No samples_per_bit_ based triggering.

134:     FrequencyDetector::FrequencyDetector(float frequency, size_t window_size)
135:         : frequency_(frequency), window_size_(window_size)
136:     {
137:         frequency_bin_ = std::floor(frequency_ * static_cast<float>(window_size_));
138:         angular_frequency_ = 2.0f * M_PI * frequency_;
139:         cos_coefficient_ = std::cos(angular_frequency_);
140:         sin_coefficient_ = std::sin(angular_frequency_);
141:         filter_coefficient_ = 2.0f * cos_coefficient_;
142: 
143:         // Initialize state buffer
144:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
145:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
146:     }
147: 
148:     void FrequencyDetector::Reset()
149:     {
150:         state_buffer_.clear();
151:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
152:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
153:     }
154: 
155:     void FrequencyDetector::ProcessSample(float sample)
156:     {
157:         if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
158:         {
159:             return;
160:         }
161: 
162:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-2]
163:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
164:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-1]
165:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
166: 
167:         float s_current = sample + filter_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2;
168: 
169:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_minus_1); // Put S[-1] back
170:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_current); // Add new S[0]
171:     }
172: 
173:     float FrequencyDetector::GetAmplitude() const
174:     {
175:         if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
176:         {
177:             return 0.0f;
178:         }
179: 
180:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_[1];                         // S[-1]
181:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_[0];                         // S[-2]
182:         float real_part = cos_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2; // Real part
183:         float imaginary_part = sin_coefficient_ * s_minus_1;        // Imaginary part
184: 
185:         return std::sqrt(real_part * real_part + imaginary_part * imaginary_part) /
186:                (static_cast<float>(window_size_) / 2.0f);
187:     }

190:     AudioSignalProcessor::AudioSignalProcessor(size_t sample_rate, size_t mark_frequency, size_t space_frequency,
191:                                                size_t bit_rate, size_t window_size)
192:         : input_buffer_size_(window_size), output_sample_count_(0)
193:     {
194:         if (sample_rate % bit_rate != 0)
195:         {
196:             // On ESP32 we can continue execution, but log the error
197:             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Sample rate %zu is not divisible by bit rate %zu", sample_rate, bit_rate);
198:         }
199: 
200:         float normalized_mark_freq = static_cast<float>(mark_frequency) / static_cast<float>(sample_rate);
201:         float normalized_space_freq = static_cast<float>(space_frequency) / static_cast<float>(sample_rate);
202: 
203:         mark_detector_ = std::make_unique<FrequencyDetector>(normalized_mark_freq, window_size);
204:         space_detector_ = std::make_unique<FrequencyDetector>(normalized_space_freq, window_size);
205: 
206:         samples_per_bit_ = sample_rate / bit_rate; // Number of samples per bit
207:     }
208: 
209:     std::vector<float> AudioSignalProcessor::ProcessAudioSamples(const std::vector<float> &samples)
210:     {
211:         std::vector<float> result;
212: 
213:         for (float sample : samples)
214:         {
215:             if (input_buffer_.size() < input_buffer_size_)
216:             {
217:                 input_buffer_.push_back(sample); // Just add, don't process yet
218:             }
219:             else
220:             {
221:                 // Input buffer is full, process the data
222:                 input_buffer_.pop_front();       // Remove oldest sample
223:                 input_buffer_.push_back(sample); // Add new sample
224:                 output_sample_count_++;
225: 
226:                 if (output_sample_count_ >= samples_per_bit_)
227:                 {
228:                     // Process all samples in the window using Goertzel algorithm
229:                     for (float window_sample : input_buffer_)
230:                     {
231:                         mark_detector_->ProcessSample(window_sample);
232:                         space_detector_->ProcessSample(window_sample);
233:                     }
234: 
235:                     float mark_amplitude = mark_detector_->GetAmplitude();   // Mark amplitude
236:                     float space_amplitude = space_detector_->GetAmplitude(); // Space amplitude
237: 
238:                     // Avoid division by zero
239:                     float mark_probability = mark_amplitude /
240:                                              (space_amplitude + mark_amplitude + std::numeric_limits<float>::epsilon());
241:                     result.push_back(mark_probability);
242: 
243:                     // Reset detector windows
244:                     mark_detector_->Reset();
245:                     space_detector_->Reset();
246:                     output_sample_count_ = 0; // Reset output counter
247:                 }
248:             }
249:         }
250: 
251:         return result;
252:     }

301:     {
302:         std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
303: 
304:         // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
305:         size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
306:         bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
307: 
308:         for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
309:         {
310:             uint8_t byte_value = 0;
311:             for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
312:             {
313:                 byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
314:             }
315:             bytes.push_back(byte_value);
316:         }
317: 
318:         return bytes;
319:     }

The `ProcessProbabilityData` function processes a stream of probability values by comparing each against a threshold to generate binary bits, which are then buffered and analyzed to detect specific transmission states (inactive, waiting, receiving). It manages state transitions based on matching predefined start and end transmission identifiers and handles data reception, including optional checksum validation upon completion. The function maintains internal buffers and state machines to coordinate the reception and processing of binary data sequences.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core idea of processing bits based on probabilities and managing a state machine is preserved.
- However, there are significant differences in how states are handled:
  - Ground truth uses `kInactive`, `kWaiting`, and `kReceiving` states, whereas the generated code uses `kInactive`, `kWaitingForData`, and `kReceivingComplete`.
  - In ground truth, transition from `kInactive` to `kWaiting` occurs when buffer size reaches start pattern length, but actual data reception begins only upon matching the start pattern. Generated code conflates these steps.
  - The logic for detecting start/end patterns differs significantly—ground truth compares snapshots of identifier_buffer_, while generated code checks within bit_buffer_ directly.
  - Checksum handling also varies: ground truth removes both payload and identifier parts before validating checksum, while generated code assumes last byte is always checksum without removing identifiers.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Both use range-based for loops over input probabilities and switch statements for state management.
- Common member variables like `identifier_buffer_`, `bit_buffer_`, `current_state_`, `start_of_transmission_`, `end_of_transmission_`, `enable_checksum_validation_`, and `decoded_text` are used similarly.
- However, some key structural elements differ:
  - Buffer management differs—ground truth maintains fixed-size identifier buffer by popping front, while generated code has more complex conditional pushing/popping.
  - State transitions don't align perfectly (e.g., missing explicit logging, different intermediate states).
  - Some helper method usages such as `ClearBuffers()` and `ConvertBitsToBytes()` appear in similar contexts but applied differently.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality of converting probabilities to bits and buffering them exists.
- Basic state machine behavior for detecting transmission boundaries is attempted.
- Missing critical behaviors:
  - Proper synchronization between identifier and bit buffers during pattern detection.
  - Accurate extraction of text data after stripping protocol overhead (start/end markers and optional checksum).
  - Handling edge cases like buffer overflow or malformed packets with appropriate resets.
  - Logging debug information as seen in ground truth (`ESP_LOGI`, `ESP_LOGW`).
  - Specific handling of minimum packet lengths and correct slicing of data segments.
  - Use of constants like `kLogTag` which may impact integration.

The `Backlight::SetBrightness` function adjusts the display backlight brightness to a specified level (0-100%), optionally saving the setting permanently. It smoothly transitions the brightness using a periodic timer and prevents redundant updates if the new value matches the current one.

qualitative.analysis:

The generated code largely preserves the intended behavior of setting and transitioning backlight brightness. It correctly clamps input values, avoids redundant updates, saves settings when required, and initiates a transition. However, there are subtle differences:

- The logic for computing `step_` is more complex in the generated version, attempting to calculate a dynamic step based on a fixed number of transition steps (`kTransitionSteps`). This deviates from the simpler sign-based approach in the ground truth.
- Timer interval differs (30ms vs 5ms), which affects smoothness of transitions.
- Use of `settings.Save()` implies an explicit save mechanism not seen in the ground truth, suggesting potential divergence in how settings persistence works.

These semantic variations may lead to different runtime behaviors but do not break core functionality.

- Control structures differ — the ground truth uses a ternary operator for determining `step_`, while the generated code implements custom rounding logic.

- Comments are more descriptive in the generated code but differ stylistically from those in the ground truth.

- Logging is omitted in the generated code, representing a minor loss in observability/debugging capability

Missing elements include logging (`ESP_LOGI`) and exact timing fidelity (5ms vs 30ms). Also, the generated code assumes a `Save()` method exists, which might not align with the actual Settings interface shown in the ground truth.

44:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Brightness value (%d) is too small, setting to default (10)", saved_brightness);
45:         saved_brightness = 10; // 设置一个较低的默认值
46:     }
47: 
48:     SetBrightness(saved_brightness);
49: }
50: 
51: void Backlight::SetBrightness(uint8_t brightness, bool permanent)
52: {
53:     if (brightness > 100)
54:     {
55:         brightness = 100;
56:     }
57: 
58:     if (brightness_ == brightness)
59:     {
60:         return;
61:     }
62: 
63:     if (permanent)
64:     {
65:         Settings settings("display", true);
66:         settings.SetInt("brightness", brightness);
67:     }
68: 
69:     target_brightness_ = brightness;
70:     step_ = (target_brightness_ > brightness_) ? 1 : -1;
71: 
72:     if (transition_timer_ != nullptr)
73:     {
74:         // 启动定时器，每 5ms 更新一次
75:         esp_timer_start_periodic(transition_timer_, 5 * 1000);
76:     }
77:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Set brightness to %d", brightness);
78: }
79: 
80: void Backlight::OnTransitionTimer()
81: {
82:     if (brightness_ == target_brightness_)
83:     {
84:         esp_timer_stop(transition_timer_);
85:         return;
86:     }
87: 
88:     brightness_ += step_;
89:     SetBrightnessImpl(brightness_);
90: 
91:     if (brightness_ == target_brightness_)
92:     {
93:         esp_timer_stop(transition_timer_);
94:     }
95: }

102: {
103:     ledc_stop(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0, 0);
104: }

106: void PwmBacklight::SetBrightnessImpl(uint8_t brightness)
107: {
108:     // LEDC resolution set to 10bits, thus: 100% = 1023
109:     uint32_t duty_cycle = (1023 * brightness) / 100;
110:     ledc_set_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0, duty_cycle);
111:     ledc_update_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0);
112: }

The `PwmBacklight` class initializes and configures a PWM-based backlight control system using the LEDC peripheral, setting up both the timer and channel to drive a display backlight with specified frequency and inversion settings. It abstracts the low-level hardware configuration to provide a clean interface for controlling display brightness. This enables dynamic adjustment of screen backlight intensity while preventing issues like inductor whining through proper frequency selection.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code is semantically very close to the ground truth. Both initialize a PWM-based backlight using the LEDC peripheral of an ESP32. They configure both the timer and channel with matching parameters such as speed mode, duty resolution, timer number, and clock configuration. The logic for setting up the LEDC hardware is preserved accurately.

Structurally, the two implementations are nearly identical in terms of function signature and overall flow. However, there are minor differences:
- The ground truth uses designated initializers more completely, including `.deconfigure = false` in the timer config which is missing from the generated version.
- In the flags section, the ground truth directly assigns the boolean value to `.output_invert`, while the generated code uses a ternary operator (`? 1 : 0`) — this has equivalent behavior but slightly differs stylistically.

Regarding functional completeness, the core functionality is fully implemented. However, the generated code omits the comment about preventing inductor whine by using a higher PWM frequency, which may be important contextually even if not critical to execution. Also, it lacks explicit initialization of `.deconfigure`, potentially leading to undefined behavior depending on compiler and struct padding.

Overall, the generated code captures the intended functionality well but misses some finer details and documentation that contribute to robustness and maintainability.

The `Board::GenerateUuid()` function generates a unique identifier (UUID) in the standard UUID v4 format using the ESP32's hardware random number generator. It ensures uniqueness by creating a 16-byte random value, setting the appropriate version and variant bits, and formatting it as a standardized 36-character hexadecimal string with hyphens. This UUID can be used for device identification or session management within the system.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both implementations correctly generate a UUIDv4 according to RFC 4122.
- They both use ESP32's hardware random number generation and properly set version and variant bits.
- The logic for formatting the final UUID string is identical.
- One minor difference: Ground truth uses `esp_fill_random()` which is more efficient for filling an array, whereas generated code uses a loop with `esp_random()`. Semantically equivalent but less optimal.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow and organization are very similar.
- Differences include: 
  * Use of loop vs direct call to fill buffer (`esp_fill_random` vs `for` + `esp_random`)
  * Slight variation in comment style and granularity
  * Formatting/naming styles differ slightly (e.g., spacing around braces)
- API usage differs only in how randomness is acquired, not in overall approach or result

Functional Completeness:
- All core functionality is preserved:
  * Random byte generation using ESP32 APIs
  * Proper setting of UUID version (4) and variant (RFC 4122)
  * Correct string formatting into standard UUID format
  * Return type and value match expected behavior
- No missing features or steps compared to reference implementation

The `Board::GetSystemInfoJson()` function generates a JSON-formatted string containing comprehensive system information, including hardware details (chip model, flash size, MAC address), application metadata (name, version, compile time), and partition table data. It also includes runtime information such as minimum free heap size and unique identifiers like UUID. This structured data provides a complete snapshot of the device's current configuration and state for diagnostics or reporting purposes.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to collect similar system information as the ground truth but deviates in several key aspects:
- It uses a flat C-style approach with snprintf instead of building structured JSON strings incrementally.
- Some values are hardcoded or use different sources (e.g., BOARD_NAME instead of Lang::CODE, fixed app name "xiaozhi").
- The JSON structure differs significantly from the expected output format described in comments and implemented in ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches, but internal organization is very different.
- Uses a monolithic snprintf instead of modular JSON construction.
- Control flow diverges greatly—uses conditional compilation and switch statements not present in the original.
- APIs called differ in scope and usage; many specific helper functions like SystemInfo::GetFlashSize(), esp_app_get_description() are missing.

Functional Completeness:
Missing major components compared to ground truth:
- No application description data (project name, IDF version, ELF SHA256)
- No partition table array iteration
- No display info handling
- No board-specific JSON inclusion
- Lacks proper escaping/handling for dynamic string fields that could contain quotes
- Fixed buffer size may lead to truncation
- Hardcoded application name and incomplete time formatting (missing 'T' and 'Z' for ISO 8601 compliance)
- Incorrectly names "partition" instead of "partition_table" and omits type/subtype fields

180:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length = %d, frame.width = %d, frame.height = %d",
181:                      mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length, frame_.width, frame_.height);
182: #endif // CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_ROTATE_CAMERA_IMAGE
183:             ESP_LOG_BUFFER_HEXDUMP(TAG, mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start, MIN(mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length, 256),
184:                                    ESP_LOG_DEBUG);
185: 
186:             switch (sensor_format_)
187:             {
188:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565:
189:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB24:
190:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUYV:
191:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUV420:
192:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_GREY:
193: #ifdef CONFIG_XIAOZHI_CAMERA_ALLOW_JPEG_INPUT
194:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_JPEG:
195: #endif // CONFIG_XIAOZHI_CAMERA_ALLOW_JPEG_INPUT
196: #ifdef CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_CAMERA_ENDIANNESS_SWAP
197:             {
198:                 auto src16 = (uint16_t *)mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start;
199:                 auto dst16 = (uint16_t *)frame_.data;
200:                 size_t count = (size_t)mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length / 2;
201:                 for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++)
202:                 {
203:                     dst16[i] = __builtin_bswap16(src16[i]);
204:                 }
205:             }
206: #else
207:                 memcpy(frame_.data, mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start,
208:                        MIN(mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length, frame_.len));
209: #endif // CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_CAMERA_ENDIANNESS_SWAP
210:                 frame_.format = sensor_format_;
211:                 break;
212:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUV422P:
213:             {
214:                 // 这个格式是 422 YUYV，不是 planer
215:                 frame_.format = V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUYV;
216: #ifdef CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_CAMERA_ENDIANNESS_SWAP
217:                 {
218:                     auto src16 = (uint16_t *)mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start;
219:                     auto dst16 = (uint16_t *)frame_.data;
220:                     size_t count = (size_t)mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length / 2;
221:                     for (size_t i = 0; i < count; i++)
222:                     {
223:                         dst16[i] = __builtin_bswap16(src16[i]);
224:                     }
225:                 }
226: #else
227:                 memcpy(frame_.data, mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start,
228:                        MIN(mmap_buffers_[buf.index].length, frame_.len));
229: #endif // CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_CAMERA_ENDIANNESS_SWAP
230:                 break;
231:             }
232:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565X:
233:             {
234:                 // 大端序的 RGB565 需要转换为小端序
235:                 // 目前 esp_video 的大小端都会返回格式为 RGB565，不会返回格式为 RGB565X，此 case 用于未来版本兼容
236:                 auto src16 = (uint16_t *)mmap_buffers_[buf.index].start;
237:                 auto dst16 = (uint16_t *)frame_.data;
238:                 size_t pixel_count = (size_t)frame_.width * (size_t)frame_.height;
239:                 for (size_t i = 0; i < pixel_count; i++)
240:                 {
241:                     dst16[i] = __builtin_bswap16(src16[i]);
242:                 }
243:                 frame_.format = V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565;
244:                 break;
245:             }
246:             default:
247:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "unsupported sensor format: 0x%08lx", sensor_format_);
248:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
249:                 {
250:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
251:                 }
252:                 return false;
253:             }
254: 
255: #ifdef CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_ROTATE_CAMERA_IMAGE
256: #ifndef CONFIG_SOC_PPA_SUPPORTED
257:             uint8_t *rotate_dst =
258:                 (uint8_t *)heap_caps_aligned_alloc(64, frame_.len, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
259:             if (rotate_dst == nullptr)
260:             {
261:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate memory for rotate image");
262:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
263:                 {
264:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
265:                 }
266:                 return false;
267:             }
268:             uint8_t *rotate_src = (uint8_t *)frame_.data;
269: 
270:             esp_imgfx_rotate_cfg_t rotate_cfg = {
271:                 .in_res =
272:                     {
273:                         .width = static_cast<int16_t>(sensor_width_),
274:                         .height = static_cast<int16_t>(sensor_height_),
275:                     },
276:                 .degree = IMAGE_ROTATION_ANGLE,
277:             };
278:             switch (frame_.format)
279:             {
280:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565:
281:                 rotate_cfg.in_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_RGB565_LE;
282:                 break;
283:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUYV:
284:                 rotate_cfg.in_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_RGB565_LE;
285:                 break;
286:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_GREY:
287:                 rotate_cfg.in_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_Y;
288:                 break;
289:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB24:
290:                 rotate_cfg.in_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_RGB888;
291:                 break;
292:             default:
293:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "unsupported sensor format: 0x%08lx", sensor_format_);
294:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
295:                 {
296:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
297:                 }
298:                 return false;
299:             }
300:             esp_imgfx_rotate_handle_t rotate_handle = nullptr;
301:             esp_imgfx_err_t imgfx_err = esp_imgfx_rotate_open(&rotate_cfg, &rotate_handle);
302:             if (imgfx_err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK || rotate_handle == nullptr)
303:             {
304:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_rotate_create failed");
305:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
306:                 {
307:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
308:                 }
309:                 return false;
310:             }
311: 
312:             esp_imgfx_data_t rotate_input_data = {
313:                 .data = rotate_src,
314:                 .data_len = frame_.len,
315:             };
316:             esp_imgfx_data_t rotate_output_data = {
317:                 .data = rotate_dst,
318:                 .data_len = frame_.len,
319:             };
320: 
321:             imgfx_err = esp_imgfx_rotate_process(rotate_handle, &rotate_input_data, &rotate_output_data);
322:             if (imgfx_err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK)
323:             {
324:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_rotate_process failed");
325:                 heap_caps_free(rotate_dst);
326:                 rotate_dst = nullptr;
327:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
328:                 {
329:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
330:                 }
331:                 esp_imgfx_rotate_close(rotate_handle);
332:                 rotate_handle = nullptr;
333:                 return false;
334:             }
335: 
336:             frame_.data = rotate_dst;
337: 
338:             heap_caps_free(rotate_src);
339:             rotate_src = nullptr;
340: 
341:             esp_imgfx_rotate_close(rotate_handle);
342:             rotate_handle = nullptr;
343: #else  // CONFIG_SOC_PPA_SUPPORTED
344:             uint8_t *rotate_src = nullptr;
345: 
346:             ppa_srm_color_mode_t ppa_color_mode;
347:             switch (frame_.format)
348:             {
349:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565:
350:                 rotate_src = (uint8_t *)frame_.data;
351:                 ppa_color_mode = PPA_SRM_COLOR_MODE_RGB565;
352:                 break;
353:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB24:
354:                 rotate_src = (uint8_t *)frame_.data;
355:                 ppa_color_mode = PPA_SRM_COLOR_MODE_RGB888;
356:                 break;
357:             case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUYV:
358:             {
359:                 ESP_LOGW(TAG, "YUYV format is not supported for PPA rotation, using software conversion to RGB888");
360:                 rotate_src = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(frame_.width * frame_.height * 3,
361:                                                          MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
362:                 if (rotate_src == nullptr)
363:                 {
364:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate memory for rotate image");
365:                     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
366:                     {
367:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
368:                     }
369:                     return false;
370:                 }
371:                 esp_imgfx_color_convert_cfg_t convert_cfg = {
372:                     .in_res = {.width = static_cast<int16_t>(frame_.width),
373:                                .height = static_cast<int16_t>(frame_.height)},
374:                     .in_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_YUYV,
375:                     .out_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_RGB888,
376:                 };
377:                 esp_imgfx_color_convert_handle_t convert_handle = nullptr;
378:                 esp_imgfx_err_t err = esp_imgfx_color_convert_open(&convert_cfg, &convert_handle);
379:                 if (err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK || convert_handle == nullptr)
380:                 {
381:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_color_convert_open failed");
382:                     heap_caps_free(rotate_src);
383:                     rotate_src = nullptr;
384:                     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
385:                     {
386:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
387:                     }
388:                     return false;
389:                 }
390:                 esp_imgfx_data_t convert_input_data = {
391:                     .data = frame_.data,
392:                     .data_len = frame_.len,
393:                 };
394:                 esp_imgfx_data_t convert_output_data = {
395:                     .data = rotate_src,
396:                     .data_len = static_cast<uint32_t>(frame_.width * frame_.height * 3),
397:                 };
398:                 err = esp_imgfx_color_convert_process(convert_handle, &convert_input_data, &convert_output_data);
399:                 if (err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK)
400:                 {
401:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_color_convert_process failed");
402:                     heap_caps_free(rotate_src);
403:                     rotate_src = nullptr;
404:                     esp_imgfx_color_convert_close(convert_handle);
405:                     convert_handle = nullptr;
406:                     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
407:                     {
408:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
409:                     }
410:                     return false;
411:                 }
412:                 esp_imgfx_color_convert_close(convert_handle);
413:                 convert_handle = nullptr;
414:                 ppa_color_mode = PPA_SRM_COLOR_MODE_RGB888;
415:                 heap_caps_free(frame_.data);
416:                 frame_.data = rotate_src;
417:                 frame_.len = frame_.width * frame_.height * 3;
418:                 break;
419:             }
420:             default:
421:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "unsupported sensor format for PPA rotation: 0x%08lx", sensor_format_);
422:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
423:                 {
424:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
425:                 }
426:                 return false;
427:             }
428: 
429:             uint8_t *rotate_dst = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(
430:                 frame_.width * frame_.height * 2, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT | MALLOC_CAP_CACHE_ALIGNED);
431:             if (rotate_dst == nullptr)
432:             {
433:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate memory for rotate image");
434:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
435:                 {
436:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
437:                 }
438:                 return false;
439:             }
440: 
441:             ppa_client_handle_t ppa_client = nullptr;
442:             ppa_client_config_t client_cfg = {
443:                 .oper_type = PPA_OPERATION_SRM,
444:                 .max_pending_trans_num = 1,
445:             };
446:             esp_err_t err = ppa_register_client(&client_cfg, &ppa_client);
447:             if (err != ESP_OK || ppa_client == nullptr)
448:             {
449:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "ppa_register_client failed: %d", (int)err);
450:                 heap_caps_free(rotate_dst);
451:                 rotate_dst = nullptr;
452:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
453:                 {
454:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
455:                 }
456:                 return false;
457:             }
458: 
459:             ppa_srm_rotation_angle_t ppa_angle = IMAGE_ROTATION_ANGLE;
460: 
461:             ppa_srm_oper_config_t srm_cfg = {};
462:             srm_cfg.in.buffer = (void *)rotate_src;
463:             srm_cfg.in.pic_w = sensor_width_;
464:             srm_cfg.in.pic_h = sensor_height_;
465:             srm_cfg.in.block_w = sensor_width_;
466:             srm_cfg.in.block_h = sensor_height_;
467:             srm_cfg.in.block_offset_x = 0;
468:             srm_cfg.in.block_offset_y = 0;
469:             srm_cfg.in.srm_cm = ppa_color_mode;
470: 
471:             srm_cfg.out.buffer = (void *)rotate_dst;
472:             srm_cfg.out.buffer_size = frame_.len;
473:             srm_cfg.out.pic_w = frame_.width;
474:             srm_cfg.out.pic_h = frame_.height;
475:             srm_cfg.out.block_offset_x = 0;
476:             srm_cfg.out.block_offset_y = 0;
477:             srm_cfg.out.srm_cm = PPA_SRM_COLOR_MODE_RGB565;
478: 
479:             // 等比例缩放 1.0
480:             srm_cfg.scale_x = 1.0f;
481:             srm_cfg.scale_y = 1.0f;
482:             srm_cfg.rotation_angle = ppa_angle;
483:             srm_cfg.mode = PPA_TRANS_MODE_BLOCKING;
484:             srm_cfg.user_data = nullptr;
485: 
486:             err = ppa_do_scale_rotate_mirror(ppa_client, &srm_cfg);
487:             if (err != ESP_OK)
488:             {
489:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "ppa_do_scale_rotate_mirror failed: %d", (int)err);
490:                 heap_caps_free(rotate_dst);
491:                 rotate_dst = nullptr;
492:                 (void)ppa_unregister_client(ppa_client);
493:                 if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
494:                 {
495:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Cleanup: VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
496:                 }
497:                 return false;
498:             }
499: 
500:             (void)ppa_unregister_client(ppa_client);
501: 
502:             frame_.data = rotate_dst;
503:             frame_.len = frame_.width * frame_.height * 2;
504:             frame_.format = V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565;
505:             heap_caps_free(rotate_src);
506:             rotate_src = nullptr;
507: #endif // CONFIG_SOC_PPA_SUPPORTED
508: #endif // CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_ROTATE_CAMERA_IMAGE
509:         }
510: 
511:         if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
512:         {
513:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
514:         }
515:     }
516: 
517:     // 显示预览图片
518:     auto display = dynamic_cast<LvglDisplay *>(Board::GetInstance().GetDisplay());
519:     if (display != nullptr)
520:     {
521:         if (!frame_.data)
522:         {
523:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "frame.data is null");
524:             return false;
525:         }
526:         uint16_t w = frame_.width;
527:         uint16_t h = frame_.height;
528:         size_t lvgl_image_size = frame_.len;
529:         size_t stride = ((w * 2) + 3) & ~3; // 4字节对齐
530:         lv_color_format_t color_format = LV_COLOR_FORMAT_RGB565;
531:         uint8_t *data = nullptr;
532: 
533:         switch (frame_.format)
534:         {
535:         // LVGL 显示 YUV 系的图像似乎都有问题，暂时转换为 RGB565 显示
536:         case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUYV:
537:         case V4L2_PIX_FMT_YUV420:
538:         case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB24:
539:         {
540:             color_format = LV_COLOR_FORMAT_RGB565;
541:             data = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(w * h * 2, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
542:             if (data == nullptr)
543:             {
544:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate memory for preview image");
545:                 return false;
546:             }
547:             esp_imgfx_color_convert_cfg_t convert_cfg = {
548:                 .in_res = {.width = static_cast<int16_t>(frame_.width),
549:                            .height = static_cast<int16_t>(frame_.height)},
550:                 .in_pixel_fmt = static_cast<esp_imgfx_pixel_fmt_t>(frame_.format),
551:                 .out_pixel_fmt = ESP_IMGFX_PIXEL_FMT_RGB565_LE,
552:                 .color_space_std = ESP_IMGFX_COLOR_SPACE_STD_BT601,
553:             };
554:             esp_imgfx_color_convert_handle_t convert_handle = nullptr;
555:             esp_imgfx_err_t err = esp_imgfx_color_convert_open(&convert_cfg, &convert_handle);
556:             if (err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK || convert_handle == nullptr)
557:             {
558:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_color_convert_open failed");
559:                 heap_caps_free(data);
560:                 data = nullptr;
561:                 return false;
562:             }
563:             esp_imgfx_data_t convert_input_data = {
564:                 .data = frame_.data,
565:                 .data_len = frame_.len,
566:             };
567:             esp_imgfx_data_t convert_output_data = {
568:                 .data = data,
569:                 .data_len = static_cast<uint32_t>(w * h * 2),
570:             };
571:             err = esp_imgfx_color_convert_process(convert_handle, &convert_input_data, &convert_output_data);
572:             if (err != ESP_IMGFX_ERR_OK)
573:             {
574:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_imgfx_color_convert_process failed");
575:                 heap_caps_free(data);
576:                 data = nullptr;
577:                 esp_imgfx_color_convert_close(convert_handle);
578:                 convert_handle = nullptr;
579:                 return false;
580:             }
581:             esp_imgfx_color_convert_close(convert_handle);
582:             convert_handle = nullptr;
583:             lvgl_image_size = w * h * 2;
584:             break;
585:         }
586: 
587:         case V4L2_PIX_FMT_RGB565:
588:             data = (uint8_t *)heap_caps_malloc(w * h * 2, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
589:             if (data == nullptr)
590:             {
591:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate memory for preview image");
592:                 return false;
593:             }
594:             memcpy(data, frame_.data, frame_.len);
595:             lvgl_image_size = frame_.len; // fallthrough 时兼顾 YUYV 与 RGB565
596:             break;
597: 
598: #ifdef CONFIG_XIAOZHI_CAMERA_ALLOW_JPEG_INPUT
599:         case V4L2_PIX_FMT_JPEG:
600:         {
601:             uint8_t *out_data = nullptr; // out data is allocated by jpeg_to_image
602:             size_t out_len = 0;
603:             size_t out_width = 0;
604:             size_t out_height = 0;
605:             size_t out_stride = 0;
606: 
607:             esp_err_t ret =
608:                 jpeg_to_image(frame_.data, frame_.len, &out_data, &out_len, &out_width, &out_height, &out_stride);
609:             if (ret != ESP_OK)
610:             {
611:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decode JPEG image: %d (%s)", (int)ret, esp_err_to_name(ret));
612:                 if (out_data)
613:                 {
614:                     heap_caps_free(out_data);
615:                     out_data = nullptr;
616:                 }
617:                 return false;
618:             }
619: 
620:             data = out_data;
621:             w = out_width;
622:             h = out_height;
623:             lvgl_image_size = out_len;
624:             stride = out_stride;
625:             break;
626:         }
627: #endif
628:         default:
629:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "unsupported frame format: 0x%08lx", frame_.format);
630:             return false;
631:         }
632: 
633:         auto image = std::make_unique<LvglAllocatedImage>(data, lvgl_image_size, w, h, stride, color_format);
634:         display->SetPreviewImage(std::move(image));
635:     }
636:     return true;
637: }

639: bool Esp32Camera::SetHMirror(bool enabled)
640: {
641:     if (video_fd_ < 0)
642:         return false;
643:     struct v4l2_ext_controls ctrls = {};
644:     struct v4l2_ext_control ctrl = {};
645:     ctrl.id = V4L2_CID_HFLIP;
646:     ctrl.value = enabled ? 1 : 0;
647:     ctrls.ctrl_class = V4L2_CTRL_CLASS_USER;
648:     ctrls.count = 1;
649:     ctrls.controls = &ctrl;
650:     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_S_EXT_CTRLS, &ctrls) != 0)
651:     {
652:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "set HFLIP failed");
653:         return false;
654:     }
655:     return true;
656: }
657: 
658: bool Esp32Camera::SetVFlip(bool enabled)
659: {
660:     if (video_fd_ < 0)
661:         return false;
662:     struct v4l2_ext_controls ctrls = {};
663:     struct v4l2_ext_control ctrl = {};
664:     ctrl.id = V4L2_CID_VFLIP;
665:     ctrl.value = enabled ? 1 : 0;
666:     ctrls.ctrl_class = V4L2_CTRL_CLASS_USER;
667:     ctrls.count = 1;
668:     ctrls.controls = &ctrl;
669:     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_S_EXT_CTRLS, &ctrls) != 0)
670:     {
671:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "set VFLIP failed");
672:         return false;
673:     }
674:     return true;
675: }
