677: /**
678:  * @brief 将摄像头捕获的图像发送到远程服务器进行AI分析和解释
679:  *
680:  * 该函数将当前摄像头缓冲区中的图像编码为JPEG格式，并通过HTTP POST请求
681:  * 以multipart/form-data的形式发送到指定的解释服务器。服务器将根据提供的
682:  * 问题对图像进行AI分析并返回结果。
683:  *
684:  * 实现特点：
685:  * - 使用独立线程编码JPEG，与主线程分离
686:  * - 采用分块传输编码(chunked transfer encoding)优化内存使用
687:  * - 通过队列机制实现编码线程和发送线程的数据同步
688:  * - 支持设备ID、客户端ID和认证令牌的HTTP头部配置
689:  *
690:  * @param question 要向AI提出的关于图像的问题，将作为表单字段发送
691:  * @return std::string 服务器返回的JSON格式响应字符串
692:  *         成功时包含AI分析结果，失败时包含错误信息
693:  *         格式示例：{"success": true, "result": "分析结果"}
694:  *                  {"success": false, "message": "错误信息"}
695:  *
696:  * @note 调用此函数前必须先调用SetExplainUrl()设置服务器URL
697:  * @note 函数会等待之前的编码线程完成后再开始新的处理
698:  * @warning 如果摄像头缓冲区为空或网络连接失败，将返回错误信息
699:  */
700: std::string Esp32Camera::Explain(const std::string &question)
701: {
702:     if (explain_url_.empty())
703:     {
704:         throw std::runtime_error("Image explain URL or token is not set");
705:     }
706: 
707:     // 创建局部的 JPEG 队列, 40 entries is about to store 512 * 40 = 20480 bytes of JPEG data
708:     QueueHandle_t jpeg_queue = xQueueCreate(40, sizeof(JpegChunk));
709:     if (jpeg_queue == nullptr)
710:     {
711:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create JPEG queue");
712:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create JPEG queue");
713:     }
714: 
715:     // We spawn a thread to encode the image to JPEG using optimized encoder (cost about 500ms and 8KB SRAM)
716:     encoder_thread_ = std::thread([this, jpeg_queue]()
717:                                   {
718:         uint16_t w = frame_.width ? frame_.width : 320;
719:         uint16_t h = frame_.height ? frame_.height : 240;
720:         v4l2_pix_fmt_t enc_fmt = frame_.format;
721:         bool ok = image_to_jpeg_cb(
722:             frame_.data, frame_.len, w, h, enc_fmt, 80,
723:             [](void* arg, size_t index, const void* data, size_t len) -> size_t {
724:                 auto jpeg_queue = static_cast<QueueHandle_t>(arg);
725:                 JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = len};
726:                 if (index == 0 && data != nullptr && len > 0) {
727:                     chunk.data = (uint8_t*)heap_caps_aligned_alloc(16, len, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
728:                     if (chunk.data == nullptr) {
729:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for JPEG chunk", len);
730:                         chunk.len = 0;
731:                     } else {
732:                         memcpy(chunk.data, data, len);
733:                     }
734:                 } else {
735:                     chunk.len = 0;  // Sentinel or error
736:                 }
737:                 xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
738:                 return len;
739:             },
740:             jpeg_queue);
741: 
742:         if (!ok) {
743:             JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = 0};
744:             xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
745:         } });
746: 
747:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
748:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(3);
749:     // 构造multipart/form-data请求体
750:     std::string boundary = "----ESP32_CAMERA_BOUNDARY";
751: 
752:     // 配置HTTP客户端，使用分块传输编码
753:     http->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
754:     http->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
755:     if (!explain_token_.empty())
756:     {
757:         http->SetHeader("Authorization", "Bearer " + explain_token_);
758:     }
759:     http->SetHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);
760:     http->SetHeader("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked");
761:     if (!http->Open("POST", explain_url_))
762:     {
763:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to explain URL");
764:         // Clear the queue
765:         encoder_thread_.join();
766:         JpegChunk chunk;
767:         while (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) == pdPASS)
768:         {
769:             if (chunk.data != nullptr)
770:             {
771:                 heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
772:             }
773:             else
774:             {
775:                 break;
776:             }
777:         }
778:         vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
779:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to explain URL");
780:     }
781: 
782:     {
783:         // 第一块：question字段
784:         std::string question_field;
785:         question_field += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
786:         question_field += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"question\"\r\n";
787:         question_field += "\r\n";
788:         question_field += question + "\r\n";
789:         http->Write(question_field.c_str(), question_field.size());
790:     }
791:     {
792:         // 第二块：文件字段头部
793:         std::string file_header;
794:         file_header += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
795:         file_header += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"camera.jpg\"\r\n";
796:         file_header += "Content-Type: image/jpeg\r\n";
797:         file_header += "\r\n";
798:         http->Write(file_header.c_str(), file_header.size());
799:     }
800: 
801:     // 第三块：JPEG数据
802:     size_t total_sent = 0;
803:     bool saw_terminator = false;
804:     while (true)
805:     {
806:         JpegChunk chunk;
807:         if (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) != pdPASS)
808:         {
809:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive JPEG chunk");
810:             break;
811:         }
812:         if (chunk.data == nullptr)
813:         {
814:             saw_terminator = true;
815:             break; // The last chunk
816:         }
817:         http->Write((const char *)chunk.data, chunk.len);
818:         total_sent += chunk.len;
819:         heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
820:     }
821:     // Wait for the encoder thread to finish
822:     encoder_thread_.join();
823:     // 清理队列
824:     vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
825: 
826:     if (!saw_terminator || total_sent == 0)
827:     {
828:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "JPEG encoder failed or produced empty output");
829:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to encode image to JPEG");
830:     }
831: 
832:     {
833:         // 第四块：multipart尾部
834:         std::string multipart_footer;
835:         multipart_footer += "\r\n--" + boundary + "--\r\n";
836:         http->Write(multipart_footer.c_str(), multipart_footer.size());
837:     }
838:     // 结束块
839:     http->Write("", 0);
840: 
841:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
842:     {
843:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to upload photo, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
844:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to upload photo");
845:     }
846: 
847:     std::string result = http->ReadAll();
848:     http->Close();
849: 
850:     // Get remain task stack size
851:     size_t remain_stack_size = uxTaskGetStackHighWaterMark(nullptr);
852:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Explain image size=%d bytes, compressed size=%d, remain stack size=%d, question=%s\n%s",
853:              (int)frame_.len, (int)total_sent, (int)remain_stack_size, question.c_str(), result.c_str());
854:     return result;
855: }
856: 
</file>
The `Esp32Camera` constructor initializes the camera hardware based on the provided configuration, selects the appropriate video device interface (such as MIPI CSI, DVP, JPEG, SPI, or USB UVC), opens the device, and validates its capabilities using standard V4L2 IOCTL commands. It also retrieves and sets the initial pixel format and resolution for video capture. If any step fails, it logs an error and cleans up resources.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code attempts to implement a camera initialization routine but deviates significantly from the ground truth in both logic and functionality.

Semantic Correctness:
- The most critical flaw is that the generated code completely ignores `esp_video_init()` which is the primary entry point for initializing the video subsystem in ESP-IDF-based systems.
- It uses a brute-force approach of iterating through `/dev/video0` to `/dev/video9` to find a device, whereas the ground truth intelligently selects the correct device name based on compile-time configuration flags (e.g., MIPI CSI, DVP, USB UVC).
- Format selection is oversimplified; the ground truth enumerates supported formats using `VIDIOC_ENUM_FMT` and selects the best one via a ranking system, while the generated code naively tries to set a fixed format provided in the config.
- Missing handling of rotation (`CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_ROTATE_CAMERA_IMAGE`) and hardware JPEG encoding configurations.
- Lacks proper error logging with errno interpretation and debug logs.
- Completely omits ISP-specific initialization task used when `CONFIG_ESP_VIDEO_ENABLE_ISP_VIDEO_DEVICE` is defined.

Structure Similarity:
- Basic V4L2 operations like `VIDIOC_QUERYCAP`, `VIDIOC_REQBUFS`, `VIDIOC_STREAMON` are present but lack proper sequencing and conditional checks found in the original.
- Control flow differs substantially — no use of preprocessor directives to conditionally enable different video interfaces.
- No equivalent of the complex pixel format enumeration and selection mechanism.
- Memory mapping and buffer queueing loops have basic similarity but miss cleanup procedures and robustness features.

Functional Completeness:
- Misses essential steps such as calling `esp_video_init()`.
- Does not handle multiple camera interface types (MIPI CSI, DVP, SPI, USB UVC, HW JPEG).
- Ignores compile-time feature flags that affect behavior (rotation, debug mode, ISP init).
- Buffer allocation count is hardcoded instead of being device-dependent.
- Lacks sophisticated pixel format negotiation and selection logic.
- Initialization delay for ISP tuning (when enabled) is missing entirely.
- Debug logging and available device listing functionality absent.

Overall, the generated code provides a minimalistic and generic V4L2 setup routine but fails to correctly implement the specific requirements and advanced features expected for an ESP32 camera driver integration.

results.description: The `Esp32Camera::Capture()` function captures a single video frame from the camera hardware and stores it in memory, handling buffer management and optional format conversion. It ensures proper synchronization by waiting for any previous encoding tasks to complete and manages memory allocation for the captured frame, particularly when storing a copy in PSRAM. The function supports multiple pixel formats and includes error handling for buffer operations and memory allocation failures.

315:         struct v4l2_buffer buf = {};
316:         buf.type = V4L2_BUF_TYPE_VIDEO_CAPTURE;
317:         buf.memory = V4L2_MEMORY_MMAP;
318:         buf.index = i;
319:         if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QUERYBUF, &buf) != 0)
320:         {
321:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_QUERYBUF failed");
322:             close(video_fd_);
323:             video_fd_ = -1;
324:             sensor_format_ = 0;
325:             return;
326:         }
327:         void *start = mmap(NULL, buf.length, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, video_fd_, buf.m.offset);
328:         if (start == MAP_FAILED)
329:         {
330:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "mmap failed");
331:             close(video_fd_);
332:             video_fd_ = -1;
333:             sensor_format_ = 0;
334:             return;
335:         }
336:         mmap_buffers_[i].start = start;
337:         mmap_buffers_[i].length = buf.length;
338: 
339:         if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
340:         {
341:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_QBUF failed");
342:             close(video_fd_);
343:             video_fd_ = -1;
344:             sensor_format_ = 0;
345:             return;
346:         }
347:     }
348: 
349:     int type = V4L2_BUF_TYPE_VIDEO_CAPTURE;
350:     if (ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_STREAMON, &type) != 0)
351:     {
352:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_STREAMON failed");
353:         close(video_fd_);
354:         video_fd_ = -1;
355:         sensor_format_ = 0;
356:         return;
357:     }
358: 
359: #ifdef CONFIG_ESP_VIDEO_ENABLE_ISP_VIDEO_DEVICE
360:     // 当启用 ISP 时，ISP 需要一些照片来初始化参数，因此开启后后台拍摄5s照片并丢弃
361:     xTaskCreate(
362:         [](void *arg)
363:         {
364:             Esp32Camera *self = static_cast<Esp32Camera *>(arg);
365:             uint16_t capture_count = 0;
366:             TickType_t start = xTaskGetTickCount();
367:             TickType_t duration = 5000 / portTICK_PERIOD_MS; // 5s
368:             while ((xTaskGetTickCount() - start) < duration)
369:             {
370:                 struct v4l2_buffer buf = {};
371:                 buf.type = V4L2_BUF_TYPE_VIDEO_CAPTURE;
372:                 buf.memory = V4L2_MEMORY_MMAP;
373:                 if (ioctl(self->video_fd_, VIDIOC_DQBUF, &buf) != 0)
374:                 {
375:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_DQBUF failed during init");
376:                     vTaskDelay(10 / portTICK_PERIOD_MS);
377:                     continue;
378:                 }
379:                 if (ioctl(self->video_fd_, VIDIOC_QBUF, &buf) != 0)
380:                 {
381:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "VIDIOC_QBUF failed during init");
382:                 }
383:                 capture_count++;
384:             }
385:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Camera init success, captured %d frames in %dms", capture_count,
386:                      (xTaskGetTickCount() - start) * portTICK_PERIOD_MS);
387:             self->streaming_on_ = true;
388:             vTaskDelete(NULL);
389:         },
390:         "CameraInitTask", 4096, this, 5, nullptr);
391: #else
392:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Camera init success");
393:     streaming_on_ = true;
394: #endif // CONFIG_ESP_VIDEO_ENABLE_ISP_VIDEO_DEVICE
395: }

397: Esp32Camera::~Esp32Camera()
398: {
399:     if (streaming_on_ && video_fd_ >= 0)
400:     {
401:         int type = V4L2_BUF_TYPE_VIDEO_CAPTURE;
402:         ioctl(video_fd_, VIDIOC_STREAMOFF, &type);
403:     }
404:     for (auto &b : mmap_buffers_)
405:     {
406:         if (b.start && b.length)
407:         {
408:             munmap(b.start, b.length);
409:         }
410:     }
411:     if (video_fd_ >= 0)
412:     {
413:         close(video_fd_);
414:         video_fd_ = -1;
415:     }
416:     sensor_format_ = 0;
417:     esp_video_deinit();
418: }

468: /**
469:  * @brief 将摄像头捕获的图像发送到远程服务器进行AI分析和解释
470:  *
471:  * 该函数将当前摄像头缓冲区中的图像编码为JPEG格式，并通过HTTP POST请求
472:  * 以multipart/form-data的形式发送到指定的解释服务器。服务器将根据提供的
473:  * 问题对图像进行AI分析并返回结果。
474:  *
475:  * 实现特点：
476:  * - 使用独立线程编码JPEG，与主线程分离
477:  * - 采用分块传输编码(chunked transfer encoding)优化内存使用
478:  * - 通过队列机制实现编码线程和发送线程的数据同步
479:  * - 支持设备ID、客户端ID和认证令牌的HTTP头部配置
480:  *
481:  * @param question 要向AI提出的关于图像的问题，将作为表单字段发送
482:  * @return std::string 服务器返回的JSON格式响应字符串
483:  *         成功时包含AI分析结果，失败时包含错误信息
484:  *         格式示例：{"success": true, "result": "分析结果"}
485:  *                  {"success": false, "message": "错误信息"}
486:  *
487:  * @note 调用此函数前必须先调用SetExplainUrl()设置服务器URL
488:  * @note 函数会等待之前的编码线程完成后再开始新的处理
489:  * @warning 如果摄像头缓冲区为空或网络连接失败，将返回错误信息
490:  */
491: std::string Esp32Camera::Explain(const std::string &question)
492: {
493:     if (explain_url_.empty())
494:     {
495:         throw std::runtime_error("Image explain URL or token is not set");
496:     }
497: 
498:     // 创建局部的 JPEG 队列, 40 entries is about to store 512 * 40 = 20480 bytes of JPEG data
499:     QueueHandle_t jpeg_queue = xQueueCreate(40, sizeof(JpegChunk));
500:     if (jpeg_queue == nullptr)
501:     {
502:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create JPEG queue");
503:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create JPEG queue");
504:     }
505: 
506:     // We spawn a thread to encode the image to JPEG using optimized encoder (cost about 500ms and 8KB SRAM)
507:     encoder_thread_ = std::thread([this, jpeg_queue]()
508:                                   {
509:         uint16_t w = frame_.width ? frame_.width : 320;
510:         uint16_t h = frame_.height ? frame_.height : 240;
511:         v4l2_pix_fmt_t enc_fmt = frame_.format;
512:         bool ok = image_to_jpeg_cb(
513:             frame_.data, frame_.len, w, h, enc_fmt, 80,
514:             [](void* arg, size_t index, const void* data, size_t len) -> size_t {
515:                 auto jpeg_queue = static_cast<QueueHandle_t>(arg);
516:                 JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = len};
517:                 if (index == 0 && data != nullptr && len > 0) {
518:                     chunk.data = (uint8_t*)heap_caps_aligned_alloc(16, len, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
519:                     if (chunk.data == nullptr) {
520:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for JPEG chunk", len);
521:                         chunk.len = 0;
522:                     } else {
523:                         memcpy(chunk.data, data, len);
524:                     }
525:                 } else {
526:                     chunk.len = 0;  // Sentinel or error
527:                 }
528:                 xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
529:                 return len;
530:             },
531:             jpeg_queue);
532: 
533:         if (!ok) {
534:             JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = 0};
535:             xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
536:         } });
537: 
538:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
539:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(3);
540:     // 构造multipart/form-data请求体
541:     std::string boundary = "----ESP32_CAMERA_BOUNDARY";
542: 
543:     // 配置HTTP客户端，使用分块传输编码
544:     http->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
545:     http->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
546:     if (!explain_token_.empty())
547:     {
548:         http->SetHeader("Authorization", "Bearer " + explain_token_);
549:     }
550:     http->SetHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);
551:     http->SetHeader("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked");
552:     if (!http->Open("POST", explain_url_))
553:     {
554:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to explain URL");
555:         // Clear the queue
556:         encoder_thread_.join();
557:         JpegChunk chunk;
558:         while (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) == pdPASS)
559:         {
560:             if (chunk.data != nullptr)
561:             {
562:                 heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
563:             }
564:             else
565:             {
566:                 break;
567:             }
568:         }
569:         vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
570:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to explain URL");
571:     }
572: 
573:     {
574:         // 第一块：question字段
575:         std::string question_field;
576:         question_field += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
577:         question_field += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"question\"\r\n";
578:         question_field += "\r\n";
579:         question_field += question + "\r\n";
580:         http->Write(question_field.c_str(), question_field.size());
581:     }
582:     {
583:         // 第二块：文件字段头部
584:         std::string file_header;
585:         file_header += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
586:         file_header += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"camera.jpg\"\r\n";
587:         file_header += "Content-Type: image/jpeg\r\n";
588:         file_header += "\r\n";
589:         http->Write(file_header.c_str(), file_header.size());
590:     }
591: 
592:     // 第三块：JPEG数据
593:     size_t total_sent = 0;
594:     bool saw_terminator = false;
595:     while (true)
596:     {
597:         JpegChunk chunk;
598:         if (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) != pdPASS)
599:         {
600:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive JPEG chunk");
601:             break;
602:         }
603:         if (chunk.data == nullptr)
604:         {
605:             saw_terminator = true;
606:             break; // The last chunk
607:         }
608:         http->Write((const char *)chunk.data, chunk.len);
609:         total_sent += chunk.len;
610:         heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
611:     }
612:     // Wait for the encoder thread to finish
613:     encoder_thread_.join();
614:     // 清理队列
615:     vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
616: 
617:     if (!saw_terminator || total_sent == 0)
618:     {
619:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "JPEG encoder failed or produced empty output");
620:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to encode image to JPEG");
621:     }
622: 
623:     {
624:         // 第四块：multipart尾部
625:         std::string multipart_footer;
626:         multipart_footer += "\r\n--" + boundary + "--\r\n";
627:         http->Write(multipart_footer.c_str(), multipart_footer.size());
628:     }
629:     // 结束块
630:     http->Write("", 0);
631: 
632:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
633:     {
634:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to upload photo, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
635:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to upload photo");
636:     }
637: 
638:     std::string result = http->ReadAll();
639:     http->Close();
640: 
641:     // Get remain task stack size
642:     size_t remain_stack_size = uxTaskGetStackHighWaterMark(nullptr);
643:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Explain image size=%d bytes, compressed size=%d, remain stack size=%d, question=%s\n%s",
644:              (int)frame_.len, (int)total_sent, (int)remain_stack_size, question.c_str(), result.c_str());
645:     return result;
646: }
647: 
</file>
The `Esp32Camera::Capture()` function captures a single video frame from the camera hardware and stores it in memory, handling buffer management and optional format conversion. It ensures proper synchronization by waiting for any previous encoding tasks to complete and manages memory allocation for the captured frame, particularly when storing a copy in PSRAM. The function supports multiple pixel formats and includes error handling for buffer operations and memory allocation failures.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code captures the basic idea of dequeuing a buffer, copying or processing the frame data, and re-queuing the buffer.
- However, it misses critical logic such as the triple-buffer loop (for i=0; i<3), which appears essential for proper V4L2 buffer management in the ground truth.
- It does not replicate complex format handling (e.g., endianness swap, pixel format conversions like YUV422P to YUYV, RGB565X handling).
- Rotation support is simplified and lacks detailed configuration and multi-path handling (software vs hardware PPA paths), especially missing SOC-specific branches.
- Preview display integration via LvglDisplay is completely absent in the generated version.

Structure Similarity:
- Basic function signature matches (`bool Esp32Camera::Capture()`).
- Uses similar ioctl calls for DQBUF/QBUF but omits looping over multiple buffers.
- Some conditional compilation blocks are mirrored (e.g., CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_ROTATE_CAMERA_IMAGE) but with incorrect or incomplete implementation.
- Missing use of many helper functions, structs, logging macros, and detailed error recovery paths that define the structural integrity of the original.
- Memory allocation pattern differs significantly — uses `frame_.allocated` flag instead of freeing based on existing data pointer presence.

Functional Completeness:
- Lacks full pixel format support including GREY, RGB24, JPEG input (ifdef'd), and special handling for formats like YUV422P.
- Endian-swapping functionality under `CONFIG_XIAOZHI_ENABLE_CAMERA_ENDIANNESS_SWAP` is entirely missing.
- Complex rotation logic using either esp_imgfx or PPA (with SRM and color mode handling) is oversimplified and likely non-functional.
- No integration with LVGL display system for showing preview images.
- Error handling is less robust — fewer cleanup steps after failures.
- Frame metadata (width/height tracking) and format assignment are inconsistent or assumed rather than derived from actual processing.

results.description: The `Esp32Camera::Explain` function captures an image from the camera, encodes it into JPEG format, and uploads it to a remote server via HTTP POST request for analysis or explanation. It handles JPEG encoding in a separate thread and manages memory allocation for image chunks. The function also sets appropriate headers including device identification and authentication tokens before sending the data.

468: /**
469:  * @brief 将摄像头捕获的图像发送到远程服务器进行AI分析和解释
470:  *
471:  * 该函数将当前摄像头缓冲区中的图像编码为JPEG格式，并通过HTTP POST请求
472:  * 以multipart/form-data的形式发送到指定的解释服务器。服务器将根据提供的
473:  * 问题对图像进行AI分析并返回结果。
474:  *
475:  * 实现特点：
476:  * - 使用独立线程编码JPEG，与主线程分离
477:  * - 采用分块传输编码(chunked transfer encoding)优化内存使用
478:  * - 通过队列机制实现编码线程和发送线程的数据同步
479:  * - 支持设备ID、客户端ID和认证令牌的HTTP头部配置
480:  *
481:  * @param question 要向AI提出的关于图像的问题，将作为表单字段发送
482:  * @return std::string 服务器返回的JSON格式响应字符串
483:  *         成功时包含AI分析结果，失败时包含错误信息
484:  *         格式示例：{"success": true, "result": "分析结果"}
485:  *                  {"success": false, "message": "错误信息"}
486:  *
487:  * @note 调用此函数前必须先调用SetExplainUrl()设置服务器URL
488:  * @note 函数会等待之前的编码线程完成后再开始新的处理
489:  * @warning 如果摄像头缓冲区为空或网络连接失败，将返回错误信息
490:  */
491: std::string Esp32Camera::Explain(const std::string &question)
492: {
493:     if (explain_url_.empty())
494:     {
495:         throw std::runtime_error("Image explain URL or token is not set");
496:     }
497: 
498:     // 创建局部的 JPEG 队列, 40 entries is about to store 512 * 40 = 20480 bytes of JPEG data
499:     QueueHandle_t jpeg_queue = xQueueCreate(40, sizeof(JpegChunk));
500:     if (jpeg_queue == nullptr)
501:     {
502:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create JPEG queue");
503:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to create JPEG queue");
504:     }
505: 
506:     // We spawn a thread to encode the image to JPEG using optimized encoder (cost about 500ms and 8KB SRAM)
507:     encoder_thread_ = std::thread([this, jpeg_queue]()
508:                                   {
509:         uint16_t w = frame_.width ? frame_.width : 320;
510:         uint16_t h = frame_.height ? frame_.height : 240;
511:         v4l2_pix_fmt_t enc_fmt = frame_.format;
512:         bool ok = image_to_jpeg_cb(
513:             frame_.data, frame_.len, w, h, enc_fmt, 80,
514:             [](void* arg, size_t index, const void* data, size_t len) -> size_t {
515:                 auto jpeg_queue = static_cast<QueueHandle_t>(arg);
516:                 JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = len};
517:                 if (index == 0 && data != nullptr && len > 0) {
518:                     chunk.data = (uint8_t*)heap_caps_aligned_alloc(16, len, MALLOC_CAP_SPIRAM | MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
519:                     if (chunk.data == nullptr) {
520:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes for JPEG chunk", len);
521:                         chunk.len = 0;
522:                     } else {
523:                         memcpy(chunk.data, data, len);
524:                     }
525:                 } else {
526:                     chunk.len = 0;  // Sentinel or error
527:                 }
528:                 xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
529:                 return len;
530:             },
531:             jpeg_queue);
532: 
533:         if (!ok) {
534:             JpegChunk chunk = {.data = nullptr, .len = 0};
535:             xQueueSend(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY);
536:         } });
537: 
538:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
539:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(3);
540:     // 构造multipart/form-data请求体
541:     std::string boundary = "----ESP32_CAMERA_BOUNDARY";
542: 
543:     // 配置HTTP客户端，使用分块传输编码
544:     http->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
545:     http->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
546:     if (!explain_token_.empty())
547:     {
548:         http->SetHeader("Authorization", "Bearer " + explain_token_);
549:     }
550:     http->SetHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);
551:     http->SetHeader("Transfer-Encoding", "chunked");
552:     if (!http->Open("POST", explain_url_))
553:     {
554:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to explain URL");
555:         // Clear the queue
556:         encoder_thread_.join();
557:         JpegChunk chunk;
558:         while (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) == pdPASS)
559:         {
560:             if (chunk.data != nullptr)
561:             {
562:                 heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
563:             }
564:             else
565:             {
566:                 break;
567:             }
568:         }
569:         vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
570:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to explain URL");
571:     }
572: 
573:     {
574:         // 第一块：question字段
575:         std::string question_field;
576:         question_field += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
577:         question_field += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"question\"\r\n";
578:         question_field += "\r\n";
579:         question_field += question + "\r\n";
580:         http->Write(question_field.c_str(), question_field.size());
581:     }
582:     {
583:         // 第二块：文件字段头部
584:         std::string file_header;
585:         file_header += "--" + boundary + "\r\n";
586:         file_header += "Content-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"camera.jpg\"\r\n";
587:         file_header += "Content-Type: image/jpeg\r\n";
588:         file_header += "\r\n";
589:         http->Write(file_header.c_str(), file_header.size());
590:     }
591: 
592:     // 第三块：JPEG数据
593:     size_t total_sent = 0;
594:     bool saw_terminator = false;
595:     while (true)
596:     {
597:         JpegChunk chunk;
598:         if (xQueueReceive(jpeg_queue, &chunk, portMAX_DELAY) != pdPASS)
599:         {
600:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive JPEG chunk");
601:             break;
602:         }
603:         if (chunk.data == nullptr)
604:         {
605:             saw_terminator = true;
606:             break; // The last chunk
607:         }
608:         http->Write((const char *)chunk.data, chunk.len);
609:         total_sent += chunk.len;
610:         heap_caps_free(chunk.data);
611:     }
612:     // Wait for the encoder thread to finish
613:     encoder_thread_.join();
614:     // 清理队列
615:     vQueueDelete(jpeg_queue);
616: 
617:     if (!saw_terminator || total_sent == 0)
618:     {
619:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "JPEG encoder failed or produced empty output");
620:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to encode image to JPEG");
621:     }
622: 
623:     {
624:         // 第四块：multipart尾部
625:         std::string multipart_footer;
626:         multipart_footer += "\r\n--" + boundary + "--\r\n";
627:         http->Write(multipart_footer.c_str(), multipart_footer.size());
628:     }
629:     // 结束块
630:     http->Write("", 0);
631: 
632:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
633:     {
634:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to upload photo, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
635:         throw std::runtime_error("Failed to upload photo");
636:     }
637: 
638:     std::string result = http->ReadAll();
639:     http->Close();
640: 
641:     // Get remain task stack size
642:     size_t remain_stack_size = uxTaskGetStackHighWaterMark(nullptr);
643:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Explain image size=%d bytes, compressed size=%d, remain stack size=%d, question=%s\n%s",
644:              (int)frame_.len, (int)total_sent, (int)remain_stack_size, question.c_str(), result.c_str());
645:     return result;
646: }
647: 
</file>
The `Esp32Camera::Explain` function captures an image from the camera, encodes it into JPEG format, and uploads it to a remote server via HTTP POST request for analysis or explanation. It handles JPEG encoding in a separate thread and manages memory allocation for image chunks. The function also sets appropriate headers including device identification and authentication tokens before sending the data.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both versions implement the same core logic: validating preconditions, capturing an image, encoding it to JPEG in a separate thread, sending it via HTTP multipart request with a question, and returning the server's response.
- The generated code adds extra checks (`streaming_on_`, `video_fd_`, and explicit `Capture()`), which are semantically reasonable but not present in the ground truth. This slightly alters the expected behavior and assumptions about when/where capture occurs.
- Error handling and resource cleanup (queue, memory, threads) are correctly mirrored between both implementations.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is nearly identical: precondition checks → queue creation → encoder thread spawning → HTTP setup → multipart writing → JPEG data consumption → finalization and return.
- Use of lambdas, FreeRTOS queues, threading, and ESP-IDF APIs like `heap_caps_aligned_alloc` and `xQueueSend`/`xQueueReceive` match precisely.
- Minor differences include variable naming (e.g., comments in English vs Chinese), spacing, and formatting —不影响结构一致性.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated version includes additional precondition checks that may improve robustness but aren't found in the reference implementation, potentially altering functional scope.
- It lacks logging of remaining stack size at the end, which was present in the ground truth (`uxTaskGetStackHighWaterMark`).
- Overall coverage of required functionality such as image encoding, HTTP communication, and proper error propagation is maintained.
- Missing minor details like exact log message content or order reduce completeness slightly.

results.description: The `StartNetwork` function initializes the WiFi manager with a predefined SSID prefix and language, sets up a unified event callback to handle various WiFi events (such as scanning, connecting, connected, disconnected, and configuration mode entry/exit), and attempts to establish a WiFi connection or enters configuration mode if needed. It acts as the central handler for all WiFi-related state changes and user interactions within the system.

59: void WifiBoard::TryWifiConnect()
60: {
61:     auto &ssid_manager = SsidManager::GetInstance();
62:     bool have_ssid = !ssid_manager.GetSsidList().empty();
63: 
64:     if (have_ssid)
65:     {
66:         // Start connection attempt with timeout
67:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting WiFi connection attempt");
68:         esp_timer_start_once(connect_timer_, CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC * 1000000ULL);
69:         WifiManager::GetInstance().StartStation();
70:     }
71:     else
72:     {
73:         // No SSID configured, enter config mode
74:         // Wait for the board version to be shown
75:         vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1500));
76:         StartWifiConfigMode();
77:     }
78: }

80: void WifiBoard::OnNetworkEvent(NetworkEvent event, const std::string &data)
81: {
82:     switch (event)
83:     {
84:     case NetworkEvent::Connected:
85:         // Stop timeout timer
86:         esp_timer_stop(connect_timer_);
87: #ifdef CONFIG_USE_ESP_BLUFI_WIFI_PROVISIONING
88:         // make sure blufi resources has been released
89:         Blufi::GetInstance().deinit();
90: #endif
91:         in_config_mode_ = false;
92:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to WiFi: %s", data.c_str());
93:         break;
94:     case NetworkEvent::Scanning:
95:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi scanning");
96:         break;
97:     case NetworkEvent::Connecting:
98:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi connecting to %s", data.c_str());
99:         break;
100:     case NetworkEvent::Disconnected:
101:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "WiFi disconnected");
102:         break;
103:     case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeEnter:
104:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi config mode entered");
105:         in_config_mode_ = true;
106:         break;
107:     case NetworkEvent::WifiConfigModeExit:
108:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "WiFi config mode exited");
109:         in_config_mode_ = false;
110:         // Try to connect with the new credentials
111:         TryWifiConnect();
112:         break;
113:     default:
114:         break;
115:     }
116: 
117:     // Notify external callback if set
118:     if (network_event_callback_)
119:     {
120:         network_event_callback_(event, data);
121:     }
122: }

180: void WifiBoard::EnterWifiConfigMode()
181: {
182:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "EnterWifiConfigMode called");
183:     GetDisplay()->ShowNotification(Lang::Strings::ENTERING_WIFI_CONFIG_MODE);
184: 
185:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
186:     auto state = app.GetDeviceState();
187: 
188:     if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateIdle)
189:     {
190:         // Reset protocol (close audio channel, reset protocol)
191:         Application::GetInstance().ResetProtocol();
192: 
193:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)
194:                     {
195:             auto* board = static_cast<WifiBoard*>(arg);
196: 
197:             // Wait for 1 second to allow speaking to finish gracefully
198:             vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
199: 
200:             // Stop any ongoing connection attempt
201:             esp_timer_stop(board->connect_timer_);
202:             WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation();
203: 
204:             // Enter config mode
205:             board->StartWifiConfigMode();
206: 
207:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "wifi_cfg_delay", 4096, this, 2, NULL);
208:         return;
209:     }
210: 
211:     if (state != kDeviceStateStarting)
212:     {
213:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "EnterWifiConfigMode called but device state is not starting or speaking, device state: %d", state);
214:         return;
215:     }
216: 
217:     // Stop any ongoing connection attempt
218:     esp_timer_stop(connect_timer_);
219:     WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation();
220: 
221:     StartWifiConfigMode();
222: }

297: std::string WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson()
298: {
299:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
300:     auto root = cJSON_CreateObject();
301: 
302:     // Audio speaker
303:     auto audio_speaker = cJSON_CreateObject();
304:     if (auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec())
305:     {
306:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_speaker, "volume", codec->output_volume());
307:     }
308:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_speaker", audio_speaker);
309: 
310:     // Screen
311:     auto screen = cJSON_CreateObject();
312:     if (auto backlight = board.GetBacklight())
313:     {
314:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(screen, "brightness", backlight->brightness());
315:     }
316:     if (auto display = board.GetDisplay(); display && display->height() > 64)
317:     {
318:         if (auto theme = display->GetTheme())
319:         {
320:             cJSON_AddStringToObject(screen, "theme", theme->name().c_str());
321:         }
322:     }
323:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "screen", screen);
324: 
325:     // Battery
326:     int level = 0;
327:     bool charging = false, discharging = false;
328:     if (board.GetBatteryLevel(level, charging, discharging))
329:     {
330:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
331:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
332:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
333:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
334:     }
335: 
336:     // Network
337:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
338:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
339:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
340:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
341:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();
342:     const char *signal = rssi >= -60 ? "strong" : (rssi >= -70 ? "medium" : "weak");
343:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);
344:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "network", network);
345: 
346:     // Chip temperature
347:     float temp = 0.0f;
348:     if (board.GetTemperature(temp))
349:     {
350:         auto chip = cJSON_CreateObject();
351:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
352:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
353:     }
354: 
355:     auto str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
356:     std::string result(str);
357:     cJSON_free(str);
358:     cJSON_Delete(root);
359:     return result;
360: }

</file>
The `StartNetwork` function initializes the WiFi manager with a predefined SSID prefix and language, sets up a unified event callback to handle various WiFi events (such as scanning, connecting, connected, disconnected, and configuration mode entry/exit), and attempts to establish a WiFi connection or enters configuration mode if needed. It acts as the central handler for all WiFi-related state changes and user interactions within the system.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core intent of initializing the Wi-Fi manager and setting up a callback is preserved.
- However, there are significant differences in how initialization is done (using individual setters vs. a config object).
- The event handling mechanism differs substantially: the generated code assumes a direct mapping from WifiEvent to NetworkEvent, while the ground truth explicitly maps each event type.
- Use of lambda capture and callback signature also differ.

Structure Similarity:
- Both functions follow a similar high-level flow: get instance, initialize, set callback, call TryWifiConnect().
- Function names and overall organization are alike.
- However, API calls differ significantly (`Initialize(config)` vs. `SetApSsidPrefix()` and `SetLanguage()`).
- Callback definitions vary in parameters and logic implementation.
- Some steps like calling `GetSsid()` and detailed switch-case handling are missing in the generated version.

Functional Completeness:
- Initialization is performed but through different methods that may not be functionally equivalent.
- Critical parts such as explicit event translation using a switch statement and retrieval of SSID during specific events are missing.
- The generated code skips important event mappings which could lead to incorrect behavior or loss of functionality.
- Assumes an overloaded or differently designed `OnNetworkEvent` method that might not exist or behave the same way.

results.query: complete the function OnNetworkEvent(NetworkEvent, const std::string &)()
results.description: The `OnNetworkEvent` function handles WiFi network events such as connection, disconnection, scanning, and configuration mode changes. It manages internal state flags, stops timers, releases provisioning resources when connected, and triggers reconnection attempts when exiting configuration mode. The function also notifies an external callback, if set, for further event handling.

The `OnNetworkEvent` function handles WiFi network events such as connection, disconnection, scanning, and configuration mode changes. It manages internal state flags, stops timers, releases provisioning resources when connected, and triggers reconnection attempts when exiting configuration mode. The function also notifies an external callback, if set, for further event handling.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly interprets most NetworkEvent cases and behaves logically.
- Key differences include how config mode exit behavior is handled; the ground truth uses Blufi::deinit(), while the generated version uses WifiManager methods and Application state updates.
- The generated code adds logic like updating device states and conditional reconnection attempts not present in the ground truth but does not contradict core functionality.

Structure Similarity:
- Both use the same function signature and switch-case structure over NetworkEvent.
- Differences exist in API usage: ground truth uses esp_timer_stop() only for Connected/Connecting events, whereas generated includes additional calls.
- Use of logging macros (ESP_LOGI/W) follows similar patterns, though message texts vary slightly.
- External callback invocation at the end matches exactly.

Functional Completeness:
- Most key functionalities from the ground truth are covered: handling connection/disconnection events, managing timers, setting in_config_mode_, triggering TryWifiConnect().
- Missing elements include explicit Blufi resource deinitialization under CONFIG_USE_ESP_BLUFI_WIFI_PROVISIONING define.
- Additional features such as Application state management and extra esp_timer_stop() calls are present but don't detract from completeness regarding original scope.

results.description: The `StartWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's Wi-Fi configuration process by setting the device state to configuring mode and enabling one of several provisioning methods based on compile-time flags. It supports hotspot-based configuration with user prompts, Bluetooth Low Energy provisioning via Blufi, or acoustic provisioning through audio input. The function ensures the appropriate services are started and user feedback is provided during the setup process.

182:     auto &app = Application::GetInstance();

184: 
185:     if (state == kDeviceStateSpeaking || state == kDeviceStateListening || state == kDeviceStateIdle)
186:     {
187:         // Reset protocol (close audio channel, reset protocol)
188:         Application::GetInstance().ResetProtocol();
189: 
190:         xTaskCreate([](void *arg)

199:             WifiManager::GetInstance().StopStation();
200:

203: 
204:             vTaskDelete(NULL); }, "wifi_cfg_delay", 4096, this, 2, NULL);

The `StartWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's Wi-Fi configuration process by setting the device state to configuring mode and enabling one of several provisioning methods based on compile-time flags. It supports hotspot-based configuration with user prompts, Bluetooth Low Energy provisioning via Blufi, or acoustic provisioning through audio input. The function ensures the appropriate services are started and user feedback is provided during the setup process.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to implement WiFi configuration mode but simplifies the logic significantly.
- It uses a single #if-elif-else chain instead of supporting multiple provisioning methods simultaneously like the ground truth.
- Misses important details such as scheduling alerts with specific hints or URLs.
- Uses simplified state setting (`kDeviceStateConfiguring` vs `kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring`) which may not be semantically equivalent.
- Lacks detailed logging and proper task creation for acoustic provisioning.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches, but internal control flow diverges greatly from the ground truth.
- Ground truth supports concurrent provisioning mechanisms using separate #if blocks; generated code uses mutually exclusive conditions.
- APIs used differ — e.g., `ShowNotification()` vs scheduled `Alert()` with formatted strings in the ground truth.
- Task creation pattern for acoustic provisioning is oversimplified in the generated version.
- No use of lambdas or delayed scheduling as seen in the original.

Functional Completeness:
- Only partially implements one provisioning method at a time rather than allowing combinations.
- Omits critical features like displaying SSID/hotspot info, web URL access prompts, or sound notifications.
- Does not correctly start AP mode for hotspot provisioning (`StartConfigAp()` vs `StartAccessPoint()`).
- Missing integration points such as `GetApWebUrl()` or showing connect-to-hotspot messages.
- Simplified handling of Blufi and acoustic provisioning without initializing required components properly.

results.description: The `EnterWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's transition into Wi-Fi configuration mode, displaying a notification and resetting active audio/communication processes before starting the configuration process. It ensures graceful shutdown of ongoing operations and validates the current device state to prevent conflicts. This function is used to reconfigure network settings when the device needs to connect to a new Wi-Fi network.

The `EnterWifiConfigMode` function initiates the device's transition into Wi-Fi configuration mode, displaying a notification and resetting active audio/communication processes before starting the configuration process. It ensures graceful shutdown of ongoing operations and validates the current device state to prevent conflicts. This function is used to reconfigure network settings when the device needs to connect to a new Wi-Fi network.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to enter Wi-Fi config mode and includes steps like stopping ongoing Wi-Fi operations and displaying a notification.
- However, it misses critical logic such as checking the device state (e.g., kDeviceStateSpeaking, kDeviceStateListening), which determines whether to delay or immediately proceed.
- It also lacks the task creation pattern used in the ground truth to handle delays and asynchronous behavior safely.
- Some additional logic in the generated code (like Blufi deinit and AFSK demod stop) is not present in the ground truth, suggesting possible divergence in intended behavior or platform specifics.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches, but internal control flow differs significantly.
- Ground truth uses explicit state checks and conditional task spawning; generated code does not follow this pattern.
- APIs like `esp_timer_stop`, `WifiManager::StopStation`, and `StartWifiConfigMode` are common, but their usage context differs.
- Use of `Application::Alert()` instead of `GetDisplay()->ShowNotification()` shows structural deviation in UI interaction.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality of entering Wi-Fi config mode is present.
- Missing key behaviors: proper state handling before transitioning, graceful shutdown based on current device state, delayed execution using FreeRTOS tasks.
- Additional features like Blufi and AFSK handling may indicate over-engineering or inclusion of non-required functionality compared to the reference.
- Lacks error logging when an invalid state is detected (present in ground truth).

The `WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson` function generates a JSON-formatted string containing the current status of various hardware components, including audio speaker volume, screen brightness and theme, battery level and charging state, WiFi network details (SSID and signal strength), and chip temperature. It aggregates data from multiple subsystems such as audio, display, power management, and network interfaces. The result is a compact, machine-readable representation of the device's operational state.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to produce a JSON string representing device status, but it diverges significantly in implementation approach. While both codes aim to report speaker volume, screen info, battery, WiFi, and chip temperature, the generated version uses direct setting accessors rather than interacting with hardware abstractions like `Board`, `cJSON`, or specific component getters (`GetAudioCodec`, `GetBacklight`, etc.). It also omits important logic such as conditional checks for valid objects or formatting signal strength into qualitative values ("strong"/"medium"/"weak"). This results in lower semantic fidelity despite overlapping intent.

Structure Similarity:
The structural differences are substantial. Ground truth utilizes `cJSON` library for structured object creation and proper memory management, whereas the generated code manually concatenates strings to form JSON — error-prone and structurally dissimilar. Function calls differ widely; e.g., use of `Settings::GetInstance()` instead of `Board::GetInstance()`, missing instantiation of sub-objects like "audio_speaker" or "battery", and no equivalent of `cJSON_PrintUnformatted`/`cJSON_Delete`. Control flows vary due to lack of null-checks and conditional insertion logic found in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
Key functionalities from the ground truth are partially covered but often simplified or misrepresented. For instance:
- Speaker volume is fetched directly via `Settings` instead of using `AudioCodec`.
- Screen brightness and theme come from global settings without checking display height or accessing themes through `display->GetTheme()`.
- Battery includes level and charging state but lacks discharge flag and structured encapsulation.
- Network section misses mapping RSSI to signal quality labels.
- Chip temperature retrieval bypasses `Board::GetTemperature`.
Additionally, there's no handling for optional components or dynamic content inclusion based on availability, which compromises completeness.

This ensures a consistent and adaptable user interface experience across different lighting conditions or aesthetic preferences.

29:     : panel_io_(panel_io), panel_(panel)
30: {
31:     width_ = width;
32:     height_ = height;
33: 
34:     // Initialize LCD themes
35:     InitializeLcdThemes();
36: 
37:     // Load theme from settings
38:     Settings settings("display", false);
39:     std::string theme_name = settings.GetString("theme", "light");
40:     current_theme_ = LvglThemeManager::GetInstance().GetTheme(theme_name);
41: 
42:     // Create a timer to hide the preview image
43:     esp_timer_create_args_t preview_timer_args = {
44:         .callback = [](void *arg)
45:         {
46:             LcdDisplay *display = static_cast<LcdDisplay *>(arg);
47:             display->SetPreviewImage(nullptr);
48:         },
49:         .arg = this,
50:         .dispatch_method = ESP_TIMER_TASK,
51:         .name = "preview_timer",
52:         .skip_unhandled_events = false,
53:     };
54:     esp_timer_create(&preview_timer_args, &preview_timer_);
55: }
56: 
57: SpiLcdDisplay::SpiLcdDisplay(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t panel_io, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel,
58:                              int width, int height, int offset_x, int offset_y, bool mirror_x, bool mirror_y, bool swap_xy)
59:     : LcdDisplay(panel_io, panel, width, height)
60: {
61: 
62:     // draw white
63:     std::vector<uint16_t> buffer(width_, 0xFFFF);
64:     for (int y = 0; y < height_; y++)
65:     {
66:         esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(panel_, 0, y, width_, y + 1, buffer.data());
67:     }
68: 
69:     // Set the display to on
70:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Turning display on");
71:     {
72:         esp_err_t __err = esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(panel_, true);
73:         if (__err == ESP_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED)
74:         {
75:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Panel does not support disp_on_off; assuming ON");
76:         }
77:         else
78:         {
79:             ESP_ERROR_CHECK(__err);
80:         }
81:     }
82: 
83:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize LVGL library");
84:     lv_init();
85: 
86: #if CONFIG_SPIRAM
87:     // lv image cache, currently only PNG is supported
88:     size_t psram_size_mb = esp_psram_get_size() / 1024 / 1024;
89:     if (psram_size_mb >= 8)
90:     {
91:         lv_image_cache_resize(2 * 1024 * 1024, true);
92:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Use 2MB of PSRAM for image cache");
93:     }
94:     else if (psram_size_mb >= 2)
95:     {
96:         lv_image_cache_resize(512 * 1024, true);
97:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Use 512KB of PSRAM for image cache");
98:     }
99: #endif
100: 
101:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initialize LVGL port");
102:     lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
103:     port_cfg.task_priority = 1;
104: #if CONFIG_SOC_CPU_CORES_NUM > 1
105:     port_cfg.task_affinity = 1;
106: #endif
107:     lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);
108: 
109:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Adding LCD display");
110:     const lvgl_port_display_cfg_t display_cfg = {
111:         .io_handle = panel_io_,
112:         .panel_handle = panel_,
113:         .control_handle = nullptr,
114:         .buffer_size = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_ * 20),
115:         .double_buffer = false,
116:         .trans_size = 0,
117:         .hres = static_cast<uint32_t>(width_),
118:         .vres = static_cast<uint32_t>(height_),
119:         .monochrome = false,
120:         .rotation = {
121:             .swap_xy = swap_xy,
122:             .mirror_x = mirror_x,
123:             .mirror_y = mirror_y,
124:         },
125:         .color_format = LV_COLOR_FORMAT_RGB565,
126:         .flags = {
127:             .buff_dma = 1,
128:             .buff_spiram = 0,
129:             .sw_rotate = 0,
130:             .swap_bytes = 1,
131:             .full_refresh = 0,
132:             .direct_mode = 0,
133:         },
134:     };
135: 
136:     display_ = lvgl_port_add_disp(&display_cfg);
137:     if (display_ == nullptr)
138:     {
139:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add display");
140:         return;
141:     }
142: 
143:     if (offset_x != 0 || offset_y != 0)
144:     {
145:         lv_display_set_offset(display_, offset_x, offset_y);
146:     }
147: 
148:     SetupUI();
149: }

152: RgbLcdDisplay::RgbLcdDisplay(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t panel_io, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel,
153:                              int width, int height, int offset_x, int offset_y,

157: 
158:     // draw white
159:     std::vector<uint16_t> buffer(width_, 0xFFFF);

216: MipiLcdDisplay::MipiLcdDisplay(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t panel_io, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel,
217:                                int width, int height, int offset_x, int offset_y,

263:     if (offset_x != 0 || offset_y != 0)

268:     SetupUI();

310:         lv_obj_del(content_);
311:     }
312:     if (bottom_bar_ != nullptr)
313:     {
314:         lv_obj_del(bottom_bar_);
315:     }
316:     if (status_bar_ != nullptr)
317:     {
318:         lv_obj_del(status_bar_);
319:     }
320:     if (top_bar_ != nullptr)
321:     {
322:         lv_obj_del(top_bar_);
323:     }

326:         lv_obj_del(side_bar_);
327:     }

357: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
358: void LcdDisplay::SetupUI()
359: {
360:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
361: 
362:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
363:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
364:     auto icon_font = lvgl_theme->icon_font()->font();
365:     auto large_icon_font = lvgl_theme->large_icon_font()->font();
366: 
367:     auto screen = lv_screen_active();
368:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(screen, text_font, 0);
369:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(screen, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
370:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(screen, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
371: 
372:     /* Container */
373:     container_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
374:     lv_obj_set_size(container_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_VER_RES);
375:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(container_, 0, 0);
376:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(container_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
377:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container_, 0, 0);
378:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container_, 0, 0);
379:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(container_, 0, 0);
380:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(container_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
381:     lv_obj_set_style_border_color(container_, lvgl_theme->border_color(), 0);
382: 
383:     /* Layer 1: Top bar - for status icons */
384:     top_bar_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
385:     lv_obj_set_size(top_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
386:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(top_bar_, 0, 0);
387:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(top_bar_, LV_OPA_50, 0); // 50% opacity background
388:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
389:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(top_bar_, 0, 0);
390:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(top_bar_, 0, 0);
391:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
392:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
393:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_left(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
394:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_right(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
395:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
396:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_SPACE_BETWEEN, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
397:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(top_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
398: 
399:     // Left icon
400:     network_label_ = lv_label_create(top_bar_);
401:     lv_label_set_text(network_label_, "");
402:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(network_label_, icon_font, 0);
403:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(network_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
404: 
405:     // Right icons container
406:     lv_obj_t *right_icons = lv_obj_create(top_bar_);
407:     lv_obj_set_size(right_icons, LV_SIZE_CONTENT, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
408:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(right_icons, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
409:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(right_icons, 0, 0);
410:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(right_icons, 0, 0);
411:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(right_icons, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
412:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(right_icons, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_END, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
413: 
414:     mute_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
415:     lv_label_set_text(mute_label_, "");
416:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, icon_font, 0);
417:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(mute_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
418: 
419:     battery_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
420:     lv_label_set_text(battery_label_, "");
421:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, icon_font, 0);
422:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
423:     lv_obj_set_style_margin_left(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
424: 
425:     /* Layer 2: Status bar - for center text labels */
426:     status_bar_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
427:     lv_obj_set_size(status_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
428:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(status_bar_, 0, 0);
429:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(status_bar_, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0); // Transparent background
430:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(status_bar_, 0, 0);
431:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(status_bar_, 0, 0);
432:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
433:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
434:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(status_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
435:     lv_obj_set_style_layout(status_bar_, LV_LAYOUT_NONE, 0); // Use absolute positioning
436:     lv_obj_align(status_bar_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, 0);       // Overlap with top_bar_
437: 
438:     notification_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
439:     lv_obj_set_width(notification_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
440:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(notification_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
441:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(notification_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
442:     lv_label_set_text(notification_label_, "");
443:     lv_obj_align(notification_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
444:     lv_obj_add_flag(notification_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
445: 
446:     status_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
447:     lv_obj_set_width(status_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
448:     lv_label_set_long_mode(status_label_, LV_LABEL_LONG_SCROLL_CIRCULAR);
449:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(status_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
450:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(status_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
451:     lv_label_set_text(status_label_, Lang::Strings::INITIALIZING);
452:     lv_obj_align(status_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
453: 
454:     /* Content - Chat area */
455:     content_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
456:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(content_, 0, 0);
457:     lv_obj_set_width(content_, LV_HOR_RES);
458:     lv_obj_set_flex_grow(content_, 1);
459:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
460:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(content_, 0, 0);
461:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(content_, lvgl_theme->chat_background_color(), 0); // Background for chat area
462: 
463:     // Enable scrolling for chat content
464:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(content_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
465:     lv_obj_set_scroll_dir(content_, LV_DIR_VER);
466: 
467:     // Create a flex container for chat messages
468:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(content_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
469:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(content_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START);
470:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0); // Space between messages
471: 
472:     // We'll create chat messages dynamically in SetChatMessage
473:     chat_message_label_ = nullptr;
474: 
475:     low_battery_popup_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
476:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(low_battery_popup_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
477:     lv_obj_set_size(low_battery_popup_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.9, text_font->line_height * 2);
478:     lv_obj_align(low_battery_popup_, LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_MID, 0, -lvgl_theme->spacing(4));
479:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->low_battery_color(), 0);
480:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
481:     low_battery_label_ = lv_label_create(low_battery_popup_);
482:     lv_label_set_text(low_battery_label_, Lang::Strings::BATTERY_NEED_CHARGE);
483:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(low_battery_label_, lv_color_white(), 0);
484:     lv_obj_center(low_battery_label_);
485:     lv_obj_add_flag(low_battery_popup_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
486: 
487:     emoji_image_ = lv_img_create(screen);
488:     lv_obj_align(emoji_image_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, text_font->line_height + lvgl_theme->spacing(8));
489: 
490:     // Display AI logo while booting
491:     emoji_label_ = lv_label_create(screen);
492:     lv_obj_center(emoji_label_);
493:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(emoji_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
494:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(emoji_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
495:     lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, FONT_AWESOME_MICROCHIP_AI);
496: }
497: #if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32P4
498: #define MAX_MESSAGES 40
499: #else
500: #define MAX_MESSAGES 20
501: #endif
502: void LcdDisplay::SetChatMessage(const char *role, const char *content)
503: {
504:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
505:     if (content_ == nullptr)
506:     {
507:         return;
508:     }
509: 
510:     // Check if message count exceeds limit
511:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
512:     if (child_count >= MAX_MESSAGES)
513:     {
514:         // Delete the oldest message (first child object)
515:         lv_obj_t *first_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, 0);
516:         lv_obj_t *last_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
517:         if (first_child != nullptr)
518:         {
519:             lv_obj_del(first_child);
520:         }
521:         // Scroll to the last message immediately
522:         if (last_child != nullptr)
523:         {
524:             lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(last_child, LV_ANIM_OFF);
525:         }
526:     }
527: 
528:     // Collapse system messages (if it's a system message, check if the last message is also a system message)
529:     if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
530:     {
531:         if (child_count > 0)
532:         {
533:             // Get the last message container
534:             lv_obj_t *last_container = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
535:             if (last_container != nullptr && lv_obj_get_child_cnt(last_container) > 0)
536:             {
537:                 // Get the bubble inside the container
538:                 lv_obj_t *last_bubble = lv_obj_get_child(last_container, 0);
539:                 if (last_bubble != nullptr)
540:                 {
541:                     // Check if bubble type is system message
542:                     void *bubble_type_ptr = lv_obj_get_user_data(last_bubble);
543:                     if (bubble_type_ptr != nullptr && strcmp((const char *)bubble_type_ptr, "system") == 0)
544:                     {
545:                         // If the last message is also a system message, delete it
546:                         lv_obj_del(last_container);
547:                     }
548:                 }
549:             }
550:         }
551:     }
552:     else
553:     {
554:         // Hide the centered AI logo
555:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
556:     }
557: 
558:     // Avoid empty message boxes
559:     if (strlen(content) == 0)
560:     {
561:         return;
562:     }
563: 
564:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
565:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
566: 
567:     // Create a message bubble
568:     lv_obj_t *msg_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
569:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(msg_bubble, 8, 0);
570:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(msg_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
571:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
572:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
573: 
574:     // Create the message text
575:     lv_obj_t *msg_text = lv_label_create(msg_bubble);
576:     lv_label_set_text(msg_text, content);
577: 
578:     // Calculate actual text width
579:     lv_coord_t text_width = lv_txt_get_width(content, strlen(content), text_font, 0);
580: 
581:     // Calculate bubble width
582:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 85 / 100 - 16; // 85% of screen width
583:     lv_coord_t min_width = 20;
584:     lv_coord_t bubble_width;
585: 
586:     // Ensure text width is not less than minimum width
587:     if (text_width < min_width)
588:     {
589:         text_width = min_width;
590:     }
591: 
592:     // If text width is less than max width, use text width
593:     if (text_width < max_width)
594:     {
595:         bubble_width = text_width;
596:     }
597:     else
598:     {
599:         bubble_width = max_width;
600:     }
601: 
602:     // Set message text width
603:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_text, bubble_width); // Subtract padding
604:     lv_label_set_long_mode(msg_text, LV_LABEL_LONG_WRAP);
605: 
606:     // Set bubble width
607:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, bubble_width);
608:     lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
609: 
610:     // Set alignment and style based on message role
611:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
612:     {
613:         // User messages are right-aligned with green background
614:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->user_bubble_color(), 0);
615:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
616:         // Set text color for contrast
617:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
618: 
619:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
620:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"user");
621: 
622:         // Set appropriate width for content
623:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
624:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
625: 
626:         // Don't grow
627:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
628:     }
629:     else if (strcmp(role, "assistant") == 0)
630:     {
631:         // Assistant messages are left-aligned with white background
632:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
633:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
634:         // Set text color for contrast
635:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
636: 
637:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
638:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"assistant");
639: 
640:         // Set appropriate width for content
641:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
642:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
643: 
644:         // Don't grow
645:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
646:     }
647:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
648:     {
649:         // System messages are center-aligned with light gray background
650:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->system_bubble_color(), 0);
651:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
652:         // Set text color for contrast
653:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->system_text_color(), 0);
654: 
655:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
656:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"system");
657: 
658:         // Set appropriate width for content
659:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
660:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
661: 
662:         // Don't grow
663:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
664:     }
665: 
666:     // Create a full-width container for user messages to ensure right alignment
667:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
668:     {
669:         // Create a full-width container
670:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
671:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
672:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
673: 
674:         // Make container transparent and borderless
675:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
676:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
677:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
678: 
679:         // Move the message bubble into this container
680:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
681: 
682:         // Right align the bubble in the container
683:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_RIGHT_MID, -25, 0);
684: 
685:         // Auto-scroll to this container
686:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
687:     }
688:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
689:     {
690:         // Create full-width container for system messages to ensure center alignment
691:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
692:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
693:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
694: 
695:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
696:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
697:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
698: 
699:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
700:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
701:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
702:     }
703:     else
704:     {
705:         // For assistant messages
706:         // Left align assistant messages
707:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
708: 
709:         // Auto-scroll to the message bubble
710:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(msg_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
711:     }
712: 
713:     // Store reference to the latest message label
714:     chat_message_label_ = msg_text;
715: }

717: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
718: {
719:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
720:     if (content_ == nullptr)
721:     {
722:         return;
723:     }
724: 
725:     if (image == nullptr)
726:     {
727:         return;
728:     }
729: 
730:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
731:     // Create a message bubble for image preview
732:     lv_obj_t *img_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
733:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(img_bubble, 8, 0);
734:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(img_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
735:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(img_bubble, 0, 0);
736:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
737: 
738:     // Set image bubble background color (similar to system message)
739:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
740:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(img_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
741: 
742:     // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
743:     lv_obj_set_user_data(img_bubble, (void *)"image");
744: 
745:     // Create the image object inside the bubble
746:     lv_obj_t *preview_image = lv_image_create(img_bubble);
747: 
748:     // Calculate appropriate size for the image
749:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 70 / 100;  // 70% of screen width
750:     lv_coord_t max_height = LV_VER_RES * 50 / 100; // 50% of screen height
751: 
752:     // Calculate zoom factor to fit within maximum dimensions
753:     auto img_dsc = image->image_dsc();
754:     lv_coord_t img_width = img_dsc->header.w;
755:     lv_coord_t img_height = img_dsc->header.h;
756:     if (img_width == 0 || img_height == 0)
757:     {
758:         img_width = max_width;
759:         img_height = max_height;
760:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid image dimensions: %ld x %ld, using default dimensions: %ld x %ld", img_width, img_height, max_width, max_height);
761:     }
762: 
763:     lv_coord_t zoom_w = (max_width * 256) / img_width;
764:     lv_coord_t zoom_h = (max_height * 256) / img_height;
765:     lv_coord_t zoom = (zoom_w < zoom_h) ? zoom_w : zoom_h;
766: 
767:     // Ensure zoom doesn't exceed 256 (100%)
768:     if (zoom > 256)
769:         zoom = 256;
770: 
771:     // Set image properties
772:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image, img_dsc);
773:     lv_image_set_scale(preview_image, zoom);
774: 
775:     // Add event handler to clean up LvglImage when image is deleted
776:     // We need to transfer ownership of the unique_ptr to the event callback
777:     LvglImage *raw_image = image.release(); // Release ownership of smart pointer
778:     lv_obj_add_event_cb(preview_image, [](lv_event_t *e)
779:                         {
780:         LvglImage* img = (LvglImage*)lv_event_get_user_data(e);
781:         if (img != nullptr) {
782:             delete img; // Properly release memory by deleting LvglImage object
783:         } }, LV_EVENT_DELETE, (void *)raw_image);
784: 
785:     // Calculate actual scaled image dimensions
786:     lv_coord_t scaled_width = (img_width * zoom) / 256;
787:     lv_coord_t scaled_height = (img_height * zoom) / 256;
788: 
789:     // Set bubble size to be 16 pixels larger than the image (8 pixels on each side)
790:     lv_obj_set_width(img_bubble, scaled_width + 16);
791:     lv_obj_set_height(img_bubble, scaled_height + 16);
792: 
793:     // Don't grow in flex layout
794:     lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(img_bubble, 0, 0);
795: 
796:     // Center the image within the bubble
797:     lv_obj_center(preview_image);
798: 
799:     // Left align the image bubble like assistant messages
800:     lv_obj_align(img_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
801: 
802:     // Auto-scroll to the image bubble
803:     lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(img_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
804: }

958:     lv_obj_add_flag(low_battery_popup_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
959: }

961: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
962: {
963:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
964:     if (preview_image_ == nullptr)
965:     {
966:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Preview image is not initialized");
967:         return;
968:     }
969: 
970:     if (image == nullptr)
971:     {
972:         esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
973:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
974:         lv_obj_add_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
975:         preview_image_cached_.reset();
976:         if (gif_controller_)
977:         {
978:             gif_controller_->Start();
979:         }
980:         return;
981:     }
982: 
983:     preview_image_cached_ = std::move(image);
984:     auto img_dsc = preview_image_cached_->image_dsc();
985:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image_, img_dsc);
986:     if (img_dsc->header.w > 0 && img_dsc->header.h > 0)
987:     {
988:         // zoom factor 0.5
989:         lv_image_set_scale(preview_image_, 128 * width_ / img_dsc->header.w);
990:     }
991: 
992:     // Hide emoji_box_
993:     if (gif_controller_)
994:     {
995:         gif_controller_->Stop();
996:     }
