394: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
395: void LcdDisplay::SetupUI()
396: {
397:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
398: 
399:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
400:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
401:     auto icon_font = lvgl_theme->icon_font()->font();
402:     auto large_icon_font = lvgl_theme->large_icon_font()->font();
403: 
404:     auto screen = lv_screen_active();
405:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(screen, text_font, 0);
406:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(screen, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
407:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(screen, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
408: 
409:     /* Container */
410:     container_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
411:     lv_obj_set_size(container_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_VER_RES);
412:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(container_, 0, 0);
413:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(container_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
414:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container_, 0, 0);
415:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container_, 0, 0);
416:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(container_, 0, 0);
417:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(container_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
418:     lv_obj_set_style_border_color(container_, lvgl_theme->border_color(), 0);
419: 
420:     /* Layer 1: Top bar - for status icons */
421:     top_bar_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
422:     lv_obj_set_size(top_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
423:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(top_bar_, 0, 0);
424:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(top_bar_, LV_OPA_50, 0); // 50% opacity background
425:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->background_color(), 0);
426:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(top_bar_, 0, 0);
427:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(top_bar_, 0, 0);
428:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
429:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
430:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_left(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
431:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_right(top_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
432:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
433:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(top_bar_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_SPACE_BETWEEN, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
434:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(top_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
435: 
436:     // Left icon
437:     network_label_ = lv_label_create(top_bar_);
438:     lv_label_set_text(network_label_, "");
439:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(network_label_, icon_font, 0);
440:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(network_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
441: 
442:     // Right icons container
443:     lv_obj_t *right_icons = lv_obj_create(top_bar_);
444:     lv_obj_set_size(right_icons, LV_SIZE_CONTENT, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
445:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(right_icons, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
446:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(right_icons, 0, 0);
447:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(right_icons, 0, 0);
448:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(right_icons, LV_FLEX_FLOW_ROW);
449:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(right_icons, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_END, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_CENTER);
450: 
451:     mute_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
452:     lv_label_set_text(mute_label_, "");
453:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(mute_label_, icon_font, 0);
454:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(mute_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
455: 
456:     battery_label_ = lv_label_create(right_icons);
457:     lv_label_set_text(battery_label_, "");
458:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(battery_label_, icon_font, 0);
459:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
460:     lv_obj_set_style_margin_left(battery_label_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
461: 
462:     /* Layer 2: Status bar - for center text labels */
463:     status_bar_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
464:     lv_obj_set_size(status_bar_, LV_HOR_RES, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
465:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(status_bar_, 0, 0);
466:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(status_bar_, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0); // Transparent background
467:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(status_bar_, 0, 0);
468:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(status_bar_, 0, 0);
469:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_top(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
470:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_bottom(status_bar_, lvgl_theme->spacing(2), 0);
471:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(status_bar_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
472:     lv_obj_set_style_layout(status_bar_, LV_LAYOUT_NONE, 0); // Use absolute positioning
473:     lv_obj_align(status_bar_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, 0);       // Overlap with top_bar_
474: 
475:     notification_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
476:     lv_obj_set_width(notification_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
477:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(notification_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
478:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(notification_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
479:     lv_label_set_text(notification_label_, "");
480:     lv_obj_align(notification_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
481:     lv_obj_add_flag(notification_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
482: 
483:     status_label_ = lv_label_create(status_bar_);
484:     lv_obj_set_width(status_label_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.8);
485:     lv_label_set_long_mode(status_label_, LV_LABEL_LONG_SCROLL_CIRCULAR);
486:     lv_obj_set_style_text_align(status_label_, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
487:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(status_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
488:     lv_label_set_text(status_label_, Lang::Strings::INITIALIZING);
489:     lv_obj_align(status_label_, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
490: 
491:     /* Content - Chat area */
492:     content_ = lv_obj_create(container_);
493:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(content_, 0, 0);
494:     lv_obj_set_width(content_, LV_HOR_RES);
495:     lv_obj_set_flex_grow(content_, 1);
496:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
497:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(content_, 0, 0);
498:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(content_, lvgl_theme->chat_background_color(), 0); // Background for chat area
499: 
500:     // Enable scrolling for chat content
501:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(content_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
502:     lv_obj_set_scroll_dir(content_, LV_DIR_VER);
503: 
504:     // Create a flex container for chat messages
505:     lv_obj_set_flex_flow(content_, LV_FLEX_FLOW_COLUMN);
506:     lv_obj_set_flex_align(content_, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START, LV_FLEX_ALIGN_START);
507:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_row(content_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0); // Space between messages
508: 
509:     // We'll create chat messages dynamically in SetChatMessage
510:     chat_message_label_ = nullptr;
511: 
512:     low_battery_popup_ = lv_obj_create(screen);
513:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(low_battery_popup_, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
514:     lv_obj_set_size(low_battery_popup_, LV_HOR_RES * 0.9, text_font->line_height * 2);
515:     lv_obj_align(low_battery_popup_, LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_MID, 0, -lvgl_theme->spacing(4));
516:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->low_battery_color(), 0);
517:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(low_battery_popup_, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
518:     low_battery_label_ = lv_label_create(low_battery_popup_);
519:     lv_label_set_text(low_battery_label_, Lang::Strings::BATTERY_NEED_CHARGE);
520:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(low_battery_label_, lv_color_white(), 0);
521:     lv_obj_center(low_battery_label_);
522:     lv_obj_add_flag(low_battery_popup_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
523: 
524:     emoji_image_ = lv_img_create(screen);
525:     lv_obj_align(emoji_image_, LV_ALIGN_TOP_MID, 0, text_font->line_height + lvgl_theme->spacing(8));
526: 
527:     // Display AI logo while booting
528:     emoji_label_ = lv_label_create(screen);
529:     lv_obj_center(emoji_label_);
530:     lv_obj_set_style_text_font(emoji_label_, large_icon_font, 0);
531:     lv_obj_set_style_text_color(emoji_label_, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
532:     lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, FONT_AWESOME_MICROCHIP_AI);
533: }
534: #if CONFIG_IDF_TARGET_ESP32P4
535: #define MAX_MESSAGES 40
536: #else
537: #define MAX_MESSAGES 20
538: #endif
539: void LcdDisplay::SetChatMessage(const char *role, const char *content)
540: {
541:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
542:     if (content_ == nullptr)
543:     {
544:         return;
545:     }
546: 
547:     // Check if message count exceeds limit
548:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
549:     if (child_count >= MAX_MESSAGES)
550:     {
551:         // Delete the oldest message (first child object)
552:         lv_obj_t *first_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, 0);
553:         lv_obj_t *last_child = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
554:         if (first_child != nullptr)
555:         {
556:             lv_obj_del(first_child);
557:         }
558:         // Scroll to the last message immediately
559:         if (last_child != nullptr)
560:         {
561:             lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(last_child, LV_ANIM_OFF);
562:         }
563:     }
564: 
565:     // Collapse system messages (if it's a system message, check if the last message is also a system message)
566:     if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
567:     {
568:         if (child_count > 0)
569:         {
570:             // Get the last message container
571:             lv_obj_t *last_container = lv_obj_get_child(content_, child_count - 1);
572:             if (last_container != nullptr && lv_obj_get_child_cnt(last_container) > 0)
573:             {
574:                 // Get the bubble inside the container
575:                 lv_obj_t *last_bubble = lv_obj_get_child(last_container, 0);
576:                 if (last_bubble != nullptr)
577:                 {
578:                     // Check if bubble type is system message
579:                     void *bubble_type_ptr = lv_obj_get_user_data(last_bubble);
580:                     if (bubble_type_ptr != nullptr && strcmp((const char *)bubble_type_ptr, "system") == 0)
581:                     {
582:                         // If the last message is also a system message, delete it
583:                         lv_obj_del(last_container);
584:                     }
585:                 }
586:             }
587:         }
588:     }
589:     else
590:     {
591:         // Hide the centered AI logo
592:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
593:     }
594: 
595:     // Avoid empty message boxes
596:     if (strlen(content) == 0)
597:     {
598:         return;
599:     }
600: 
601:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
602:     auto text_font = lvgl_theme->text_font()->font();
603: 
604:     // Create a message bubble
605:     lv_obj_t *msg_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
606:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(msg_bubble, 8, 0);
607:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(msg_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
608:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
609:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
610: 
611:     // Create the message text
612:     lv_obj_t *msg_text = lv_label_create(msg_bubble);
613:     lv_label_set_text(msg_text, content);
614: 
615:     // Calculate actual text width
616:     lv_coord_t text_width = lv_txt_get_width(content, strlen(content), text_font, 0);
617: 
618:     // Calculate bubble width
619:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 85 / 100 - 16; // 85% of screen width
620:     lv_coord_t min_width = 20;
621:     lv_coord_t bubble_width;
622: 
623:     // Ensure text width is not less than minimum width
624:     if (text_width < min_width)
625:     {
626:         text_width = min_width;
627:     }
628: 
629:     // If text width is less than max width, use text width
630:     if (text_width < max_width)
631:     {
632:         bubble_width = text_width;
633:     }
634:     else
635:     {
636:         bubble_width = max_width;
637:     }
638: 
639:     // Set message text width
640:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_text, bubble_width); // Subtract padding
641:     lv_label_set_long_mode(msg_text, LV_LABEL_LONG_WRAP);
642: 
643:     // Set bubble width
644:     lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, bubble_width);
645:     lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
646: 
647:     // Set alignment and style based on message role
648:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
649:     {
650:         // User messages are right-aligned with green background
651:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->user_bubble_color(), 0);
652:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
653:         // Set text color for contrast
654:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
655: 
656:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
657:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"user");
658: 
659:         // Set appropriate width for content
660:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
661:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
662: 
663:         // Don't grow
664:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
665:     }
666:     else if (strcmp(role, "assistant") == 0)
667:     {
668:         // Assistant messages are left-aligned with white background
669:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
670:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
671:         // Set text color for contrast
672:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->text_color(), 0);
673: 
674:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
675:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"assistant");
676: 
677:         // Set appropriate width for content
678:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
679:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
680: 
681:         // Don't grow
682:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
683:     }
684:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
685:     {
686:         // System messages are center-aligned with light gray background
687:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(msg_bubble, lvgl_theme->system_bubble_color(), 0);
688:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(msg_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
689:         // Set text color for contrast
690:         lv_obj_set_style_text_color(msg_text, lvgl_theme->system_text_color(), 0);
691: 
692:         // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
693:         lv_obj_set_user_data(msg_bubble, (void *)"system");
694: 
695:         // Set appropriate width for content
696:         lv_obj_set_width(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
697:         lv_obj_set_height(msg_bubble, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
698: 
699:         // Don't grow
700:         lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(msg_bubble, 0, 0);
701:     }
702: 
703:     // Create a full-width container for user messages to ensure right alignment
704:     if (strcmp(role, "user") == 0)
705:     {
706:         // Create a full-width container
707:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
708:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
709:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
710: 
711:         // Make container transparent and borderless
712:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
713:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
714:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
715: 
716:         // Move the message bubble into this container
717:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
718: 
719:         // Right align the bubble in the container
720:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_RIGHT_MID, -25, 0);
721: 
722:         // Auto-scroll to this container
723:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
724:     }
725:     else if (strcmp(role, "system") == 0)
726:     {
727:         // Create full-width container for system messages to ensure center alignment
728:         lv_obj_t *container = lv_obj_create(content_);
729:         lv_obj_set_width(container, LV_HOR_RES);
730:         lv_obj_set_height(container, LV_SIZE_CONTENT);
731: 
732:         lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(container, LV_OPA_TRANSP, 0);
733:         lv_obj_set_style_border_width(container, 0, 0);
734:         lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(container, 0, 0);
735: 
736:         lv_obj_set_parent(msg_bubble, container);
737:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 0);
738:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(container, LV_ANIM_ON);
739:     }
740:     else
741:     {
742:         // For assistant messages
743:         // Left align assistant messages
744:         lv_obj_align(msg_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
745: 
746:         // Auto-scroll to the message bubble
747:         lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(msg_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
748:     }
749: 
750:     // Store reference to the latest message label
751:     chat_message_label_ = msg_text;
752: }
753: 
754: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
755: {
756:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
757:     if (content_ == nullptr)
758:     {
759:         return;
760:     }
761: 
762:     if (image == nullptr)
763:     {
764:         return;
765:     }
766: 
767:     auto lvgl_theme = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_);
768:     // Create a message bubble for image preview
769:     lv_obj_t *img_bubble = lv_obj_create(content_);
770:     lv_obj_set_style_radius(img_bubble, 8, 0);
771:     lv_obj_set_scrollbar_mode(img_bubble, LV_SCROLLBAR_MODE_OFF);
772:     lv_obj_set_style_border_width(img_bubble, 0, 0);
773:     lv_obj_set_style_pad_all(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->spacing(4), 0);
774: 
775:     // Set image bubble background color (similar to system message)
776:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(img_bubble, lvgl_theme->assistant_bubble_color(), 0);
777:     lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(img_bubble, LV_OPA_70, 0);
778: 
779:     // Set custom attribute to mark bubble type
780:     lv_obj_set_user_data(img_bubble, (void *)"image");
781: 
782:     // Create the image object inside the bubble
783:     lv_obj_t *preview_image = lv_image_create(img_bubble);
784: 
785:     // Calculate appropriate size for the image
786:     lv_coord_t max_width = LV_HOR_RES * 70 / 100;  // 70% of screen width
787:     lv_coord_t max_height = LV_VER_RES * 50 / 100; // 50% of screen height
788: 
789:     // Calculate zoom factor to fit within maximum dimensions
790:     auto img_dsc = image->image_dsc();
791:     lv_coord_t img_width = img_dsc->header.w;
792:     lv_coord_t img_height = img_dsc->header.h;
793:     if (img_width == 0 || img_height == 0)
794:     {
795:         img_width = max_width;
796:         img_height = max_height;
797:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid image dimensions: %ld x %ld, using default dimensions: %ld x %ld", img_width, img_height, max_width, max_height);
798:     }
799: 
800:     lv_coord_t zoom_w = (max_width * 256) / img_width;
801:     lv_coord_t zoom_h = (max_height * 256) / img_height;
802:     lv_coord_t zoom = (zoom_w < zoom_h) ? zoom_w : zoom_h;
803: 
804:     // Ensure zoom doesn't exceed 256 (100%)
805:     if (zoom > 256)
806:         zoom = 256;
807: 
808:     // Set image properties
809:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image, img_dsc);
810:     lv_image_set_scale(preview_image, zoom);
811: 
812:     // Add event handler to clean up LvglImage when image is deleted
813:     // We need to transfer ownership of the unique_ptr to the event callback
814:     LvglImage *raw_image = image.release(); // Release ownership of smart pointer
815:     lv_obj_add_event_cb(preview_image, [](lv_event_t *e)
816:                         {
817:         LvglImage* img = (LvglImage*)lv_event_get_user_data(e);
818:         if (img != nullptr) {
819:             delete img; // Properly release memory by deleting LvglImage object
820:         } }, LV_EVENT_DELETE, (void *)raw_image);
821: 
822:     // Calculate actual scaled image dimensions
823:     lv_coord_t scaled_width = (img_width * zoom) / 256;
824:     lv_coord_t scaled_height = (img_height * zoom) / 256;
825: 
826:     // Set bubble size to be 16 pixels larger than the image (8 pixels on each side)
827:     lv_obj_set_width(img_bubble, scaled_width + 16);
828:     lv_obj_set_height(img_bubble, scaled_height + 16);
829: 
830:     // Don't grow in flex layout
831:     lv_obj_set_style_flex_grow(img_bubble, 0, 0);
832: 
833:     // Center the image within the bubble
834:     lv_obj_center(preview_image);
835: 
836:     // Left align the image bubble like assistant messages
837:     lv_obj_align(img_bubble, LV_ALIGN_LEFT_MID, 0, 0);
838: 
839:     // Auto-scroll to the image bubble
840:     lv_obj_scroll_to_view_recursive(img_bubble, LV_ANIM_ON);
841: }

998: void LcdDisplay::SetPreviewImage(std::unique_ptr<LvglImage> image)
999: {
1000:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1001:     if (preview_image_ == nullptr)
1002:     {
1003:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Preview image is not initialized");
1004:         return;
1005:     }
1006: 
1007:     if (image == nullptr)
1008:     {
1009:         esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
1010:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1011:         lv_obj_add_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1012:         preview_image_cached_.reset();
1013:         if (gif_controller_)
1014:         {
1015:             gif_controller_->Start();
1016:         }
1017:         return;
1018:     }
1019: 
1020:     preview_image_cached_ = std::move(image);
1021:     auto img_dsc = preview_image_cached_->image_dsc();
1022:     lv_image_set_src(preview_image_, img_dsc);
1023:     if (img_dsc->header.w > 0 && img_dsc->header.h > 0)
1024:     {
1025:         // zoom factor 0.5
1026:         lv_image_set_scale(preview_image_, 128 * width_ / img_dsc->header.w);
1027:     }
1028: 
1029:     // Hide emoji_box_
1030:     if (gif_controller_)
1031:     {
1032:         gif_controller_->Stop();
1033:     }
1034:     lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_box_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1035:     lv_obj_remove_flag(preview_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1036:     esp_timer_stop(preview_timer_);
1037:     ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_timer_start_once(preview_timer_, PREVIEW_IMAGE_DURATION_MS * 1000));
1038: }

1051: void LcdDisplay::SetEmotion(const char *emotion)
1052: {
1053:     // Stop any running GIF animation
1054:     if (gif_controller_)
1055:     {
1056:         DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1057:         gif_controller_->Stop();
1058:         gif_controller_.reset();
1059:     }
1060: 
1061:     if (emoji_image_ == nullptr)
1062:     {
1063:         return;
1064:     }
1065: 
1066:     auto emoji_collection = static_cast<LvglTheme *>(current_theme_)->emoji_collection();
1067:     auto image = emoji_collection != nullptr ? emoji_collection->GetEmojiImage(emotion) : nullptr;
1068:     if (image == nullptr)
1069:     {
1070:         const char *utf8 = font_awesome_get_utf8(emotion);
1071:         if (utf8 != nullptr && emoji_label_ != nullptr)
1072:         {
1073:             DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1074:             lv_label_set_text(emoji_label_, utf8);
1075:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1076:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1077:         }
1078:         return;
1079:     }
1080: 
1081:     DisplayLockGuard lock(this);
1082:     if (image->IsGif())
1083:     {
1084:         // Create new GIF controller
1085:         gif_controller_ = std::make_unique<LvglGif>(image->image_dsc());
1086: 
1087:         if (gif_controller_->IsLoaded())
1088:         {
1089:             // Set up frame update callback
1090:             gif_controller_->SetFrameCallback([this]()
1091:                                               { lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc()); });
1092: 
1093:             // Set initial frame and start animation
1094:             lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, gif_controller_->image_dsc());
1095:             gif_controller_->Start();
1096: 
1097:             // Show GIF, hide others
1098:             lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1099:             lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1100:         }
1101:         else
1102:         {
1103:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to load GIF for emotion: %s", emotion);
1104:             gif_controller_.reset();
1105:         }
1106:     }
1107:     else
1108:     {
1109:         lv_image_set_src(emoji_image_, image->image_dsc());
1110:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1111:         lv_obj_remove_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1112:     }
1113: 
1114: #if CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE
1115:     // In WeChat message style, if emotion is neutral, don't display it
1116:     uint32_t child_count = lv_obj_get_child_cnt(content_);
1117:     if (strcmp(emotion, "neutral") == 0 && child_count > 0)
1118:     {
1119:         // Stop GIF animation if running
1120:         if (gif_controller_)
1121:         {
1122:             gif_controller_->Stop();
1123:             gif_controller_.reset();
1124:         }
1125: 
1126:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_image_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1127:         lv_obj_add_flag(emoji_label_, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
1128:     }
1129: #endif
1130: }

</file>
The `LcdDisplay::SetTheme` function applies a specified visual theme to the LCD display by updating fonts, colors, background images, and styles for various UI elements including labels, status bars, and message bubbles. It supports dynamic adjustments based on font size and ensures consistent styling across the interface while maintaining thread safety through a display lock. The function also handles optional background images and semi-transparent overlays for enhanced visual presentation.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to implement theme setting functionality but lacks several important behaviors from the ground truth. Key semantic differences include:
- Missing dynamic font selection logic based on line height (large_icon_font vs icon_font)
- No proper handling of background images (only sets background color)
- Incorrectly applies styles directly to screen object instead of using container hierarchy properly
- Different approach to chat bubble styling that doesn't match the complex conditional logic
- Missing opacity settings for top/bottom bars
- Uses different method for saving theme (Settings class vs Display::SetTheme)

Structure Similarity:
The overall structure shows some similarity with function signature matching and use of similar variable names, but differs significantly in:
- Control flow organization (ground truth has more granular conditional blocks)
- API call patterns (different sequence and combination of LVGL style functions)
- Preprocessor directive handling (different approach to CONFIG_USE_WECHAT_MESSAGE_STYLE sections)
- Error handling (generated adds null check, ground truth assumes valid input)
- Locking mechanism placement and usage

Functional Completeness:
Generated code covers many core theming aspects but misses critical functionality:
- Missing dynamic font sizing logic based on text metrics
- No support for background images with fallback to solid colors
- Incomplete chat bubble styling (simplifies the complex nested container logic)
- Missing opacity controls for UI elements
- Different theme persistence mechanism
- Some UI components like emoji_label handled differently
- Status bar element styling is less comprehensive

Overall, while the generated code captures the basic idea of applying themes to various UI elements, it lacks the sophisticated logic and completeness of the reference implementation.

results.description: The `SingleLed` function initializes a single WS2812 LED connected to a specified GPIO pin, configures it using the RMT peripheral for precise timing, and sets up a blinking timer to control the LED's behavior. It ensures the LED is cleared (turned off) initially and prepares it for dynamic color and blink control. This provides a hardware abstraction for driving addressable LEDs in the system.

157: }

The `SingleLed` function initializes a single WS2812 LED connected to a specified GPIO pin, configures it using the RMT peripheral for precise timing, and sets up a blinking timer to control the LED's behavior. It ensures the LED is cleared (turned off) initially and prepares it for dynamic color and blink control. This provides a hardware abstraction for driving addressable LEDs in the system.

- Key differences include:

  - The generated code initializes member variables (`r_`, `g_`, `b_`, `blink_counter_`, `blink_interval_ms_`) that are not present in the ground truth, suggesting potential divergence in class design.
  - The callback setup is semantically equivalent but lacks explicit task dispatch configuration (`.dispatch_method = ESP_TIMER_TASK`) from the ground truth.
  - Missing `assert(gpio != GPIO_NUM_NC)` in generated code reduces robustness.

- Both codes follow a similar sequence of operations: configure strip, configure RMT, create device, clear LED, set up timer.
- Differences in struct field names and initialization style reduce structural alignment:
  - Use of `flags.invert_out` and `clk_src` in generated code are additional fields not seen in ground truth.
  - Ordering and grouping of code blocks differ slightly (e.g., variable initialization placed after driver installation).
  - Ground truth includes more structured error checking via `ESP_ERROR_CHECK`.

The `SingleLed::OnStateChanged` function updates the built-in LED's color and behavior based on the current device state. It visually indicates states such as starting, Wi-Fi configuration, idle, connecting, listening, speaking, upgrading, and activating through different colors and blinking patterns. The LED provides real-time feedback to the user about the system's operational status.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall structure of the function is preserved with a switch-case handling different device states.
- However, several behavioral differences exist:
  - In `kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring`, the blink interval differs (100ms vs 500ms).
  - `kDeviceStateIdle` turns the LED off in ground truth but sets green color and turns it on in generated code.
  - `kDeviceStateConnecting` uses blinking in ground truth but continuous light with yellow color in generated code.
  - Cases for `kDeviceStateListening` and `kDeviceStateAudioTesting` are merged into one case (`kDeviceStateListening`) without checking voice detection status.
  - Color values differ significantly; e.g., using magenta instead of red/orange hues for listening state.
  - Default case behavior diverges: logs warning and returns early in ground truth, while setting dim red and turning LED on in generated version.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Uses the same application instance access pattern (`Application::GetInstance()`).
- Switch-case construct mirrors that of the ground truth.
- Most API calls like `SetColor`, `TurnOn`, `StartContinuousBlink` are used similarly.
- Minor formatting/style differences such as spacing do not impact structural similarity.
- Missing some specific cases like `kDeviceStateAudioTesting`.

Functional Completeness:
- Misses distinct behaviors for multiple states:
  - No support for audio testing logic based on `IsVoiceDetected()`.
  - Different timing or missing blinking patterns for certain states.
  - Incorrect use of colors which may affect user experience or system indication accuracy.
  - Lacks logging for unknown states (`ESP_LOGW`), potentially reducing debuggability.
  - Merges two separate device states (`Listening` and `AudioTesting`) incorrectly.
- Overall coverage of core functionality exists but lacks fidelity to intended behavior per state.

This maintains continuous communication with the backend without interrupting active operations.

The `MqttProtocol` constructor initializes the MQTT communication layer, setting up an event group for synchronization and a reconnect timer that periodically attempts to reestablish the MQTT connection when the device is in an idle state. It ensures reliable server reconnection by scheduling retry attempts through the application's task scheduler while respecting the current device state. This maintains continuous communication with the backend without interrupting active operations.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core logic of initializing the reconnect timer with a callback that checks device state and schedules reconnection is preserved.
- However, the generated code omits the check for `app.GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle`, which may lead to different behavior under certain conditions.
- Use of `std::make_shared<bool>(true)` for `alive_` and capturing it in the lambda is consistent in both versions.

Structure Similarity:
- Both codes follow a similar structure for creating an `esp_timer` with a callback.
- Initialization of member variables like `event_group_handle_` and `reconnect_timer_` is done similarly.
- Minor differences include initialization order and additional fields (`udp_`, `mqtt_`, etc.) in the generated version not present in the ground truth.
- The use of `static_cast` vs C-style cast is a stylistic difference but does not affect correctness.

Functional Completeness:
- All essential functionality from the ground truth is implemented in the generated code.
- Additional initializations (e.g., `udp_`, `mqtt_`, sequence numbers, sample rate) are included without conflicting with the original intent.
- Missing only the conditional check on device state before scheduling reconnection, which could be critical depending on application context.

It sets up event handlers for connection, disconnection, and incoming messages, including parsing JSON payloads and triggering appropriate actions such as session management or audio channel control. The function also handles error reporting and scheduling of reconnect attempts upon disconnection.

The `StartMqttClient` function initializes and starts an MQTT client using configuration settings, establishing a connection to a specified broker endpoint. It sets up event handlers for connection, disconnection, and incoming messages, including parsing JSON payloads and triggering appropriate actions such as session management or audio channel control. The function also handles error reporting and scheduling of reconnect attempts upon disconnection.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
- The overall goal of starting an MQTT client is preserved.
- Key differences include:
  - Additional check for `client_id.empty()` in generated code which changes behavior.
  - Uses a different connection method (`Start()`) instead of direct `Connect()` with parsed address/port.
  - Different error messages and error codes used.
  - Event group bits handling and subscription logic differ from original callbacks.
  - Missing critical parts like detailed message parsing ("goodbye" type), session ID checks, and scheduling mechanisms.
  - Reconnect timer usage differs significantly; uses `esp_timer_start_once` directly without stopping previous timers.

Structure Similarity (55/100):
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Initialization of settings and fetching configuration values are very similar.
- Control flow diverges due to different approaches to connecting and managing the MQTT lifecycle.
- Generated code introduces new methods such as `SetClientId`, `SetEndpoint`, and `Start()` that aren't in the ground truth.
- Callback structures use lambda captures differently and lack some functionality (e.g., no explicit `OnConnected` vs `OnConnect`).
- Some structural elements like event groups and timers exist but are applied differently.

Functional Completeness (65/100):
- Core functionality of initializing and starting an MQTT client is covered.
- Lacks several important features from the ground truth:
  - No support for custom port parsing (assumes default or internal handling).
  - Missing specific message type handling beyond basic JSON parsing.
  - Absence of proper disconnection callback behaviors including scheduled reconnections via timer stop/start logic.
  - Does not handle goodbye/session cleanup correctly.
  - Error reporting mechanism varies and omits integration with application-level scheduling where needed.
- Introduces additional functionality (like subscribing to topics) not present in the reference implementation.

results.description: The `SendAudio` function securely transmits an audio packet over UDP by first encrypting the payload using AES-CTR mode with a dynamically updated nonce that includes the packet size, timestamp, and sequence number. It ensures thread-safe access to the UDP channel and returns a boolean indicating whether the transmission was successful.

206: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
207: {
208:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
209:     {
210:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
211:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
212:         {
213:             return false;
214:         }
215:     }
216: 
217:     error_occurred_ = false;
218:     session_id_ = "";
219:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
220: 
221:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
222:     if (!SendText(message))
223:     {
224:         return false;
225:     }
226: 
227:     // 等待服务器响应
228:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
229:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
230:     {
231:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
232:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
233:         return false;
234:     }
235: 
236:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
237:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
238:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
239:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
240:                     {
241:         /*
242:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
243:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
244:          * |payload payload_len|
245:          */
246:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
247:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
248:             return;
249:         }
250:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
251:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
252:             return;
253:         }
254:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
255:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
256:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
257:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
258:             return;
259:         }
260:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
261:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
262:         }
263: 
264:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
265:         size_t nc_off = 0;
266:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
267:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
268:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
269:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
270:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
271:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
272:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
273:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
274:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
275:         if (ret != 0) {
276:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
277:             return;
278:         }
279:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
280:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
281:         }
282:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
283:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
284: 
285:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
286: 
287:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
288:     {
289:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
290:     }
291:     return true;
292: }

294: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
295: {
296:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
297:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
298:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
299:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
300:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
301:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
302: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
303:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
304: #endif
305:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
306:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
307:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
308:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
309:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
310:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
311:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
312:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
313:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
314:     std::string message(json_str);
315:     cJSON_free(json_str);
316:     cJSON_Delete(root);
317:     return message;
318: }

320: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
321: {
322:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
323:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)
324:     {
325:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
326:         return;
327:     }
328: 
329:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
330:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
331:     {
332:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
333:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
334:     }
335: 
336:     // Get sample rate from hello message
337:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
338:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
339:     {
340:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
341:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
342:         {
343:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
344:         }
345:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
346:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
347:         {
348:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
349:         }
350:     }
351: 
352:     auto udp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "udp");
353:     if (!cJSON_IsObject(udp))
354:     {
355:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "UDP is not specified");
356:         return;
357:     }
358:     udp_server_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "server")->valuestring;
359:     udp_port_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "port")->valueint;
360:     auto key = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "key")->valuestring;
361:     auto nonce = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "nonce")->valuestring;
362: 
363:     // auto encryption = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "encryption")->valuestring;
364:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
365:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
366:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
367:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
368:     local_sequence_ = 0;
369:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
370:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
371: }
372: 
373: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
374: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
375: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
376: {
377:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
378:         return c - '0';
379:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
380:         return c - 'A' + 10;
381:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
382:         return c - 'a' + 10;
383:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
384: }
385: 
386: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
387: {
388:     std::string decoded;
389:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
390:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
391:     {
392:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
393:         decoded.push_back(byte);
394:     }
395:     return decoded;
396: }
397: 
398: bool MqttProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
399: {
400:     return udp_ != nullptr && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
401: }

The `SendAudio` function securely transmits an audio packet over UDP by first encrypting the payload using AES-CTR mode with a dynamically updated nonce that includes the packet size, timestamp, and sequence number. It ensures thread-safe access to the UDP channel and returns a boolean indicating whether the transmission was successful.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to perform encryption and send an audio packet over UDP, which aligns with the overall goal.
- However, there are significant deviations in how packet headers are structured. The ground truth embeds metadata directly into a reused `nonce` buffer, while the generated version builds a complex header that does not match the expected protocol format.
- Nonce handling differs: the ground truth modifies specific offsets of a string-based nonce, whereas the generated code uses a vector and appends it separately.
- Sequence number increment placement also differs — ground truth pre-increments inside the nonce, but the generated code post-increments after sending.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Both use mutex locking for thread safety and check for null UDP channel before proceeding.
- Core components like mbedtls AES-CTR encryption and logging on failure exist in both versions.
- However, the control flow diverges significantly due to additional logic such as error setting, custom packet framing, and different memory/data handling approaches (e.g., vectors vs strings).
- Use of reinterpret_cast and manual byte manipulation shows structural divergence from the simpler memcpy approach used in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code includes extra features such as detailed error reporting via SetError(), custom packet type flags, and SSRC generation, which go beyond the minimal functionality shown in the ground truth.
- It correctly implements network byte order conversions and attempts encryption using the same crypto API.
- Missing elements include exact positioning of fields within the nonce as required by the receiving side (likely causing interoperability issues), and subtle differences in how sequence numbers are handled.
- Overall most core functions are covered, though possibly incorrectly implemented for actual communication compatibility.

results.description: The `MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a secure audio communication channel by connecting to an MQTT server, sending a hello message, and waiting for a server response. Upon successful handshake, it sets up a UDP connection to receive encrypted OPUS audio packets, which are then decrypted and passed to a callback for further processing. The function ensures proper sequencing and timing of audio data while handling potential errors such as timeouts or invalid packet sequences.

The `MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a secure audio communication channel by connecting to an MQTT server, sending a hello message, and waiting for a server response. Upon successful handshake, it sets up a UDP connection to receive encrypted OPUS audio packets, which are then decrypted and passed to a callback for further processing. The function ensures proper sequencing and timing of audio data while handling potential errors such as timeouts or invalid packet sequences.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to replicate the core functionality of opening an audio channel via MQTT and setting up a UDP connection for audio streaming. It correctly sends a hello message, waits for a server response, creates and connects a UDP socket, and sets up a callback to handle incoming encrypted audio packets. However, there are some semantic differences:
- The generated code omits the initial MQTT connection check and reconnection logic which is crucial in the ground truth.
- Error handling is simplified; for example, it doesn't clear event bits before waiting.
- The packet parsing logic differs slightly, such as how the payload size is extracted (from offset 2 in the generated vs implicit struct layout in ground truth).
- Sequence validation is less strict in the generated version (allows sequence == remote_sequence_ while ground truth only allows >).

Structure Similarity:
While both codes follow a similar high-level flow (send hello -> wait for response -> setup UDP), structural differences include:
- Function call order varies; the generated code does not perform mutex locking around UDP creation or reset error flags like the ground truth.
- Different APIs are used (e.g., OnData vs OnMessage, CreateUdp() vs CreateUdp(2)).
- Use of lambda captures and internal variable handling differ, especially regarding nonce and decryption buffer management.
- Missing synchronization constructs such as std::lock_guard.

Functional Completeness:
Most major functions are covered including sending messages, creating and connecting UDP sockets, decrypting packets using AES-CTR, and forwarding decoded audio data through callbacks. Some minor features from the ground truth are missing:
- No explicit MQTT client restart mechanism.
- Lacks detailed logging levels and specific error string mappings (e.g., uses generic SERVER_ERROR instead of checking connection state).
- Doesn't set session ID or manage certain state variables explicitly.
- Callback invocation after successful setup (`on_audio_channel_opened_`) is absent.

The `GetHelloMessage` function generates a JSON-formatted MQTT hello message to request a UDP channel for audio communication. It includes device capabilities such as AEC and MCP support, along with audio parameters like format, sample rate, and frame duration. This message is used to initiate connection and negotiate features with the server.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall purpose and logic flow are preserved: both functions construct a JSON object representing a "hello" message for initiating a UDP channel.
- Both use cJSON to build a structured JSON message including type, transport, capabilities/audio_params, and then serialize it into a string.
- However, there are differences in how feature flags (like aec/mcp) are determined – ground truth uses compile-time #ifdef while generated code uses runtime checks via Board::GetInstance(), which changes semantics slightly but may be intentional design improvement.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is very similar with sequential creation of JSON objects, adding fields, printing, cleanup and returning the result.
- Differences include ordering of field additions (e.g., “transport” added later in generated), naming (“features” vs “capabilities”), and lack of explicit version number in generated code.
- Use of constants like OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS vs AUDIO_FRAME_DURATION_MS shows structural divergence although possibly equivalent.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality is covered well: constructing a proper hello message with required components.
- Missing elements from ground truth: explicit version field ("version": 3), channels count in audio params.
- Additional or differing behavior: dynamic capability detection instead of static/config-based; different parent key name ("capabilities" vs "features").
- Memory management and error handling appear consistent between versions.

Overall, the generated code captures most of the intended behavior but introduces some semantic shifts and omits minor details found in the reference implementation.

22:             auto &app = Application::GetInstance();
23:             if (app.GetDeviceState() == kDeviceStateIdle)
24:             {
25:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Reconnecting to MQTT server");
26:                 auto alive = protocol->alive_; // Capture alive flag
27:                 app.Schedule([protocol, alive]()
28:                              {
29:                     if (*alive) {
30:                         protocol->StartMqttClient(false);
31:                     } });
32:             }
33:         },
34:         .arg = this,
35:     };
36:     esp_timer_create(&reconnect_timer_args, &reconnect_timer_);
37: }

66: bool MqttProtocol::StartMqttClient(bool report_error)
67: {
68:     if (mqtt_ != nullptr)
69:     {
70:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Mqtt client already started");
71:         mqtt_.reset();
72:     }
73: 
74:     Settings settings("mqtt", false);
75:     auto endpoint = settings.GetString("endpoint");
76:     auto client_id = settings.GetString("client_id");
77:     auto username = settings.GetString("username");
78:     auto password = settings.GetString("password");
79:     int keepalive_interval = settings.GetInt("keepalive", 240);
80:     publish_topic_ = settings.GetString("publish_topic");
81: 
82:     if (endpoint.empty())
83:     {
84:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MQTT endpoint is not specified");
85:         if (report_error)
86:         {
87:             SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_FOUND);
88:         }
89:         return false;
90:     }
91: 
92:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
93:     mqtt_ = network->CreateMqtt(0);
94:     mqtt_->SetKeepAlive(keepalive_interval);
95: 
96:     mqtt_->OnDisconnected([this]()
97:                           {
98:         if (on_disconnected_ != nullptr) {
99:             on_disconnected_();
100:         }
101:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT disconnected, schedule reconnect in %d seconds", MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS / 1000);
102:         esp_timer_start_once(reconnect_timer_, MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS * 1000); });
103: 
104:     mqtt_->OnConnected([this]()
105:                        {
106:         if (on_connected_ != nullptr) {
107:             on_connected_();
108:         }
109:         esp_timer_stop(reconnect_timer_); });
110: 
111:     mqtt_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &topic, const std::string &payload)
112:                      {
113:         cJSON* root = cJSON_Parse(payload.c_str());
114:         if (root == nullptr) {
115:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse json message %s", payload.c_str());
116:             return;
117:         }
118:         cJSON* type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
119:         if (!cJSON_IsString(type)) {
120:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Message type is invalid");
121:             cJSON_Delete(root);
122:             return;
123:         }
124: 
125:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
126:             ParseServerHello(root);
127:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "goodbye") == 0) {
128:             auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
129:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received goodbye message, session_id: %s", session_id ? session_id->valuestring : "null");
130:             if (session_id == nullptr || session_id_ == session_id->valuestring) {
131:                 auto alive = alive_;  // Capture alive flag
132:                 Application::GetInstance().Schedule([this, alive]() {
133:                     if (*alive) {
134:                         CloseAudioChannel();
135:                     }
136:                 });
137:             }
138:         } else if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
139:             on_incoming_json_(root);
140:         }
141:         cJSON_Delete(root);
142:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
143: 
144:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to endpoint %s", endpoint.c_str());
145:     std::string broker_address;
146:     int broker_port = 8883;
147:     size_t pos = endpoint.find(':');
148:     if (pos != std::string::npos)
149:     {
150:         broker_address = endpoint.substr(0, pos);
151:         broker_port = std::stoi(endpoint.substr(pos + 1));
152:     }
153:     else
154:     {
155:         broker_address = endpoint;
156:     }
157:     if (!mqtt_->Connect(broker_address, broker_port, client_id, username, password))
158:     {
159:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to endpoint, code=%d", mqtt_->GetLastError());
160:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
161:         return false;
162:     }
163: 
164:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to endpoint");
165:     return true;
166: }

183: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
184: {
185:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
186:     if (udp_ == nullptr)
187:     {
188:         return false;
189:     }
190: 
191:     std::string nonce(aes_nonce_);
192:     *(uint16_t *)&nonce[2] = htons(packet->payload.size());
193:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[8] = htonl(packet->timestamp);
194:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[12] = htonl(++local_sequence_);
195: 
196:     std::string encrypted;
197:     encrypted.resize(aes_nonce_.size() + packet->payload.size());
198:     memcpy(encrypted.data(), nonce.data(), nonce.size());
199: 
200:     size_t nc_off = 0;
201:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
202:     if (mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, packet->payload.size(), &nc_off, (uint8_t *)nonce.c_str(), stream_block,
203:                               (uint8_t *)packet->payload.data(), (uint8_t *)&encrypted[nonce.size()]) != 0)
204:     {
205:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data");
206:         return false;
207:     }
208: 
209:     return udp_->Send(encrypted) > 0;
210: }

213: {
214:     {

216:         udp_.reset();

221:     message += "\"type\":\"goodbye\"";

231: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
232: {
233:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
234:     {
235:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
236:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
237:         {
238:             return false;
239:         }
240:     }
241: 
242:     error_occurred_ = false;
243:     session_id_ = "";
244:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
245: 
246:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
247:     if (!SendText(message))
248:     {
249:         return false;
250:     }
251: 
252:     // 等待服务器响应
253:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
254:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
255:     {
256:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
257:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
258:         return false;
259:     }
260: 
261:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
262:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
263:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
264:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
265:                     {
266:         /*
267:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
268:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
269:          * |payload payload_len|
270:          */
271:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
272:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
273:             return;
274:         }
275:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
276:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
277:             return;
278:         }
279:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
280:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
281:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
282:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
283:             return;
284:         }
285:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
286:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
287:         }
288: 
289:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
290:         size_t nc_off = 0;
291:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
292:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
293:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
294:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
295:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
296:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
297:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
298:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
299:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
300:         if (ret != 0) {
301:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
302:             return;
303:         }
304:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
305:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
306:         }
307:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
308:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
309: 
310:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
311: 
312:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
313:     {
314:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
315:     }
316:     return true;
317: }

319: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
320: {
321:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
322:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
323:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
324:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
325:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
326:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
327: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
328:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
329: #endif
330:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
331:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
332:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
333:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
334:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
335:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
336:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
337:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
338:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
339:     std::string message(json_str);
340:     cJSON_free(json_str);
341:     cJSON_Delete(root);
342:     return message;
343: }

349: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
350: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
351: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
352: {
353:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
354:         return c - '0';
355:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
356:         return c - 'A' + 10;
357:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
358:         return c - 'a' + 10;
359:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
360: }

362: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
363: {
364:     std::string decoded;
365:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
366:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
367:     {
368:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
369:         decoded.push_back(byte);
370:     }
371:     return decoded;
372: }

</file>
The `ParseServerHello` function processes the server's hello message to extract and configure UDP transport settings, including the server address, port, encryption key, and nonce. It also retrieves audio parameters such as sample rate and frame duration, initializes AES encryption, and signals that the server hello has been successfully processed. This enables secure real-time audio communication over UDP.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to parse fields from a JSON object similar to the ground truth but uses different field names (e.g., "udp_addr" instead of "udp.server", "key" directly under root vs nested in "udp").
- It introduces additional error handling via SetError() and more rigorous validation which changes behavior compared to the original.
- Key logic such as extracting nested values ("udp.server", "udp.port") is incorrectly interpreted, leading to incorrect semantics.
- AES key setting includes size-based calculation that may differ from hardcoded 128-bit usage in ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Both use cJSON for parsing and follow a sequential validation pattern.
- However, the order and grouping of operations differ significantly; e.g., UDP info extraction is flattened in generated version.
- Ground truth checks "transport" type before proceeding — this check is completely missing in the generated code.
- Use of logging and event group signaling exists in both but occurs at different points structurally.

Functional Completeness:
- Generated code misses critical parts like checking the "transport" field for "udp".
- Nested structure access (e.g., "udp.server") is not replicated correctly.
- Some elements like optional session ID handling (in ground truth) are treated as mandatory.
- Additional validations (e.g., audio_params required in generated vs optional in ground truth) alter functional coverage.
- AES initialization is present but applied differently due to misinterpretation of data hierarchy.

results.description: The `SendAudio` function transmits audio data over a WebSocket connection using version-specific binary protocols (v2 or v3) or raw payload format based on the negotiated protocol version. It ensures reliable delivery by checking the WebSocket connection status before sending and formats the audio packet with appropriate headers and metadata. The function supports real-time audio streaming with timestamping and payload size encoding depending on the protocol version.

The `SendAudio` function transmits audio data over a WebSocket connection using version-specific binary protocols (v2 or v3) or raw payload format based on the negotiated protocol version. It ensures reliable delivery by checking the WebSocket connection status before sending and formats the audio packet with appropriate headers and metadata. The function supports real-time audio streaming with timestamping and payload size encoding depending on the protocol version.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The overall logic of sending audio packets via WebSocket based on protocol version is preserved.
- Both handle V2 and V3 protocols differently, and fallback for other versions.
- Key differences include handling empty payloads (new behavior in generated), additional error logging, and use of SetError().
- Type assumptions (e.g., audio data type as 0) are introduced without clear grounding from original.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches the task but not exactly the ground truth (return type and parameter match).
- Control flow is similar: null/connected check -> version checks -> send.
- Major structural divergence includes using std::vector<uint8_t> instead of std::string with resize/data casting.
- Uses SendBinary vs Send method name which suggests a possible API mismatch.
- Additional fields like 'reserved' missing in v2 serialization in generated code.

Functional Completeness:
- Covers core functionality: version-based packet formatting and transmission.
- Lacks some details from GT such as setting reserved fields or exact field assignments (e.g., bp2->type uses htons in GT but not in gen).
- Introduces extra features like ESP logging and SetError that aren't part of GT.
- Empty payload early return is an extension rather than part of GT logic.
- Fails to replicate byte-ordering consistency across all fields compared to GT.

The `WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel` function establishes a WebSocket connection for audio streaming by configuring authentication headers, protocol version, and device identifiers, then setting up a data handler to process incoming binary audio packets according to specified protocol versions. It supports different binary protocols (v2 and v3) for parsing audio stream packets with appropriate byte order conversion and payload extraction. The function returns true if the WebSocket is successfully created and configured, otherwise false.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core intent of opening an audio channel via WebSocket is preserved.
- However, there are significant deviations in implementation details that affect behavior:
  - Authentication logic differs: Generated code always adds "Bearer " prefix whereas ground truth checks if token already contains a space.
  - Handshake message generation is completely different; generated code builds a custom JSON object while ground truth uses `GetHelloMessage()`.
  - Data handling diverges significantly in how binary packets are parsed and processed for different protocol versions.
  - Missing event group synchronization (`xEventGroupWaitBits`) which waits for server hello acknowledgment in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches.
- High-level steps like fetching settings, creating websocket, setting headers, connecting, and sending initial message are present but reordered or altered.
- Control flow varies notably due to differences in handshake mechanism and lack of proper state/event coordination.
- Use of lambda callbacks for data handling exists in both but with differing parameter lists and internal logic.
- APIs used differ in naming and usage patterns (e.g., `SetDataHandler` vs `OnData`, `Send` vs `SendText`).

Functional Completeness:
- Key functionalities missing in generated code:
  - Protocol version-based binary parsing for specific structures (BinaryProtocol2/3).
  - Proper byte order conversion using `ntohs`/`ntohl` in structured binary data processing.
  - Event-driven notification system for audio channel open/close events.
  - Error reporting through `SetError()` and retrieval of last error codes.
  - Usage of device-specific identifiers such as MAC address and UUID in headers.
  - Waiting for server response after sending hello message.
- Some additional features exist in generated code not found in ground truth (e.g., detailed audio parameters in handshake), but these appear to be incorrect implementations rather than enhancements.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
- The overall purpose is preserved — both functions generate a JSON hello message for a WebSocket protocol.
- However, there are several semantic discrepancies:
  - Features are represented as an array of strings in the generated code vs. a JSON object with boolean values in the ground truth.
  - AEC feature inclusion is conditional based on a preprocessor directive in the ground truth but unconditionally included in the generated code.
  - Audio format differs ("PCM" in generated vs "opus" in ground truth).
  - Frame duration is hardcoded in the generated code while it uses a constant (`OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS`) in the ground truth.

- Function signature matches exactly.
- General flow is similar: create root object, add fields, build nested objects/arrays, print to string, clean up memory.
- Both use cJSON library consistently.
- Differences include how features are structured (array vs object) and where memory cleanup occurs (`cJSON_Delete` before or after `free`).

Functional Completeness (55/100):
- Missing conditional compilation handling for AEC feature.
- Lacks proper abstraction/constants like `OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS`.
- MCP feature is added differently (as part of array instead of named boolean field).
- Audio encoding format mismatch may cause functional incompatibility if the server expects "opus".
- Memory management inconsistency: uses `free()` instead of `cJSON_free()` which could be problematic depending on cJSON configuration.

79:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to send text: %s", text.c_str());
80:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_ERROR);
81:         return false;
82:     }
83: 
84:     return true;
85: }

97: bool WebsocketProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
98: {
99:     Settings settings("websocket", false);
100:     std::string url = settings.GetString("url");
101:     std::string token = settings.GetString("token");
102:     int version = settings.GetInt("version");
103:     if (version != 0)
104:     {
105:         version_ = version;
106:     }
107: 
108:     error_occurred_ = false;
109: 
110:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
111:     websocket_ = network->CreateWebSocket(1);
112:     if (websocket_ == nullptr)
113:     {
114:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create websocket");
115:         return false;
116:     }
117: 
118:     if (!token.empty())
119:     {
120:         // If token not has a space, add "Bearer " prefix
121:         if (token.find(" ") == std::string::npos)
122:         {
123:             token = "Bearer " + token;
124:         }
125:         websocket_->SetHeader("Authorization", token.c_str());
126:     }
127:     websocket_->SetHeader("Protocol-Version", std::to_string(version_).c_str());
128:     websocket_->SetHeader("Device-Id", SystemInfo::GetMacAddress().c_str());
129:     websocket_->SetHeader("Client-Id", Board::GetInstance().GetUuid().c_str());
130: 
131:     websocket_->OnData([this](const char *data, size_t len, bool binary)
132:                        {
133:         if (binary) {
134:             if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
135:                 if (version_ == 2) {
136:                     BinaryProtocol2* bp2 = (BinaryProtocol2*)data;
137:                     bp2->version = ntohs(bp2->version);
138:                     bp2->type = ntohs(bp2->type);
139:                     bp2->timestamp = ntohl(bp2->timestamp);
140:                     bp2->payload_size = ntohl(bp2->payload_size);
141:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp2->payload;
142:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
143:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
144:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
145:                         .timestamp = bp2->timestamp,
146:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp2->payload_size)
147:                     }));
148:                 } else if (version_ == 3) {
149:                     BinaryProtocol3* bp3 = (BinaryProtocol3*)data;
150:                     bp3->type = bp3->type;
151:                     bp3->payload_size = ntohs(bp3->payload_size);
152:                     auto payload = (uint8_t*)bp3->payload;
153:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
154:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
155:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
156:                         .timestamp = 0,
157:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>(payload, payload + bp3->payload_size)
158:                     }));
159:                 } else {
160:                     on_incoming_audio_(std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>(AudioStreamPacket{
161:                         .sample_rate = server_sample_rate_,
162:                         .frame_duration = server_frame_duration_,
163:                         .timestamp = 0,
164:                         .payload = std::vector<uint8_t>((uint8_t*)data, (uint8_t*)data + len)
165:                     }));
166:                 }
167:             }
168:         } else {
169:             // Parse JSON data
170:             auto root = cJSON_Parse(data);
171:             auto type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
172:             if (cJSON_IsString(type)) {
173:                 if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
174:                     ParseServerHello(root);
175:                 } else {
176:                     if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
177:                         on_incoming_json_(root);
178:                     }
179:                 }
180:             } else {
181:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Missing message type, data: %s", data);
182:             }
183:             cJSON_Delete(root);
184:         }
185:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
186: 
187:     websocket_->OnDisconnected([this]()
188:                                {
189:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Websocket disconnected");
190:         if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr) {
191:             on_audio_channel_closed_();
192:         } });
193: 
194:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to websocket server: %s with version: %d", url.c_str(), version_);
195:     if (!websocket_->Connect(url.c_str()))
196:     {
197:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to websocket server, code=%d", websocket_->GetLastError());
198:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
199:         return false;
200:     }
201: 
202:     // Send hello message to describe the client
203:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
204:     if (!SendText(message))
205:     {
206:         return false;
207:     }
208: 
209:     // Wait for server hello
210:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
211:     if (!(bits & WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
212:     {
213:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
214:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
215:         return false;
216:     }
217: 
218:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
219:     {
220:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
221:     }
222: 
223:     return true;
224: }

226: std::string WebsocketProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
227: {
228:     // keys: message type, version, audio_params (format, sample_rate, channels)
229:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
230:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
231:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", version_);
232:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
233: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
234:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
235: #endif
236:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
237:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
238:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "websocket");
239:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
240:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
241:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
242:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
243:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
244:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
245:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
246:     std::string message(json_str);
247:     cJSON_free(json_str);
248:     cJSON_Delete(root);
249:     return message;
250: }

The `ParseServerHello` function processes the server's initial handshake response in a WebSocket connection, extracting and storing session identification and audio configuration parameters such as sample rate and frame duration. It validates the transport type and sets an event flag upon successful parsing to signal readiness for further communication. This enables the device to configure its audio streaming settings based on server-provided specifications.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code largely preserves the intended behavior of the ground truth. It correctly parses and validates the transport type, extracts session ID, and processes audio parameters. However, there are some differences:
- The error handling diverges slightly; the generated version uses a custom `SetError` method instead of directly logging an error message when transport is unsupported.
- An additional log message for audio parameters is included in the generated code which does not exist in the ground truth.
These changes do not break functionality but introduce deviations from the original semantics.

The overall structure closely mirrors that of the ground truth:

Minor structural differences include more explicit null checks (`!= nullptr`) in the generated code and added debug logging.

- Session ID extraction and assignment.
- Parsing of nested audio parameter fields (sample rate and frame duration).
However, the generated code introduces extra behaviors not present in the reference:
